# C# and .NET basics - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#c-and-net-basics)

C# and .NET basics[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#c-and-net-basics "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================================

What is C#[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#what-is-c "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

C# is an object-oriented programming language that can be used to build various types of extensions and applications. As an object-oriented language, C# is easy to learn and read even if you have no prior programming experience. It also provides a structured means for writing compact and reusable code.

C# is our language of choice for cBots, plugins and indicators because of the following:

*   Shallow learning curve: Even if you have never worked with C# before, creating and deploying [**your first bot**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/creating-and-running-a-cbot/)
     or an [**indicator**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/creating-an-indicator/)
     should only take a couple of minutes.
*   Language efficiency: As C# is easy to use, you can quickly rewrite any code snippets from this documentation to suit your needs.
*   Extensive class library: When using C# and .NET, you can access a large number of libraries containing predefined classes and methods. These methods can efficiently handle common tasks, leaving you free to solve complex trading problems.
*   Support of concurrent operations: In C#, you can write code that does not block server threads upon execution. In other words, you can start certain tasks simultaneously with other tasks.

To be executed, C# source code has to be compiled into an Intermediary Language (IL). IL code, in turn, is necessary to ensure compliance with the international Common Language Infrastructure (CLI) specification. During execution, IL code is compiled into a set of native machine instructions.

What is .NET[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#what-is-net "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

.NET is a virtual execution system based on a number of class libraries and the Common Language Runtime, Microsoft's interpretation of the CLI. Without going into technical intricacies, .NET fulfills the following functions.

*   Facilitating the development and execution of applications: The [**.NET SDK**](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/download)
     already contains several built-in compilers and build engines, eliminating the need for creating any custom solutions.
*   Providing runtime libraries: If you would like to add certain data types or collections to your code, there is a high chance that .NET already has the corresponding set of classes and methods.

In our opinion, .NET is the perfect environment for creating and running trading bots or indicators.

The basics of C#[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#the-basics-of-c "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Data types and variable declarations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#data-types-and-variable-declarations "Permanent link")

Data types are a means of categorising data so that C# knows exactly how to treat variables and properties. In variable/property declarations, data types always precede the variable/property name.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1   | `string developer = "I am developing cBots, plugins, and indicators!";` |

Alternatively, you can use the `var` keyword to avoid specifying a data type.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1   | `var developer = "I am developing cBots, plugins, and indicators!";` |

### Objects and classes[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#objects-and-classes "Permanent link")

Think of objects as abstractions of tangible or intangible entities. These entities can have certain characteristics (properties) and can perform various operations (methods). In turn, classes serve as templates for object creation.

As C# is an object-oriented language, classes can also inherit properties and methods from other classes. Consider the following example in which we declare a new `NewBot` class that inherits from the `Robot` class. We also define some new class properties.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br> 2<br> 3<br> 4<br> 5<br> 6<br> 7<br> 8<br> 9<br>10<br>11<br>12<br>13<br>14<br>15<br>16<br>17<br>18<br>19<br>20<br>21<br>22<br>23 | `/* We use the colon sign to designate inheritance. Additionally, we used expressions in square brackets to specify the parameters that apply to the entire class */ [Robot(AccessRights = AccessRights.None)] public class NewBot : Robot {     /* We declare a custom class property and make it     read-only. */     public string CustomProperty { get; }      /* In this declaration, we define the default value of a     custom parameter. */     [Parameter("BotName", DefaultValue = "Traders First!")]      /* We declare the BotName property which is changeable via     the "BotName" parameter. */     public string BotName { get; }      /* We also declare the BotComment parameter.     It can be both read and set. */     [Parameter("BotComment", DefaultValue = "Our super-duper bot!")]     public string BotComment { get; set; } }` |

### Data types[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#data-types "Permanent link")

As C# is a strongly-typed language, it is necessary to specify data types when declaring variables and class properties. In brief, data types constitute unique classes with different sets of behaviors. Consider the following examples in which various data types are specified for different class properties and related parameters.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br>2 | `[Parameter("Price")] public DataSeries Source { get; }` |

In the above code, the `DataSeries` data type represents a read-only list of values and is, therefore, typically used to represent market prices.

When there is a need to create multi-option parameters, you can use the built-in `enum` data type as detailed below. You can think of `enum`'s as special classes that contain various constants.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br>2<br>3<br>4<br>5<br>6<br>7<br>8<br>9 | `public enum Option {     First,     Second,     Third }  [Parameter("Option", DefaultValue = Option.Third)] public Option SelectedOption { get; set; }` |

To access a constant contained in an `enum`, use the following syntax.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1   | `var newOption = Option.First;` |

If a parameter or a property needs to be a number, you will most often use the `int` (integer) or `double` (decimal) data types. While the `double` type has less precision than the `decimal` type, it is also less resource-intensive.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br>2 | `[Parameter("MA Periods", DefaultValue = 14, MinValue = 1, MaxValue = 20)] public int Periods { get; set; }` |

Lastly, the `bool` type typically represents a yes or no input. The values yes and no correspond to `true` and `false`, respectively.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br>2 | `[Parameter("Message", DefaultValue = true)] public bool DisplayChartMessage { get; set; }` |

### Namespaces[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#namespaces "Permanent link")

Namespaces act as designated collections of classes. Once you assign a class to a namespace, it can later be accessed by using the `Namespace.ClassName` notation. We will assign our `NewBot` to the `CoolTradingBots` namespace.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br>2<br>3<br>4<br>5<br>6<br>7<br>8 | `namespace CoolTradingBots {     [Robot(AccessRights = AccessRights.None)]     public class NewBot : Robot     {         // ...     } }` |

### Class methods[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#class-methods "Permanent link")

Class methods are defined following class declaration. All objects of our `NewBot` class will be able to call the `CustomTradeOperation()` method. It takes two arguments, namely a `Symbol` object, and a `double` object. Our method is supposed to take a symbol and execute some sort of trading action for the specified volume

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br> 2<br> 3<br> 4<br> 5<br> 6<br> 7<br> 8<br> 9<br>10<br>11<br>12<br>13<br>14<br>15 | `namespace CoolTradingBots {     [Robot(AccessRights = AccessRights.None)]     public class NewBot : Robot     {         // ... Defining the class parameters.          // We declare the CustomTradeOperation method.          protected override void CustomTradeOperation(string symbolName, double volume)          {             // This space is for declaring the method logic.          }     } }` |

### Class libraries[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#class-libraries "Permanent link")

Class libraries (and, subsequently, class methods) can be accessed by their namespace as shown in the below example.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1   | `CoolTradingBots.NewBot.CustomTradeOperation(symbolName, 10000)` |

Alternatively, you can type in the `using` keyword at the beginning of your code to designate certain namespaces and avoid redundancies. Consider the following code snippet.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br>2<br>3 | `using CoolTradingBots;  NewBot.CustomTradeOperation(symbolName, 10000)` |

### Conditional statements[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#conditional-statements "Permanent link")

To use conditional statements, use a keyword followed by an expression in round brackets. The below example uses the `if` keyword to finalize our `CustomTradingOperation()` method. To do so, we use the `EvaluateMarket()` method which is defined in the `Analytics.Actions` namespace.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br> 2<br> 3<br> 4<br> 5<br> 6<br> 7<br> 8<br> 9<br>10<br>11<br>12<br>13<br>14<br>15<br>16<br>17<br>18<br>19<br>20 | `namespace CoolTradingBots {     [Robot(AccessRights = AccessRights.None)]     public class NewBot : Robot     {         // ... Defining the class parameters.          protected override void CustomTradeOperation(string symbolName, double volume)         {             // We declare and initialize the 'result' variable.             var result = Analytics.Action.EvaluateMarket();              // We use a conditional statement based on the IsSuccessful property.             if (result.IsSuccessful)             {                 Print("Operation successful!")             }         }     } }` |

### Collections[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#collections "Permanent link")

Collections are defined as containers that can store one or more objects of a particular class.

Collections are fully indexable, meaning that their members can be accessed by passing a certain `integer` value in square bracers. Consider the following example in which the `Calculate()` method prints the properties of a bar accessed via its index.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br>2<br>3<br>4<br>5 | `public override void Calculate(int index) {        var bar = Bars[index];        Print($"{bar.Open} \| {bar.High} \| {bar.Low} \| {bar.Close}"); }` |

### Create cTrader extensions[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#create-ctrader-extensions "Permanent link")

In the below snippet, we create a basic cBot using only the knowledge covered in the above sections.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br> 2<br> 3<br> 4<br> 5<br> 6<br> 7<br> 8<br> 9<br>10<br>11<br>12<br>13<br>14<br>15<br>16<br>17<br>18<br>19<br>20<br>21<br>22<br>23<br>24 | `using System;   namespace Spotware.cBots {     [Robot(AccessRights = AccessRights.None)]     public class SuperAwesomeBot : Robot     {          /* In the below method, we define the operation(s) that our bot should         perform when it is launched. */         protected override void OnStart()         {             var result = ExecuteMarketOrder(TradeType.Buy, symbolName, 10000);              /* We use a conditional statement using the IsSuccessful property of the             TradeResult object. */             if (result.IsSuccessful)             {                 var position = result.Position;                 Print ("Position entry price is {0}", position.EntryPrice);             }          }     } }` |

### Synchronous and asynchronous operations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#synchronous-and-asynchronous-operations "Permanent link")

As stated [**previously**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/c-net-basics/#what-is-c)
, C# fully supports asynchronous operations. The diagram below defines a basic example of how trading activities are performed in synchronous execution.

Synchronous execution carries one important drawback. In the above example, the action of executing a market order fully occupies all server threads, meaning that your cBot is unable to do anything else before this operation is completed.

This is less than ideal when you want to quickly react to market events and volatility. Ideally, your cBot should be able to store various actions or put certain tasks on hold to engage in other, more pressing activities. We will expand our example to better reflect how asynchronous operations are performed.

In the above example, our cBot simultaneously placed orders in both directions for hedging its positions. In contrast to synchronous operations, there is no need to wait for one operation to complete before proceeding with another one. This significantly expands developers' opportunities for creating efficient and reliable cBots.

Note that asynchronous execution is different from multi-threading:

*   In asynchronous execution, all tasks are started on the same thread. When they are stored or put on hold, they free this thread and, when their execution continues later, it occurs on a different thread chosen from the thread pool.
*   When multi-threading, all tasks start on different threads and continue their execution on their initial threads. There is no thread shuffling.

cTrader never invokes your methods in parallel so you do not have to worry about multi-threading issues.

Back to top

# Parameter types - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/guides/parameter-types/#parameter-types)

Parameter types[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/guides/parameter-types/#parameter-types "Permanent link")

===================================================================================================================

As C# is a strongly-typed language, it is necessary to specify data types when declaring variables and class properties in cBots, indicators and plugins. The API does not support all data types for parameters, which is why it is essential for algorithm developers to navigate across them.

Parameter use cases and UI[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/guides/parameter-types/#parameter-use-cases-and-ui "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cTrader supports only these parameter types with the key use cases and related UI elements reflected in the table below.

| Data types | Use cases | UI  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `int` | Order volume, number of bars, number of periods, etc. | Number input field (with stepper) |
| `double` | Price value, order volume, etc. | Number input field (with stepper) |
| `string` | Custom message, position label, etc. | Text input field |
| `bool` | Protection mechanisms, allow trading, allow email, etc. | Dropdown list Yes/No |
| `DataSeries` | Source of market prices, etc. | Dropdown list |
| `TimeFrame` | Chosen time frame, etc. | Timeframe selector |
| `enum` | Chart drawings alignment, individual risk levels, etc. | Dropdown list |
| `Color` | Chart drawings, colour of technical analysis means, custom elements, etc. | Colour picker |

For example, the UI of cTrader reflects `bool`, `double`, and `int` parameters as follows.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/guides/parameter-types/01.png)

The next three examples show `DataSeries`, custom `enum` data (for which we also provide the full code in this guide) and `string` data.

[DataSeries](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/guides/parameter-types/#__tabbed_1_1)
[Enum](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/guides/parameter-types/#__tabbed_1_2)
[String](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/guides/parameter-types/#__tabbed_1_3)

[![DataSeries](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/guides/parameter-types/02.png)](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/guides/parameter-types/02.png)

[![Enum](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/guides/parameter-types/03.png)](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/guides/parameter-types/03.png)

[![String](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/guides/parameter-types/04.png)](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/guides/parameter-types/04.png)

As shown below, the `Color` parameter type is represented by a colour picker.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/guides/parameter-types/05.png)

Finally, the UI of `TimeFrame` data duplicates the timeframe of trading charts in the **Trade** application.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/guides/parameter-types/06.png)

cBot examples[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/guides/parameter-types/#cbot-examples "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The position label is a `string` parameter in the following cBot.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br> 2<br> 3<br> 4<br> 5<br> 6<br> 7<br> 8<br> 9<br>10<br>11<br>12<br>13<br>14<br>15<br>16<br>17<br>18<br>19<br>20<br>21<br>22<br>23<br>24<br>25<br>26<br>27<br>28<br>29 | `using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Linq; using System.Text; using cAlgo.API; using cAlgo.API.Collections; using cAlgo.API.Indicators; using cAlgo.API.Internals;  namespace cAlgo.Robots {     [Robot(AccessRights = AccessRights.None)]     public class NewcBot2 : Robot     {         [Parameter(DefaultValue = "my label")]         public string PositionLabel { get; set; }          protected override void OnStart()         {             ExecuteMarketOrder(TradeType.Buy, "XAGUSD", 1000, PositionLabel);         }          protected override void OnStop()         {             var positionToFind = Positions.Find(PositionLabel);             positionToFind.Close();         }     } }` |

`DataSeries`, `int` and `bool` data are exemplified in the algorithm below.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br> 2<br> 3<br> 4<br> 5<br> 6<br> 7<br> 8<br> 9<br>10<br>11<br>12<br>13<br>14<br>15<br>16<br>17<br>18<br>19<br>20<br>21<br>22<br>23<br>24<br>25<br>26<br>27<br>28<br>29<br>30<br>31<br>32<br>33<br>34<br>35<br>36<br>37<br>38<br>39<br>40<br>41<br>42<br>43<br>44<br>45<br>46<br>47<br>48<br>49<br>50<br>51 | `using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Linq; using System.Text; using cAlgo.API; using cAlgo.API.Collections; using cAlgo.API.Indicators; using cAlgo.API.Internals;  namespace cAlgo {     [Robot(TimeZone = TimeZones.UTC, AccessRights = AccessRights.None)]     public class SamplecBotReferenceSMA : Robot     {         [Parameter("Source")]         public DataSeries Source { get; set; }          [Parameter("SMA Period", DefaultValue = 14)]         public int SmaPeriod { get; set; }          [Parameter("Enable Trade", DefaultValue = true)]         public bool EnableTrade { get; set; }          private SimpleMovingAverage sma;          protected override void OnStart()         {             sma = Indicators.SimpleMovingAverage(Source, SmaPeriod);         }          protected override void OnTick()         {             double currentSMA = sma.Result.LastValue;             double currentPrice = Symbol.Bid;              if (EnableTrade)             {                 if (currentPrice > currentSMA)                 {                     ExecuteMarketOrder(TradeType.Buy, Symbol, 1000, "Buy Order");                 }                 else if (currentPrice < currentSMA)                 {                     ExecuteMarketOrder(TradeType.Sell, Symbol, 1000, "Sell Order");                 }             }              Print("Current Price: {0}, Current SMA: {1}", currentPrice, currentSMA);         }     } }` |

This cBot uses `Color` data as a parameter to define the text visualisation in the chart area.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br> 2<br> 3<br> 4<br> 5<br> 6<br> 7<br> 8<br> 9<br>10<br>11<br>12<br>13<br>14<br>15<br>16<br>17<br>18<br>19<br>20<br>21<br>22<br>23 | `using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Linq; using System.Text; using cAlgo.API; using cAlgo.API.Collections; using cAlgo.API.Indicators; using cAlgo.API.Internals;  namespace cAlgo.Robots {     [Robot(AccessRights = AccessRights.None)]     public class NewcBot : Robot     {         [Parameter(DefaultValue = "Yellow")]         public Color TextColor { get; set; }          protected override void OnBar()          {             Chart.DrawStaticText("static", "cBot running!", VerticalAlignment.Center, HorizontalAlignment.Left, TextColor);         }     } }` |

In the example below, `double` data type serves as a parameter to input the order volume in lots. The cBot executes a buy market order after three consecutive red bars.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br> 2<br> 3<br> 4<br> 5<br> 6<br> 7<br> 8<br> 9<br>10<br>11<br>12<br>13<br>14<br>15<br>16<br>17<br>18<br>19<br>20<br>21<br>22<br>23<br>24<br>25<br>26<br>27<br>28<br>29<br>30<br>31<br>32<br>33<br>34<br>35<br>36<br>37<br>38<br>39<br>40<br>41<br>42<br>43<br>44<br>45<br>46<br>47<br>48<br>49<br>50<br>51<br>52<br>53 | `using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Linq; using System.Text; using cAlgo.API; using cAlgo.API.Collections; using cAlgo.API.Indicators; using cAlgo.API.Internals;  namespace cAlgo {     [Robot(TimeZone = TimeZones.UTC, AccessRights = AccessRights.None)]     public class RedBarsBot : Robot     {         [Parameter("Order Volume (Lots)", DefaultValue = 0.1)]         public double OrderVolume { get; set; }          private int redBarsCount = 0;          protected override void OnBar()         {             if (IsRedBar())             {                 redBarsCount++;                 if (redBarsCount == 3)                 {                     PlaceMarketOrder(TradeType.Buy);                     redBarsCount = 0;                 }             }             else             {                 redBarsCount = 0;             }         }          private bool IsRedBar()         {             var currentBar = MarketSeries.Close.Last(1);             var previousBar = MarketSeries.Close.Last(2);              return currentBar < previousBar;         }          private void PlaceMarketOrder(TradeType tradeType)         {             var symbol = Symbol;             var volume = Symbol.QuantityToVolume(OrderVolume);              ExecuteMarketOrder(tradeType, symbol, volume);         }     } }` |

Indicator examples[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/guides/parameter-types/#indicator-examples "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following indicator code illustrates how `TimeFrame` parameters are used.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br> 2<br> 3<br> 4<br> 5<br> 6<br> 7<br> 8<br> 9<br>10<br>11<br>12<br>13<br>14<br>15<br>16<br>17<br>18<br>19<br>20<br>21<br>22<br>23<br>24<br>25<br>26<br>27<br>28<br>29<br>30<br>31<br>32<br>33<br>34<br>35 | `using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Linq; using System.Text; using cAlgo.API; using cAlgo.API.Collections; using cAlgo.API.Indicators; using cAlgo.API.Internals;  namespace cAlgo {     [Indicator(AccessRights = AccessRights.None)]     public class NewIndicator4 : Indicator     {         private Bars _hourlyTimeFrameBars;         private Bars _targetTimeFrameBars;          [Parameter("Chosen Time Frame")]         public TimeFrame TargetTimeFrame { get; set; }          [Output("Main")]         public IndicatorDataSeries Result { get; set; }          protected override void Initialize()         {             _hourlyTimeFrameBars = MarketData.GetBars(TimeFrame.Hour);             _targetTimeFrameBars = MarketData.GetBars(TargetTimeFrame);         }          public override void Calculate(int index)         {             Result[index] = _hourlyTimeFrameBars.HighPrices[index] - _targetTimeFrameBars.HighPrices[index];         }     } }` |

There is a playful (colour-blind test) indicator, which offers `enum` colour vision options (for example, normal, colour-blind and greyscale) for users to determine the colour of a horizontal line drawn on the chart.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| 1<br> 2<br> 3<br> 4<br> 5<br> 6<br> 7<br> 8<br> 9<br>10<br>11<br>12<br>13<br>14<br>15<br>16<br>17<br>18<br>19<br>20<br>21<br>22<br>23<br>24<br>25<br>26<br>27<br>28<br>29<br>30<br>31<br>32<br>33<br>34<br>35<br>36<br>37<br>38<br>39<br>40<br>41<br>42<br>43<br>44<br>45<br>46<br>47<br>48<br>49<br>50<br>51<br>52<br>53 | `using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Linq; using System.Text; using cAlgo.API; using cAlgo.API.Collections; using cAlgo.API.Indicators; using cAlgo.API.Internals;  namespace cAlgo {      public enum ColorVision     {         Normal,         Colorblind,         Greyscale     }      [Indicator(AccessRights = AccessRights.None)]     public class ColorblindTest : Indicator     {          [Parameter("Color Vision", DefaultValue = ColorVision.Normal)]         public ColorVision ColorVision { get; set; }          public override void Calculate(int index) {}          protected override void Initialize()         {              Color lineColor = Color.Green;              switch (ColorVision)              {                 case ColorVision.Normal:                     lineColor = Color.Red;                     break;                 case ColorVision.Colorblind:                     lineColor = Color.Yellow;                     break;                 case ColorVision.Greyscale:                     lineColor = Color.White;                     break;              }              var trendLine = Chart.DrawHorizontalLine("line", Bars.HighPrices.Maximum(10), lineColor);         }      }  }` |

To summarise, by choosing the correct data type for the declared variables and class properties, you will be able to create cBots and indicators that can handle with even non-standard tasks.

Back to top

# Introduction - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/#introduction)

Introduction[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/#introduction "Permanent link")

======================================================================================================

Synchronisation with Cloud is a feature that allows you to run cBots in a dedicated environment independent of your local machine.

It eliminates the need for third-party services that provide similar environments. With Cloud, you can simply create an instance, start it in one click, and then close cTrader entirely or turn off your local machine. Your instance will continue running 24/7 unless you stop it manually or it encounters a technical issue.

Key features

*   Start cBots anywhere - regardless of where you are and what you are doing, you can start a cBot in just one click.
*   Launch and forget - your cBots will continue running even if you close cTrader or turn off your computer.
*   Customise your products - you can set the algorithm name, upload an avatar and add backtesting results to make your cBots more attractive to end users.

Note

You can simultaneously run 1 cBot instance on demo accounts and up to 10 cBot instances on live accounts in Cloud. Please note that these limits may vary depending on your broker.

Tip

Cloud supports cBots that have external dependencies. When creating new cBots, you can freely add third-party packages such as machine learning libraries. However, there are some requirements that your cBots need to meet to ensure that Cloud can fully execute code containing external dependencies. [**Read more here**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/requirements-for-cbots/)
.

How it works[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/#how-it-works "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### For users[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/#for-users "Permanent link")

You can start an instance of any cBot available to you in the **Algo** app. Instances can be run on any symbol available to your account and on any chart timeframe. Instances are launched and run in Cloud, which means that cTrader itself takes care to execute and maintain the algorithm.

As a result, there is no need to keep cTrader Windows open and active. You can also turn off your local machine and any cBots you may have launched will continue running.

Local instances

You can still start algorithms locally, in which case they will run on your machine and require cTrader Windows to be open.

Before launching a cBot, you have full control over the advanced parameters if the cBot contains them. You can have multiple instances of the same cBot running with different parameters or on different symbols and timeframes.

Note

cBots in Cloud are run in safe and isolated environments. Even if they have elevated access rights, algorithms cannot access your personal information, your system settings or any files saved on your device. Depending on their access rights, local instances can sometimes access data on your machine.

Here is how you can receive new algorithms:

*   Automatically. The Algo app always contains all algorithms available to you. If you have a partner and they decide to share their new algorithm with others, you should see the algorithm being added to the app. The same algorithms will be shown on your partner's profile page in the Partner app.
*   By double-clicking an `.algo` file shared with you on any channel. If you have access to the algorithm, it will be added to the Algo application.
*   By creating a new algorithm and then synchronising it with Cloud. You can still launch algorithms locally without enabling synchronisation.

For partners and developers[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/#for-partners-and-developers "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cTrader Algo provides unlimited opportunities for sharing algos with others and maximising your user and referral base.

Here are all the ways you can share algorithms:

*   Once you synchronise an algorithm with Cloud and enable sharing, the algorithm will be shown to your invited traders in their Algo apps. They can also be added to your cTrader profile page.
*   After sharing is enabled, you can also generate links to certain algorithms and send them to your invited traders. These links will not work for any users who are not among your invited traders.
*   You can simply share an `.algo` file with any user you want on any suitable channel. Provided you have allowed the user to access the algo, they will be able to launch your cBot in one or two clicks.

Note

While plugins and custom indicators can be freely shared using the above methods, only cBots can be run in Cloud. Users, however, can still run plugins and custom indicators locally.

You are also given several options for customising how your algorithms look and behave.

*   You can upload a custom image that will serve as an avatar for your algorithm. It will be displayed to all users.
*   You can customise whether your live account statistics are displayed on the cBot page. Note that these statistics will cover your entire account of choice, not just the trading operations performed by the cBot.
*   You can set default instance settings (such as symbol and timeframe) that will be provided to all users on every instance start.
*   You can show a backtesting report generated for a cBot on its page

Structure[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/#structure "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can also read detailed tutorials about the different aspects of synchronisation and running cBots in Cloud.

*   [**Manage synchronisation settings**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/managing-synchronisation-settings/)
    
*   [**Manage Cloud instance parameters**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/managing-instance-parameters/)
    
*   [**Customise an algorithm**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/customising-an-algo/)
    
*   [**Share an algorithm**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/sharing-an-algo/)
    
*   [**Requirements for cBots**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/requirements-for-cbots/)
    

There is also an extensive [**FAQ**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/faq/)
.

Back to top

---

# Introduction - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/#introduction)

Introduction[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/#introduction "Permanent link")

==================================================================================================

cTrader Algo is an app that gives all cTrader Mobile users the option to run cBots in Cloud on any device.

Key terms

*   **cBot** - an algorithm that can trade on your behalf or autonomously perform other actions such as placing stop losses for any new positions you open.
*   **algorithm** - a set of rules for performing certain trading operations under specific conditions.
*   **instance** - a specific realisation of an algorithm. An instance acts according to the instructions specified in the algorithm and under a certain set of customisable rules and parameters. cBot instances always have to be attached to a specific symbol and timeframe.
*   **Cloud** - a dedicated environment where instances execute their operations.

Key features

*   Start cBots anywhere. Regardless of where you are and what you are doing, you can start a cBot in just one tap.
*   Launch and forget. Your cBots will continue running even if you close cTrader or turn off your mobile phone.
*   Customise your products. You can upload an avatar and backtesting results to make your cBots stand out and make them more attractive to end users.

How it works[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/#how-it-works "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### For users[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/#for-users "Permanent link")

You can start an instance of any cBot available to you in the **Algo** app. Instances can be run on any symbol available to your account and on any chart timeframe. Instances are launched and run in Cloud, which means that cTrader itself takes care to execute and maintain the algorithm.

As a result, there is no need to keep cTrader Mobile (or even your device) open and active. Any launched cBots will be running 24/7 unless you stop them or they encounter an error.

Before starting a cBot, you have full control over the advanced parameters if the cBot contains them. You can have multiple instances of the same cBot running with different parameters or on different symbols and timeframes.

Note

*   Only cBots can be launched in Cloud. This functionality is unavailable for plugins and custom indicators.
    
*   cBots are run in safe and isolated environments. Algorithms cannot access your personal information, your system settings or any files saved on your device.
    
*   There is a limit on how many running instances can exist at any given time. You will not be able to start new instances if the limit is exceeded.
    

Here is how you can receive new algorithms:

*   Automatically. The **Algo** app always contains all algorithms available to you. If you have a partner and they decide to share their new algorithm with others, you should see the algorithm being added to the app. The same algorithms will be shown on your partner's profile page in the **Partner** app.
*   By tapping an `.algo` file shared with you on any channel. Your device should prompt you to open the file in a suitable application. Select cTrader and, if you have access to the algorithm, it will be added to the app.
*   By creating a new algorithm via cTrader Windows and then synchronising it with Cloud.

### For partners and developers[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/#for-partners-and-developers "Permanent link")

cTrader Algo provides unlimited opportunities for sharing algos with others and maximising your user and referral base.

Here are all the ways you can share algorithms on mobile devices:

*   Once you synchronise an algorithm with Cloud and enable sharing, the algorithm will be shown to your invited traders in their **Algo** apps. They can also be added to your cTrader profile page.
*   After sharing is enabled, you can also generate links to certain algorithms and send them to your invited traders. These links will not work for any users who are not among your invited traders.
*   You can simply share an `.algo` file with any user you want on any suitable channel. Provided you have allowed the user to access the algo, they will be able to launch your cBot with one tap.

Note

The above actions can also be performed with plugins and custom indicators. However, these types of algos can only be launched locally on cTrader Windows.

You are also given several options for customising how your algorithms look and behave.

*   You can upload a custom image that will serve as an avatar for your algorithm. It will be displayed to all users.
*   You can customise whether your live account statistics are displayed on the cBot page. Note that these statistics will cover your entire account of choice, not just the trading operations performed by the cBot.
*   You can set default instance settings (symbol, timeframe, etc.) that will be provided to all users on every instance start.

Structure[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/#structure "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can also read detailed tutorials about the different functionalities of cTrader Algo.

*   [**Starting, stopping and deleting an instance**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/starting-stopping-deleting-instance/)
    
*   [**Managing instance parameters**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/managing-instance-parameters/)
    
*   [**Customising an algorithm**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/customising-an-algo/)
    
*   [**Receiving and sharing an algorithm**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/sharing-an-algo/)
    

There is also a detailed [**FAQ**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/faq/)
.

Back to top

# cTrader Troubleshooting

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/troubleshooting/#unstable-or-slow-internet-connection)

Troubleshooting
===============

Unstable or Slow Internet Connection[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/troubleshooting/#unstable-or-slow-internet-connection "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The unstable internet connection can intercept the trading process. Note that an unstable connection isn't equal to a slow connection. It's highly recommended to check your internet connection with your Internet Service Provider or your system administrator.

The low bandwidth connection can also cause cTrader to restart the login process or even the connection fails.

To guarantee the best trading experience cTrader checks your connection to the proxy and if the connection bandwidth is unable to maintain the connection to a good level, which might result in, for example, a Trade with huge slippage, cTrader gets logged out and tries to log in again to find a proxy with lower latency to connect.

This is a safety mechanism, so you should just check your connection and log in again.

Experienced traders having multiple charts open need to have a connection with higher bandwidth than users that use just one or two charts simultaneously. Please, check the internet connection requirements and your internet connection before you start trading.

Possible Chart Inaccuracy[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/troubleshooting/#possible-chart-inaccuracy "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In very rare cases some false historical data could be stored in the cache. This could result in your chart showing a false value in a historical bar. This can be simply fixed by refreshing your charts. Right-click on the chart and click the refresh field to the bottom.

cTrader will clear the cache and redownload the historical data. Alternatively, you can clear the cache manually from your browser settings and refresh the page by pressing F5 on your keyboard.

Note

In some cases the inconsistent data displayed in the chart can be caused by the broker. If you couldn’t fix the problem by yourself, try to contact your broker.

Non-onboarding US Users[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/troubleshooting/#non-onboarding-us-users "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Why US Users Are Not Serviced[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/troubleshooting/#why-us-users-are-not-serviced "Permanent link")

cTrader does not offer its services to United States residents due to regulatory reasons. Contract for difference (CFD) trading is prohibited by the US financial authorities.

Warning

cTrader does not service US residents. An attempt to sign up/in will fail if you confirm your US residence.

Although we regret to lack any presence in the US market, other users worldwide may benefit from this situation. Owing to its compliance with the regulations of various other countries, cTrader remains a stable and reliable trading terminal, which is ready to address diverse needs in international markets. The global cTrader community remains truly multinational, without any mainstream group dominating it.

### Locale Detection[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/troubleshooting/#locale-detection "Permanent link")

Still, users from other than the US countries can accidentally see the following warning message when opening cTrader. This may occur because they are using a VPN service with a US IP address. Or it is the cTrader geo IP service that has detected the locale incorrectly.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/troubleshooting/img/01.PNG)

If you are facing this issue without real grounds, just confirm your non-US residence. For US residents, the cTrader app will forcibly shut down.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/troubleshooting/img/02.PNG)

You can always contact your broker directly regarding their countries of presence and other residence restrictions.

Need cTrader support?

Reach out through our official channels to resolve any issues:

*   Ask questions and connect with traders, developers and other users in the cTrader [**Discord Community**](https://go.spotware.com/discord)
    .
*   Browse archived discussions on the cTrader [**Community Forum**](https://community.ctrader.com/)
    . While the forum is no longer open for new posts, it is full of helpful answers to common issues.
*   Contact the cTrader Support Team at [**support@ctrader.com**](mailto:support@ctrader.com)
     for technical assistance.

Note

The more information on the problem is provided (detailed description, screenshots, etc.), the faster your issue will be solved.

Alternatively, use the hot keys Ctrl+Alt+Shift+T while using cTrader Windows application to invoke a Trobleshooting form.

Enter your information and click on 'Submit' to automatically send it to the support team.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/troubleshooting/img/troubleshooting-0.png)

Also, you can join our official [Telegram group](https://t.me/cTrader_Official)
 to ask your question or give your feedback.

---

# Introduction - Partner tools

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/#getting-started)

Getting started[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/#getting-started "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================

Introduction[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/#introduction "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This section of the Help Centre is dedicated to helping partners of all kinds achieve higher efficacy by fully leveraging valuable cTrader features in unique ways.

Who are partners?

Partners (IBs) work in close collaboration with their broker to attract other traders and incentivise their active live trading in various ways (sharing trading products, posting signals, offering technical analysis insights, providing strategies and more). To receive compensation for their work, partners enroll in their broker's referral programme; typically, this compensation takes the form of rebates.

If you have not considered branching out into partnership before, here are just some of the benefits you may gain when doing so:

*   Build and participate in trading communities where each participant shares knowledge.
*   Quickly and easily learn from others and improve your approach to trading by attaining feedback from your peers.
*   Attain a social media following from other traders who may consider your trading insight to be reliable and effective.
*   Potentially earn additional remuneration from your broker as part of the referral programme.

What is cTrader Invite?

To equip partners for winning referrals, cTrader offers the [**Invite**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/invite-link/)
 application with its handy functionalities to manage invite links and share QR codes, track invited traders' statistics, display personal contacts and promote partner products. cTrader Invite is an all-in-one solution allowing partners to boost the conversion of new traders into active investors.

If you are already a partner, the guides in this section should help you plan your activities and execute your plans to their fullest potential. If you are just starting out, our articles will become your top resource on the built-in cTrader features for partners.

Check the full [**list of partner tools**](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/list-of-tools/)
 offered by cTrader.

Structure[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/#structure "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Our tutorials are divided into sub-sections based on specific partner types to deliver the highest value. Note that it is impossible to avoid overlapping between these partner types, as partners often use a combined strategy.

*   [**For social traders**](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/for-social-traders/)
    . Here, you will learn how to take advantage of the socially-oriented cTrader tools.
*   [**For educators**](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/for-educators/)
    . The guides in this sub-section discuss how you can easily and effectively share knowledge with other traders.
*   [**For money managers**](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/for-money-managers/)
    . In this sub-section, we demonstrate how you can improve the way you trade on behalf of others.
*   [**For networkers and affiliates**](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/for-networkers-affiliates/)
    . The tutorials in this sub-section showcase how you can effectively generate traffic for your web resources or those owned by your broker.

---

# FAQ - cTrader Copy

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/faq/#faq)

FAQ[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/faq/#faq "Permanent link")

========================================================================

General[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/faq/#general "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### The ROI chart I am looking at does not show any information, why is this?

In most cases, the ROI chart does not show any dynamics because there was no activity during the reviewed period. Try reconfiguring the chart to display a longer period. If there is still no information, please report this problem to us.

### Can I trade myself when I am copying other strategies?

When you are copying a strategy, you can still place your own trades using a regular account. However, you cannot trade manually using a copy-trading account.

### Can live accounts copy demo strategies?

Yes, you can copy demo strategies with your live account. However, you should be aware that demo traders take more risks with virtual funds and may provide more aggressive strategies.

For investors[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/faq/#for-investors "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### How much do I need to pay to copy a strategy?

This fully depends on the copy-trading commission established by the strategy provider, which can include [**performance, management and volume fees**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/fees-calculation/)
 in any combination. Some strategies are provided on a free basis. Before you start copying a strategy, you will see all applicable fees on the **Start copying** button.

### What happens if the strategy provider stops providing the strategy that I am copying?

Your strategy provider can stop providing the strategy you are copying at any time. In this case, all your copied open positions will be closed, and all the unrealised performance and management fees will be realised. The preview status of your copy-trading account will be changed to "stopped". The stopped strategies can be restarted by the strategy provider and can be copied again by the investor unless they are removed.

### Can I close the trades that I have copied from my strategy provider?

All the positions that your strategy provider has opened on your behalf are listed in the **Positions** tab of your investment profile (just like your positions in a regular account). However, you can only view opened positions in a copy-trading account. The only way for investors to close all positions at once is to stop copying the strategy.

### How many strategy providers can I copy from?

When you start copying a strategy, you allocate a certain amount of funds from your regular account to a copy-trading account. This means that you can allocate funds to as many copy-trading accounts as your account balance allows. Technically, there is no limit on how many strategies you can copy on cTrader.

### What happens when I stop copying a strategy?

When you stop copying a strategy, all open positions copied from that strategy will be automatically closed for your copy-trading account, and the unrealised performance and management fees will be realised after that. This is because you cannot receive a close signal once you have stopped copying.

### Can I contact my strategy provider?

You can contact your strategy provider if they have specified their contacts in the strategy profile. Partners who provide strategies and have public profiles usually share their contact details with invited traders in the [**Partner**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/partner/)
 tab of the **Invite** application.

### Are the stop-loss and take-profit protections copied from the strategy I copy?

The protections (stop loss and take profit) are not copied by copy-trading accounts. However, the closing signal of the strategy is copied. Therefore, if a stop-loss or take-profit protection is executed by the strategy provider and the respective position is closed, this position will also be closed in the investor's copy-trading account.

### My copy-trading account history contains trades that do not exist in the strategy provider's history. Why is this happening?

After any balance operation in the investor's or strategy provider's account, cTrader Copy changes positions in the investor's copy-trading account to implement the equity-to-equity algorithm. In this case, the copy-trading account history can log the trades that are missing in the strategy provider's account.

### My copy-trading account history is missing trades that have been executed by the strategy provider. Why is this happening?

If a copy-trading account does not have sufficient funds to copy a strategy, or an investor does not have the trading instruments that the strategy provider is using, the respective trades of the strategy provider's account will not be copied, and therefore will not be logged in the investor's copy-trading account.

If the investor's account leverage is lower than the strategy provider's account leverage, there is a chance that the investor's free margin will not be enough to copy all the trades of this strategy, and the copy-trading account may reach the stop out earlier than the strategy provider's account. Such trades may fail to be copied, and therefore they will not be logged in the copy-trading account history.

### How can I check the fees that have been charged to my account when copying a strategy?

You can always check the strategy conditions in the [**Details**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/investing-in-strategies/)
 section of the investment profile. The applied performance and management fees are displayed in the **Transactions** tab of your copy-trading account. The volume fee can be located in the **History** tab, and the unrealised volume fee is available in the **Positions** tab. The respective columns are disabled by default but you can right-click the column headings and enable them from the drop-down.

### What if my account currency is other than USD? How are the commissions that I pay calculated?

The fees to be deducted will be converted from USD into the currency of your copy-trading account at the actual rate at the processing time.

For strategy providers[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/faq/#for-strategy-providers "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Who can see my strategy?

By default, anyone who can access cTrader Copy will see your strategy in the strategy list if it meets the [**inclusion requirements**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/becoming-a-strategy-provider/)
. However, strategy listing may vary from broker to broker. To start copying a strategy, investors will have to log in using their [**cTrader ID**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/)
 credentials. If your strategy is out of the strategy list, you can still offer it for copying via a direct link.

### Can I make my strategy completely private and visible only to certain investors?

No, you cannot make your strategy private. Even if your strategy is not shown in the common strategy list, anyone who has a direct link can access it. However, you can limit the visibility of your open positions in the [**strategy settings**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/becoming-a-strategy-provider/)
.

### How many strategies can I create?

On cTrader, there is no limit on the number of strategies you can create and provide. However, you will need a separate trading account for every new strategy you are creating. Note that new accounts are created with the broker and should be linked to your cTrader ID.

### Can I copy other strategy providers' strategies if I am already a strategy provider?

Yes, you can copy other strategies while providing your own strategy for copying. This is possible due to the allocation of a copy-trading account. When you start copying a strategy, a specified amount of funds is added from your regular account to your copy-trading account. As a result, the copy-trading account does not directly interact with the main account which provides a strategy for copying.

### Can I contact the investors who copy my strategy?

All investors will stay anonymous unless they initiate contact with you via the channels you can specify in the strategy profile. If you are promoting strategies as a partner, invited traders can see your contact details in the [**Partner**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/partner/)
 tab of cTrader Invite.

### Are my pending orders visible to other cTrader Copy users?

All limit, stop and stop limit orders with the applied protection mechanisms will remain hidden. Regarding your open positions, you can configure in the strategy settings who will see them: everyone, only investors or no one.

### Can I stop being a strategy provider?

Yes, you can stop providing a strategy at any time, then adjust its settings, and restart if needed. Note that you cannot delete strategies. They are [**auto-deleted**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/becoming-a-strategy-provider/)
 after a period of inactivity. When you stop a strategy, all open positions within this strategy will be closed for the investors who are copying it.

### Will my trades be copied by investors if I execute them from different platforms (cTrader Web, cTrader Mobile, etc.)?

You can trade on your strategy from any cTrader platform you consider convenient. All trades placed on your account will be automatically copied by your investors.

### How much can I charge for my strategy?

If you wish, you can provide your strategy on a free basis. Alternatively, you can establish [**three types of fees**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/fees-calculation/)
 in any combination: performance fee (up to 30% of the investor's net profit), management fee (up to 10% of the investor's equity) and volume fee (up to USD 10 per million volume).

### How will the fees be paid to me?

All copy-trading fees are paid by the broker as a deposit into an account other than the strategy account as soon as these fees are charged to the investor's copy-trading account.

### Are there any fees for providing strategies?

No, Spotware does not apply any fees to strategy providers.

### Can strategy providers charge commissions in lots instead of units?

No, you cannot configure the type of volume in which you charge for your strategy, everything is converted into units of USD volume.

Need cTrader support?

Reach out through our official channels to resolve any issues:

*   Ask questions and connect with traders, developers and other users in the cTrader [**Discord community**](https://go.spotware.com/discord)
    .
*   Browse archived discussions on the cTrader [**Community forum**](https://community.ctrader.com/)
    . While the forum is no longer open for new posts, it is full of helpful answers to common issues.
*   Contact the cTrader support team at [**support@ctrader.com**](mailto:support@ctrader.com)
     for technical assistance.

---

# Introduction - cTrader Copy

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/#introduction)

Introduction[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/#introduction "Permanent link")

======================================================================================

cTrader Copy is a fully integrated cTrader application and a flexible investment platform that allows for copying trading strategies, as well as providing one’s own strategies for copying by other traders.

It is an easy-to-use reliable solution with a clearly defined fee system and transparent strategy information and history that offers an easy copying mechanism for a better trading experience.

Once logged in to cTrader web application with [**cTrader ID**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/)
 (cTID), a trader can proceed to the Copy section, check the available strategies, compare their history, details and fees, allocate funds and start copying in two clicks.

All the strategies can be discovered and copied from the cTrader Copy main page without any long-term commitment, and any trader signed in with cTrader ID can invest in a strategy as long as their broker offers cTrader Copy.

While using cTrader Copy, you may encounter these terms:

*   **Strategy** – aggregated actions of a trader provided for copying to investors under certain conditions.
*   **Investor** – a trader who allocates a certain amount of funds from their trading account for copying a strategy under certain conditions (investing).
*   **Copy-trading account** – an account created by allocating a certain amount of funds from a trading account for copying a strategy.
*   **Strategy provider** – a social trader who provides their trading strategy for copying by other traders.

cTrader Copy offers a powerful [**toolkit for partners**](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/list-of-tools/)
 allowing them to effortlessly share strategies, promote them via different channels, earn investor fees and gain referrals.

General copying logic[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/#general-copying-logic "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The general logic of the copying mechanism in cTrader Copy is that an investor can allocate their funds to copy a specific trading strategy under specific conditions.

The funds allocated from the investor’s account are moved to a copy-trading account, which is a separate trading account under the same cTID that can be used only for copying the specific strategy.

A strategy provider executes trades from their trading account, and all investors copying the strategy automatically copy all its trades.

Example

If a strategy provider modifies the open positions on their strategy account (for example, closes some positions), the same actions will be copied for the investor as well.

Warning

cTrader Copy forbids copying any trades made for symbols representing stocks and shares. While a strategy provider can still place new orders and open new positions for these symbols (such as AAPL or TSLA), these trades will not be copied by their investors.

cTrader automatically adjusts the volume of open positions and future trades based on both the provider's and investor's withdrawals or deposits. The equity-to-equity ratio is used as the copying model of cTrader Copy.

Warning

The provider’s and investor’s prices may differ due to variations in trading conditions and execution time.

The strategy provider's trades will not be copied by the investor's copy-trading account in the following cases:

*   There are not enough funds in the investor’s account.
*   The investor does not have the trading instruments that the strategy provider is operating with.
*   The investor’s leverage is lower than the strategy provider’s leverage, and the investor’s free margin might not be enough to copy all trades from this strategy.

Equity-to-equity ratio[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/#equity-to-equity-ratio "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The copying model of cTrader Copy is based on the equity-to-equity ratio, which means that the volume of a trade that will be copied is defined according to both the strategy provider’s and the investor’s equities.

According to the equity-to-equity copying model, a volume to be copied for trade by a copy-trading account is calculated as follows:

`Investor’s Equity / Strategy Provider’s Equity * Strategy Provider’s Volume`

Example

The strategy provider's equity at the moment of copying is 4,000 USD, and the investor’s equity is 1,000 USD.

The provider opens 4 lots for trading.

This trade will be copied with the copying volume: 1,000 of the investor’s equity divided by 4,000 of the strategy provider equity and multiplied by 4 lots traded equals 1 lot, or:

`1,000 ÷ 4,000 × 4 = 1`

Example

An investor is going to copy a strategy with the following parameters:

*   Own funds: USD 100,000 - the funds on the strategy provider’s trading account. The strategy provider’s own funds can change depending on the provider’s deposits and withdrawals.
*   Min investment: USD 100.

An investor allocates 1,000 USD and starts copying this strategy. After some trades, the investor decides to stop copying. The copy-trading history will look as follows:

| Strategy history | Investor history |
| --- | --- |
| USD 1,000,000 → Buy EURUSD Entry 1.12261 - Close 1.12261 (com -0.10) = -0.06 USD 999,999.94 | Not copying yet |
| USD 999,999.94 → Sell EURJPY Entry 121.161 - Close 121.175 (com -9.00) = -20.55 → USD 999,979.39 | Starts copying: USD 1,000 → Sell EURJPY Entry 121.161 - Close 121.175 (com -0.06) = -0.19 → USD 999.81 |
| USD 999,979.39 → Buy EURJPY Entry 121.145 - Close 121.175 (com -9.00) = 98.19 → USD 1,000,077.58 | USD 999.81 → Buy EURJPY Entry 121.145 - Close 121.175 (com -0.06) = 1.15 → USD 1,000.96 |
| USD 1,000,077.58 → Buy EURJPY Entry 121.268 - Close 121.248 (com -9.00) = -25.50 → USD 1,000,052.08 | USD 1,000.96 → Buy EURJPY Entry 121.268 - Close 121.248 (com -0.06) = -0.25 → USD 1,000.71 |

Deposits and withdrawals[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/#deposits-and-withdrawals "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All deposits and withdrawals are taken into account, and the position sizes are kept relative to the amounts that each party has invested.

Warning

Depositing or withdrawing funds by the strategy provider to and from their strategy account with open positions can cause losses for investors' copy-trading accounts. Since equity is recalculated every time the account balance changes, the current open positions and future trades will be adjusted according to the equity-to-equity model.

Investors can add or remove funds to and from their copy-trading accounts at any time while copying strategies. Similarly, this will affect the volume of the copied positions and future trades.

Note

When the copied positions are recalculated due to balance operations (deposit or withdrawal) on the strategy provider’s or investor’s account, the volume fee will not be charged.

The amount deposited by an investor to a copy-trading account should be equal or higher than the minimum investment requirement when copying is in progress. This means that an investor can only withdraw amounts exceeding the minimum investment while copying a strategy.

If investors want to withdraw all funds including the minimum investment, they should stop copying the strategy and remove all funds. See our [**guide for investors**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/investing-in-strategies/)
 on how to add and remove funds.

Exceptions[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/#exceptions "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you start copying a strategy, all the existing open positions of the strategy will be opened for your copy-trading account with the current market rates, and all the strategy actions will start being copied.

In some cases, the copying mechanism may undergo some exceptions. For example:

*   In cases where, after calculation based on the equity-to-equity ratio, the trade volume in the copied strategy is lower than the minimum trading volume allowed by the investor’s broker, the trade size will be adjusted to the step allowed by the broker.
    
*   In cases when the volume of a trade to be executed in a copied strategy is higher than the maximum ticket size allowed by the broker, then no position will be opened.
    
*   In cases when the market is closed for trading a certain symbol in a strategy, the position for such a symbol will be opened only when the market is opened.
    
*   In case of insufficient margin on your account, the position will not be copied.

---

# Shared Access - cTrader Mobile (iOS)

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/shared-access-ios/#shared-access)

Shared Access[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/shared-access-ios/#shared-access "Permanent link")

================================================================================================================

Money managers and other partners have found it difficult to execute remote access to individual accounts for the purpose of trading on behalf of a third party. From a different perspective, having granted access to a partner, account owners would like to observe deals in real time and reserve the right to revoke access any moment.

To resolve these issues, the cTrader mobile app on iOS offers a built-in shared access feature. This guide will explain in detail how to share and revoke access in the cTrader mobile app on your Apple device.

Shared Access in One Minute!

*   Due to the shared access feature of cTrader on iOS, traders can grant access to money managers for trading on their behalf.
*   Users can share access from the ‘Manage Trading Accounts’ screen.
*   Once access is shared, any of the parties can promptly revoke/reject it by making just several taps on iPhone.
*   All trading decisions remain transparent and trackable, which contributes to an account owner’s sense of control. In addition, deposit and withdrawal operations remain open only for account holders.

Benefits of Shared Access[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/shared-access-ios/#benefits-of-shared-access "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Remote trading.** As a money manager, you are granted access to a third-party account. When a new cTrader account is shared with you, you can enjoy the full functionality of the shared account except for deposit/withdrawal operations and receiving/editing alerts.

**Full control and transparency.** As a cTrader account owner, you can delegate trading activities to more experienced partners without fear of losing your own access and transparency. You will be able to revoke access any moment you consider necessary.

**Win-win partnership.** When sharing accounts with experienced money managers, traders may generate higher streams of revenue due to skillful portfolio management. In turn, money managers will benefit from easily accessing all shared accounts in one place.

**Mobility and accessibility.** Shared access and further money/portfolio management are available on iPhone, which adds to accessibility and allows partners and account owners to interact with each other on the go.

How to Share Access[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/shared-access-ios/#how-to-share-access "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1\. To share access with a partner, open the hamburger menu in your cTrader app and tap on ‘Manage Trading Accounts’.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/01.png)

2\. If you have several accounts, you can choose which one to share. Tap on ‘Share Account’ under the corresponding account.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/02.png)

3\. You will then be asked to input an email or a cTrader ID (nickname) of the person you are sharing with and tap on ‘Next’. Note that an email should be registered as a cTrader user, and you cannot share access with yourself. If you acknowledge the consequences shown on the disclaimer, tap on ‘Share Access’. You will receive a notification email that your account has been shared.

Input Email or NicknameDisclaimer

[![Input Email or Nickname](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/03.png)](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/03.png)

[![Disclaimer](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/04.png)](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/04.png)

4\. Upon sharing, any party can see the account(s) shared by/with them in the drop-down lists in the hamburger menu and at the top of the screen.

Access to Shared Accounts AAccess to Shared Accounts B

[![Access to Shared Accounts A](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/05.png)](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/05.png)

[![Access to Shared Accounts B](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/06.png)](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/06.png)

5\. If you tap again on ‘Manage Trading Accounts’ in the hamburger menu, a separate section will list the accounts shared by you. Your partners’ email will be indicated in case you forget whom you have shared this account with.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/07.png)

How to Revoke Access[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/shared-access-ios/#how-to-revoke-access "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1\. You can easily revoke access to your account by tapping on ‘Revoke Access’ in the ‘Manage Trading Accounts’ section in the hamburger menu (see the screenshot above). A notification informing that you will be logged out of your account for security reasons will pop up.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/08.png)

2\. Access can be terminated from any end. If it is the partner who initiates the sharing cessation, they should open ‘Manage Trading Accounts’ in the hamburger menu and tap on 'Reject Access’ under the needed account shared with them. A confirmation notice will pop up to prevent users from an accidental rejection.

Reject AccessConfirm Rejecting?

[![Reject Access](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/09.png)](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/09.png)

[![Confirm Rejecting?](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/10.png)](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/img/sharing-features/shared-access/10.png)

After the access has been canceled, a notifying email will be sent to your address registered as a cTrader login. You can then share access with a different partner or with the same partner again.

To summarize, the easy access sharing functionality of the cTrader app on iOS enables account owners and their partners to collaborate effectively and generate higher revenue streams. Remote access to individual accounts is especially beneficial to money managers and partners who can use this feature for trading on behalf of a third party. Access can be revoked by any of the parties at any time, thus making the sharing feature totally controlled and safe.

# cTrader Mobile (iOS)

 

Home
====

This guide provides information on how to get started with the cTrader Mobile iOS platform, the trading and charting tools, and how to use them, the platfor's interface, and its additional functionality.

cTrader Mobile application is a fully functional iOS version of the cTrader trading platform with all the charting and trading features as the other cTrader versions.

As cTrader Mobile offers similar functionality for manual trading and charting to the other versions of the application, it allows users to get access to their trading accounts and trade from a variety of devices and operating systems.

To use the cTrader Mobile version it should be installed on your device.
