# Fees calculation - cTrader Copy

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/fees-calculation/#fees-calculation)

Fees calculation[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/fees-calculation/#fees-calculation "Permanent link")

===============================================================================================================

For investors[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/fees-calculation/#for-investors "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

While strategy providers can offer their strategies for copying on a free basis, it is also possible for providers to configure strategy fees in the [**Strategy Settings**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/becoming-a-strategy-provider/)
 window. The types of charges for investors include performance fee, management fee and volume fee, in any combination depending on the strategy provider's preferences.

An investor can instantly see all fees to be paid on the **Start copying** button before copying a strategy.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/img/fees-calculation/01.png)

### Performance fee[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/fees-calculation/#performance-fee "Permanent link")

Performance fee (**P**) is the amount investors will pay to the strategy provider based on the established percentage of their net profit calculated by means of the [**high-water mark (HWM) model**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/high-water-mark-model/)
.

The performance fee cannot exceed 30%.

### Management fee[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/fees-calculation/#management-fee "Permanent link")

Management fee (**M**) involves a periodic payment to the strategy provider for managing investors' funds regardless of the strategy performance. Management fees are accrued on a daily basis and set by the strategy provider as an annual percentage of the investor's equity.

The management fee cannot exceed 10%.

### Volume fee[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/fees-calculation/#volume-fee "Permanent link")

Volume fee (**V**) is the amount investors will pay to the strategy provider per one million of volume copied. It is calculated per side and added to each position. If a strategy provider charges, for example, USD 10 per million, and an investor copies the volume of one million, they will pay USD 10 to the strategy provider upon opening the position and USD 10 upon closing the position.

The volume fee is charged only for the strategy provider's trading actions and is not applied for balance operations (deposits or withdrawals).

The volume fee cannot exceed USD 10.

Example

Let us assume that an investor is planning to allocate USD 1,000 for copying a strategy with the following fees preset by the strategy provider.

*   Performance fee: 10%
*   Management fee: 5%
*   Volume fee: USD 5

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/img/fees-calculation/02.png)

This means that:

*   The investor will incur a fee amounting to 10% of the total net profit generated by the strategy. For instance, if the copy-trading account generates USD 700 as the total net profit, its holder will pay USD 70 for performance.
*   The investor will incur a charge of 5% of the total equity allocated to their copy-trading account annually, equating to USD 50 out of the allocated USD 1,000 per year. The management fee will constitute `1,000.00 * 0.05 / 365 = 0.14` per day.
*   The investor will be charged USD 5 for each USD million of the copied volume from the strategy, or USD 0.5 for one lot copied. The calculation of the volume fee for one lot of EURUSD will look as follows: `Volume fee = 1.19 * 100,000 / 10^6 * 5 * 2 = 1.19` This calculation is valid if:
    *   `1 lot = 100,000`
    *   `Current quote = 1.19/1.19`
    *   `EUR 100,000 = 1.19 * USD 100,000`
    *   `10^6 = 1 million`
    *   `2 = position sides (opening and closing)`

### Fee logs[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/fees-calculation/#fee-logs "Permanent link")

Investors can locate the applied performance and management fees on their [**copy-trading account page**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/investing-in-strategies/)
 in the **Transactions** tab.  
The volume fee log entry is available in the **History** tab. The unrealised volume fee can be found in the **Positions** tab.

Note

The **Volume fee** and the **Unr. volume fee** columns are disabled by default. Investors and strategy providers can right-click on any column heading in the **History** and **Positions** tabs and enable these columns for viewing in the drop-down menu.

For strategy providers[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/fees-calculation/#for-strategy-providers "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A strategy provider is free to decide whether to establish zero fees for copying their strategy for live and demo accounts, or charge fees in any combination but within the allowed boundaries discussed above.

The copy-trading commission is the sum of all three types of fees (performance, management and volume) charged to the investor's account.

Warning

Any of the fees can be adjusted by the strategy provider only after the strategy is stopped and no investors are copying it. New fees will be applied to the investors only after they restart copying the strategy.

Fees are paid to the strategy provider in the form of a deposit from the broker to one of the accounts specified by the provider earlier. The copy-trading commission is automatically converted to the provider's account currency. All fees paid by investors are deposited to the provider's account with a separate transaction per investor.

The broker will immediately reimburse the strategy provider for any realised performance or management fees applied to any investor. The volume fee will be charged to the investor's copy-trading account when the deals are executed, but it will be deposited to the provider's account along with the performance or management fee.

When fees are deposited to the provider's account, the strategy provider will automatically receive an email with a detailed description of the fees paid per strategy.

Fee payment time and terms (triggers)[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/fees-calculation/#fee-payment-time-and-terms-triggers "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All fees regardless of their type are charged to the investor's copy-trading account on the 1st of each month. The payout to the strategy provider's account will be made immediately after the investor is charged.

Additionally, the following events trigger the application of fees:

*   The strategy provider stops providing their strategy. In this case, all investors automatically stop copying it, and all open positions within the strategy will be automatically closed for the investors, and all unrealised fees will be charged after the positions are closed.
*   The investor stops copying the strategy themselves.
*   The investor withdraws funds from their copy-trading account (for example, removes funds to their main trading account). In this case, the platform also automatically recalculates the equity and adjusts the current and future trades according to the equity-to-equity model. If the investor removes funds while copying the strategy, all fees will be charged at once and in full.

Note

When the provider adds their own funds to the strategy account, the platform automatically decreases the investor's positions; and when the provider withdraws funds decreasing their balance, the platform automatically increases the investor's positions. Depositing the investor's fees to the provider's account does not trigger the recalculation of the investor's volume to avoid any unplanned volume corrections.

If the investor is copying several strategies, fees will be applied to each dedicated copy-trading account independently.

---

# Share an algorithm - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/sharing-an-algo/#share-an-algorithm)

Share an algorithm[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/sharing-an-algo/#share-an-algorithm "Permanent link")

==================================================================================================================================

If you are the author of an algorithm, you have several options for sharing it with other users.

*   Automatically
*   Via an `.algo` file
*   Via an invite link

Automatically[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/sharing-an-algo/#automatically "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can make it so that access to an algorithm is automatically given to all your [**invited traders**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/invited-traders/)
. Note that this is done on a per-algorithm basis.

To enable this functionality, do the following:

1.  Right-click an algorithm and select **Public settings**
2.  Navigate to the **Invite** section
3.  Tick the **Enable access to your invited traders** checkbox
4.  Click **Apply**

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/enabledInviteSharing.png)

Afterwards, your invited traders will see the algorithm in their cTrader **Algo** applications. If it is a cBot, invited traders will be able to create new Cloud instances of it.

Via an invite link[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/sharing-an-algo/#via-an-invite-link "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can also share an algorithm with your invited traders using a link.

To enable this functionality, perform the same steps as discussed in the previous section.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/shareViaInviteLink.png)

After access is enabled, cTrader will generate a link to the algorithm that will function as an invite link. As is the case with any other cTrader link, you can share it anywhere, and easily get new invited traders. Any of your invited traders who click the link will be able to access the algorithm.

Note

When you share an algorithm via an invite link, you do so for free. At the moment, cTrader does not offer a mechanism to charge traders for access to algorithms.

Profile page

If you want, your algos can also appear on your profile page as product cards. Your invited traders will be able to launch new instances and access algorithms directly from this page. If your profile is public, it can be accessed by traders by clicking your nickname in the **Author** field.

Via an .algo file[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/sharing-an-algo/#via-an-algo-file "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You have several options for sharing an `.algo` file containing your algorithm.

*   No source code, access per user

Note

If this option is chosen, only a specific user will be able to access the algorithm. If another user tries to launch the `.algo` file, they will encounter an error.

*   No source code, anyone can access
*   With source code, anyone can access

To create an `.algo` file, right-click any of your algorithms and select **Export** in the context menu.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/exportAlgorithmDesktop.png)

In the newly opened window, select the export option you would like to use. If you select the **No source code, access per user** option, also specify the nickname of the user with whom you are planning to share the algorithm.

After selecting the desired option, click **Export** to save the `.algo` file.

Note

You can freely send `.algo` files to users of cTrader Mobile. When an `.algo` file is opened on a mobile device, the user should select cTrader among the list of applications with which this file could be opened. Afterwards, the algorithm will be added to their **Algo** app.

Back to top

---

# Introduction - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/?q=#introduction)

Introduction[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/?q=#introduction "Permanent link")

=====================================================================================================

cTrader Algo is an app that gives all cTrader Mobile users the option to run cBots in Cloud on any device.

Key terms

*   **cBot** - an algorithm that can trade on your behalf or autonomously perform other actions such as placing stop losses for any new positions you open.
*   **algorithm** - a set of rules for performing certain trading operations under specific conditions.
*   **instance** - a specific realisation of an algorithm. An instance acts according to the instructions specified in the algorithm and under a certain set of customisable rules and parameters. cBot instances always have to be attached to a specific symbol and timeframe.
*   **Cloud** - a dedicated environment where instances execute their operations.

Key features

*   Start cBots anywhere. Regardless of where you are and what you are doing, you can start a cBot in just one tap.
*   Launch and forget. Your cBots will continue running even if you close cTrader or turn off your mobile phone.
*   Customise your products. You can upload an avatar and backtesting results to make your cBots stand out and make them more attractive to end users.

How it works[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/?q=#how-it-works "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### For users[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/?q=#for-users "Permanent link")

You can start an instance of any cBot available to you in the **Algo** app. Instances can be run on any symbol available to your account and on any chart timeframe. Instances are launched and run in Cloud, which means that cTrader itself takes care to execute and maintain the algorithm.

As a result, there is no need to keep cTrader Mobile (or even your device) open and active. Any launched cBots will be running 24/7 unless you stop them or they encounter an error.

Before starting a cBot, you have full control over the advanced parameters if the cBot contains them. You can have multiple instances of the same cBot running with different parameters or on different symbols and timeframes.

Note

*   Only cBots can be launched in Cloud. This functionality is unavailable for plugins and custom indicators.
    
*   cBots are run in safe and isolated environments. Algorithms cannot access your personal information, your system settings or any files saved on your device.
    
*   There is a limit on how many running instances can exist at any given time. You will not be able to start new instances if the limit is exceeded.
    

Here is how you can receive new algorithms:

*   Automatically. The **Algo** app always contains all algorithms available to you. If you have a partner and they decide to share their new algorithm with others, you should see the algorithm being added to the app. The same algorithms will be shown on your partner's profile page in the **Partner** app.
*   By tapping an `.algo` file shared with you on any channel. Your device should prompt you to open the file in a suitable application. Select cTrader and, if you have access to the algorithm, it will be added to the app.
*   By creating a new algorithm via cTrader Windows and then synchronising it with Cloud.

### For partners and developers[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/?q=#for-partners-and-developers "Permanent link")

cTrader Algo provides unlimited opportunities for sharing algos with others and maximising your user and referral base.

Here are all the ways you can share algorithms on mobile devices:

*   Once you synchronise an algorithm with Cloud and enable sharing, the algorithm will be shown to your invited traders in their **Algo** apps. They can also be added to your cTrader profile page.
*   After sharing is enabled, you can also generate links to certain algorithms and send them to your invited traders. These links will not work for any users who are not among your invited traders.
*   You can simply share an `.algo` file with any user you want on any suitable channel. Provided you have allowed the user to access the algo, they will be able to launch your cBot with one tap.

Note

The above actions can also be performed with plugins and custom indicators. However, these types of algos can only be launched locally on cTrader Windows.

You are also given several options for customising how your algorithms look and behave.

*   You can upload a custom image that will serve as an avatar for your algorithm. It will be displayed to all users.
*   You can customise whether your live account statistics are displayed on the cBot page. Note that these statistics will cover your entire account of choice, not just the trading operations performed by the cBot.
*   You can set default instance settings (symbol, timeframe, etc.) that will be provided to all users on every instance start.

Structure[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/?q=#structure "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can also read detailed tutorials about the different functionalities of cTrader Algo.

*   [**Starting, stopping and deleting an instance**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/starting-stopping-deleting-instance/)
    
*   [**Managing instance parameters**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/managing-instance-parameters/)
    
*   [**Customising an algorithm**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/customising-an-algo/)
    
*   [**Receiving and sharing an algorithm**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/sharing-an-algo/)
    

There is also a detailed [**FAQ**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/faq/)
.

Back to top

# Start, stop and delete an instance - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/starting-stopping-deleting-instance/#start-stop-and-delete-an-instance)

Start, stop and delete an instance[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/starting-stopping-deleting-instance/#start-stop-and-delete-an-instance "Permanent link")

=================================================================================================================================================================================

Start an instance[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/starting-stopping-deleting-instance/#start-an-instance "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

After receiving access to a cBot, you can create a new instance as follows:

*   In the **cBots** tab of the **Algo** application, tap the **Add instance** button located below the list of all currently running instances of a specific cBot.
*   Open the algo page for a specific cBot, and tap the green **Start cBot** button at the bottom.

You will be presented with a screen allowing you to set up your new instance. The screen will show the following:

*   The account the instance will run on.
*   The symbol and timeframe to which the instance is supposed to be attached.
*   Any advanced parameters that the cBot has.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/instanceCreationMobile.jpg)

After configuring the instance, tap the green **Start cBot** button at the bottom.

Your instance will also be added to the instance list in the **cBots** tab. If it stops for whatever reason, tap the green **Play** icon to restart it or go to the instance screen and tap **Start cBot instance**.

Stop an instance[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/starting-stopping-deleting-instance/#stop-an-instance "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You have two options for stopping a running instance.

*   In the **cBots** tab, tap the grey **Stop** icon to the right of the instance name.
*   Tap an instance to see its dedicated page. At the bottom, tap the grey **Stop cBot instance** button.

Note that, after being stopped, your instance will not be deleted; it will still be shown in the **cBots** tab. You can restart it by tapping the green **Play** button in the cBot list or the **Start cBot instance** on the instance screen.

Note

Apart from stopping it manually, an instance can also be stopped automatically for various reasons such as a lack of response from the server.

Delete an instance[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/starting-stopping-deleting-instance/#delete-an-instance "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can delete an instance by tapping it and opening its page. Then, tap the **three dots** icon in the bottom right corner to open the context menu. In the menu, tap **Delete cBot instance**.

Note

It is only possible to delete stopped instances. You cannot delete an instance that is currently running.

After deleting an instance, you still retain access to the algorithm. If you delete all instances of a particular algorithm, you will still be able to create new instances of this algorithm.

Back to top

---

# Manage instance parameters - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/managing-instance-parameters/#manage-instance-parameters)

Manage instance parameters[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/managing-instance-parameters/#manage-instance-parameters "Permanent link")

===========================================================================================================================================================

For traders[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/managing-instance-parameters/#for-traders "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A cBot instance always has the following parameters:

*   The trading account whose funds the instance will use for trading
*   The symbol to which the instance is attached
*   The time frame to which an instance is attached

Depending on the algorithm, instances may also have advanced parameters. These parameters are defined entirely by the algorithm author.

You have two options for managing instance parameters:

*   In the window that opens when creating a new instance
*   In the **Parameters** tab of the page of an already existing instance

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/instanceParametersMobile.jpg)

Note

*   It is only possible to modify the parameters of a stopped instance. Before changing the parameters of an existing instance, make sure it is stopped.
    
*   Be cautious when modifying additional parameters as these can significantly change how a cBot behaves. If you are unsure what the optimal settings are, contact the algo author for assistance. The author's contact details can be found in their profile which can be accessed by tapping the author's nickname in the **Author** field.
    

After defining parameters, tap the **Start cBot** or **Start cBot instance** button to start the instance with your chosen settings.

For algo authors[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/managing-instance-parameters/#for-algo-authors "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As an algo author, you can customise what parameter values are considered the default for all new instances. When a user attempts to add a new instance of your algorithm, your chosen default settings will be presented to them first. However, users will still be able to override them freely.

To customise the default parameter values, do the following:

1.  Open the page for one of your algorithms
2.  Tap the **three dots** icon in the bottom-right corner
3.  Select **Public settings**
4.  In the newly appeared screen, switch to the **Parameters** tab
5.  Set default instance parameters and tap **Apply**

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/defaultInstanceParamsMobile.jpg)

Back to top

---

# For money managers - Partner tools

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/for-money-managers/#for-money-managers)

For money managers[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/for-money-managers/#for-money-managers "Permanent link")

======================================================================================================================

Who are money managers[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/for-money-managers/#who-are-money-managers "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Money managers are skilled and experienced traders who trade on behalf of others. This is usually done through a shared investment pool or by providing a money manager with access to their client's trading account.

Here are the key features cTrader offers for money managers:

*   [**cTrader Copy**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-copy/)
    
*   [**cTrader Algo**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/)
    
*   Investor Access
*   [**Shared Access**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mobile-ios/shared-access-ios/)
    

Guides for money managers[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/for-money-managers/#guides-for-money-managers "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To learn more about being an effective money manager, read the following tutorials:

*   [**Boost your authority the cTrader way**](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/how-to-boost-your-authority-the-ctrader-way/)
    . This tutorial explains how you can build your authority by leveraging cTrader features.

# Chart Trading - cTrader Windows

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/chart-trading/#chart-trading)

Chart Trading[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/chart-trading/#chart-trading "Permanent link")

=========================================================================================================

cTrader allows creating orders, price alerts, modifying the open positions as well as setting and modifying the Stop Loss and Take Profit protections directly in the chart.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/chart-trading-0.png)

QuickTrade[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/chart-trading/#quicktrade "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To enter a trade in the QuickTrade mode directly from the chart, hover over the Sell or Buy QuickTrade buttons to the top of the chart - the volume selector will appear automatically between them.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/chart-trading-1.png)

Adjust the volume of the trade with the toggles, by typing in the desired value, or selecting the value from the drop-down, and click Buy or Sell button (or double-click depending on your QuickTrade settings) to send an order.

The order will be sent immediately and the respective notification will pop-up to the upper right (if the notifications are enabled).

Note

Depending on the QuickTrade settings, cTrader will either send an order or pop-up the Create Order menu.

Drag and drop the Buy Limit/Stop Order box to the right (or Sell Limit/Stop Order box to the left) to place a Limit or Stop Order. The orders below and between the spread are the Buy Limit Orders, above the spread - the Buy Stop Orders, above and between the spread - the Sell Limit Orders, below the spread - the Sell Stop Orders.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/chart-trading-2.png)

The pending orders will display in the chart right on the price level you have placed them. You can drag and drop them to modify orders directly in the chart.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/chart-trading-3.png)

Please check the Application Settings section for more details on how the QuickTrade works.

Orders[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/chart-trading/#orders "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Alternatively, to create orders directly from the chart, right-click on the desired price level and select Create New Order to proceed to the New Order menu, or select Sell/Buy Limit, Buy/Sell Stop, or Buy/Sell Limit to create the respective order directly on the desired price level.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/chart-trading-4.png)

Note

The bid and ask price lines are moving up and down with each tick of the current spot price.

Therefore, depending on where you are going to create an order (above the bid price or below the Ask price), cTrader will automatically suggest the direction of the order - Buy Limit, Sell Stop and Sell Stop Limit below the Ask price, or Sell Limit, Buy Stop and Buy Stop Limit above the bid price.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/chart-trading-5.png)

The new order badge will appear on the chart. It contains the number of lots being traded, the pips, the current position net profit or loss, as well as the managing and protection buttons. Hover over the Order badge to see its detailed information.

To modify your order, drug and drop it along the price axis or double-click to open the Modify Order menu. To modify Stop Loss or Take Profit protections, drag and drop the respective badge below or above the order badge.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/chart-trading-6.png)

Price Alerts[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/chart-trading/#price-alerts "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To set the Price Alerts directly from the chart, right-click on the desired price level and select the desired Price Alert at Bid or Ask price.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/chart-trading-7.png)

The Price Alert line will automatically appear on the chart. Drag and drop the Price Alert badge to modify the price alert, or use the "x" button to cancel it.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/chart-trading-8.png)

Positions[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/chart-trading/#positions "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When you open a position, the position badge becomes visible on the symbol chart. It contains the number of lots trading, the pips, the current position net profit or loss, as well as the managing and protection buttons. You can see the Position reflected on your Chart as a dash and dot line.

If you don't see your current trading positions in the chart, hover over the View Options icon () to the top of the chart to make sure Orders and Positions are selected.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/chart-trading-9.png)

Double-click on the position badge in the chart to open the Modify Position menu.

Click and drag the TP or SL sliders to the desired price level to set or modify the Stop Loss or Take Profit protections. The standard Advanced Protection, Reverse, and Double buttons are available right next to the position preview.

Hover over the current Position or Order line to highlight the line and the corresponding Order/Position in TradeWatch and its open price on the price axis.

The dot on the position line shows the time when this order has been created.

Click the "x" button next to the desired position to close it.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/chart-trading-10.png)

Pending Orders[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/chart-trading/#pending-orders "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When creating a Pending Order, you can see it reflected on the symbol chart as a dash and dot line with the respective order badge. Click on the desired order icon to open the Modify Order menu.

The Stop Loss and Take Profit levels are reflected on the Chart as dotted lines above and beneath the Order Line.

Until the current price reaches the price you set for a pending order to be filled, you can drag and drop the order badge to change the price.

You can also drag and drop both Stop Loss and Take Profit lines. Use the respective badges to the left to do so.

If you would like to cancel Pending Order or remove Stop Loss/Take Profit, click on the "x" button next to the respective badge.

The dot on the order line shows the time when this order has been created.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/chart-trading-11.png)

---

# Market Replay - cTrader Windows

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/market-replay/#market-replay)

Market Replay[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/market-replay/#market-replay "Permanent link")

=========================================================================================================

The Market Replay feature allows you to metaphorically travel back in time to refine your manual trading skills on historical market data. Think of it as a chance to ‘relive’ certain market conditions and test various approaches to trading without risking any real funds.

Consult the bullet points below to learn how Market Replay works in one minute or less!

Market Replay in One Minute!

*   To use Market Replay, click on the ‘Market Replay’ icon in the toolbar to the right of the traded chart.
*   On the chart itself, choose a start date, adjust other settings, and click on ‘Play’. When the historical trading chart loads, freely place orders and analyze your reactions to certain market movements!
*   Dynamically regulate the playback speed to fast-forward to certain events and evaluate the long-term performance of the positions you have opened!
*   Analyze your trading strategies by using the equity chart and detailed trade statistics! Use this information on subsequent replays to grow your trading skills.

This extended guide explains where Market Replay can be accessed from, how it can be configured, and why it constitutes a great tool for designing and improving trading strategies.

How to Start Market Replay[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/market-replay/#how-to-start-market-replay "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In cTrader Windows, you can find the ‘Market Replay’ icon at the bottom of the toolbar which is to the right of the traded chart.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/01.png)

After clicking on the ‘Market Replay’ icon, you should move back the pointer along the chart timeframe and choose a moment from which the playback will start. The settings window will offer to specify the following initial parameters of the Market Replay.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/02.png)

**Starting capital** - Input your starting capital. Since you simulate trading on historical data, any amount may be added here.

**Start date and time** - You can adjust the start date and time if your initial choice was inaccurate.

**Data** - Choose between m1 open prices and the most precise real historical tick data.

**Speed** - Set the playback speed.

Click on ‘Start’ to open the Market Replay interface in a new window. Note that cTrader will need time to load data, and the loading speed will depend on the selected data type.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/03.png)

The Market Replay UI consists of six key elements.

*   Market Replay controls including the ‘Play/Pause’ and ‘Stop’ icons, the ‘Fast-forward 1 bar’ icon, the ‘Add timestamp’ icon, the calendar progress bar, and the playback speed slider.
    
*   Trading chart for the currently selected symbol.
    
*   Tradewatch with five tabs, such as ‘Positions’, ‘Orders’, ‘History’, ‘Equity’, and ‘Trade Statistics’.
    
*   ‘New Order’ tab.
    
*   Market Replay ‘Settings’ tab.
    
*   ‘Timeline’ tab.
    

How to Adjust Market Replay Settings[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/market-replay/#how-to-adjust-market-replay-settings "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When the Market Replay is running, you can return to the ‘Settings’ tab and adjust your initial parameters. To do so, first, click on the ‘Stop’ icon located in the upper-left corner of the Market Replay window.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/04.png)

The ‘Settings’ tab is now active and you can configure the next parameters.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/05.png)

**Date Range** - Use the left and right date/time menus to specify the start and end times for which Market Replay should load historical data. For your convenience, a calendar grid will appear after clicking on the arrow to the right of the date.

**Starting Capital** - Increase or decrease the initial starting capital. We recommend trying different amounts of starting capital to get a feel for your risk tolerance levels.

**Data** - Two types of data are available for Market Replay.

*   **m1 bars from Server.** Market Replay will source m1 data using open prices only. The data you will trade on may be slightly inaccurate compared to tick data. Nonetheless, selecting this option is fully justified when you wish to quickly load historical data over a large period.
    
*   **Tick data from Server.** Market Replay will source tick data directly from the trading server. Selecting this data type means that you will trade on data that is highly accurate. Although, this option may also become resource-intensive.
    

Note

If the ‘m1 bars from Server’ data is selected, you need to specify the spread manually. For the ‘Tick data from Server’ data, the spread is determined based on historical data.

**Spread** - Either type in or select a certain fixed spread value. Alternatively, click on ‘Random’ to configure a random spread value ranging between the specified minimum and maximum boundaries.

How to Trade on Historical Data[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/market-replay/#how-to-trade-on-historical-data "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Make sure that the Market Replay with your adjusted parameters is launched again. Use the controls in the top left corner of the Market Replay window for this purpose.

Now, the trading chart starts outputting price data, and you can enjoy the standard functionality of cTrader for trading operations and technical analysis. For example, you may draw Fibonacci fans, place horizontal lines, or do market snapshots due to the toolbar which is located to the right of the chart. The trend lines below attract attention to certain market movements.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/06.png)

You can also adjust the playback speed dynamically by moving the ‘Speed’ slider, inputting values manually, or using up and down arrows. This functionality allows for fast-forwarding to certain events or, alternatively, slowing down the action to make a weighted trading decision.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/07.png)

As with the standard UI of cTrader, you can place an order in three different ways.

1\. Use the ‘New Order’ tab located to the right of the trading chart.

2\. Use QuickTrade buttons available at the top of the trading chart.

3\. Right-click within the chart area and place an order via the context menu.

Close your currently open positions one by one or altogether from the ‘Trade Watch’ tab below the trading chart.

The calendar slider at the top of the screen will progress from left to right matching your chosen playback speed. You cannot rewind the Market Replay using the calendar slider, but you can jump ahead to any moment by dragging the slider right. In addition to the ‘Play/Pause’ and ‘Stop’ controls, use the ‘Forward 1 Bar’ icon to allow only one bar at a click (according to the chosen timeframe).

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/08.png)

How to Add Timestamps[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/market-replay/#how-to-add-timestamps "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Think of timestamps as ‘bookmarks’ which you can place on certain points of the timeline and quickly fast-forward to these events.

To add timestamps while a Market Replay, click on the ‘Add timestamp’ icon to the left of the calendar slider. You can also drag the slider right (not left) to any moment and press ‘Add timestamp’.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/09.png)

A new timestamp will appear just above the calendar slider. To place timestamps up or down the date range, simply right-click on the calendar slider at an approximate place you would like to bookmark.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/10.png)

To edit the timestamp position, drag-and-drop a timestamp along the calendar slider. Alternatively, right-click on it to open the ‘Edit timestamp’ menu. Here, you can edit the date and the exact time.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/11.png)

How to Analyze Replay Performance[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/market-replay/#how-to-analyze-replay-performance "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In addition to the ‘Positions’, ‘Orders’, and ‘History’ tabs, the ‘Trade Watch’ display in the Market Replay window also shows the ‘Equity’ and ‘Trade Statistics’ tabs. The ‘Equity’ tab offers a dynamic equity chart that acts as a great visual aid for evaluating trader performance.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/12.png)

On the equity chart, the X axis illustrates the total number of trades you have made. The Y axis tracks funds.

Note

Market Replay does not affect any funds in your actual trading account be it Live or Demo.

In turn, the ‘Trade Statistics’ tab provides the following summary.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/13.png)

The chart highlights important statistics, such as the total number of winning trades you have made.

To associate your performance with certain events that have occurred throughout the Market Replay, turn to the ‘Timeline’ tab. It contains a dynamically updated record of all timestamps you have added and all orders you have placed during the currently active Market Replay.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/14.png)

Additionally, you can edit timestamps directly from the ‘Timeline’ tab. After hovering over a timestamp, a ‘Cog’ icon will appear to the left of the ‘Bin’ icon. You can set a new date and time after clicking on it.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/15.png)

At the bottom of the ‘Timeline’ tab, there are several controls that allow for customizing the display filters, adding a timestamp right from this tab, and exporting/importing timestamps.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/trading/market-replay/16.png)

This functionality is particularly useful when you want to retry certain Market Replays and would like to save time on timestamp management. You should download and upload .rptm files for this purpose.

Key Benefits of Market Replay[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/trading/market-replay/#key-benefits-of-market-replay "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Market Replay is an invaluable tool for evaluating and refining one’s manual trading skills. Specifically, it provides the following benefits.

**Safe environment.** You can use the standard order functionality from cTrader to emulate trading without risking real funds in Market Replay.

**Quick strategy testing.** Due to the playback speed feature, Market Replay allows for quickly testing your manual trading strategies in the short and long-term.

**Easy navigation.** Traders do not waste time on repeated replays, as Market Replay allows for managing timestamps in just a few clicks.

**Trackable statistics.** Market Replay offers a detailed timeline and trading statistics, giving users accurate information for refining a trading strategy.

To summarize, Market Replay is a customizable tool for testing and enhancing one’s fundamental trading skills. Even if you consider yourself an experienced trader, you can still refine your strategy trading on historical market data without the risk of losing real funds.

---

# Introduction - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/?q=#introduction)

Introduction[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/?q=#introduction "Permanent link")

=========================================================================================================

Synchronisation with Cloud is a feature that allows you to run cBots in a dedicated environment independent of your local machine.

It eliminates the need for third-party services that provide similar environments. With Cloud, you can simply create an instance, start it in one click, and then close cTrader entirely or turn off your local machine. Your instance will continue running 24/7 unless you stop it manually or it encounters a technical issue.

Key features

*   Start cBots anywhere - regardless of where you are and what you are doing, you can start a cBot in just one click.
*   Launch and forget - your cBots will continue running even if you close cTrader or turn off your computer.
*   Customise your products - you can set the algorithm name, upload an avatar and add backtesting results to make your cBots more attractive to end users.

Note

You can simultaneously run 1 cBot instance on demo accounts and up to 10 cBot instances on live accounts in Cloud. Please note that these limits may vary depending on your broker.

Tip

Cloud supports cBots that have external dependencies. When creating new cBots, you can freely add third-party packages such as machine learning libraries. However, there are some requirements that your cBots need to meet to ensure that Cloud can fully execute code containing external dependencies. [**Read more here**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/requirements-for-cbots/)
.

How it works[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/?q=#how-it-works "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### For users[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/?q=#for-users "Permanent link")

You can start an instance of any cBot available to you in the **Algo** app. Instances can be run on any symbol available to your account and on any chart timeframe. Instances are launched and run in Cloud, which means that cTrader itself takes care to execute and maintain the algorithm.

As a result, there is no need to keep cTrader Windows open and active. You can also turn off your local machine and any cBots you may have launched will continue running.

Local instances

You can still start algorithms locally, in which case they will run on your machine and require cTrader Windows to be open.

Before launching a cBot, you have full control over the advanced parameters if the cBot contains them. You can have multiple instances of the same cBot running with different parameters or on different symbols and timeframes.

Note

cBots in Cloud are run in safe and isolated environments. Even if they have elevated access rights, algorithms cannot access your personal information, your system settings or any files saved on your device. Depending on their access rights, local instances can sometimes access data on your machine.

Here is how you can receive new algorithms:

*   Automatically. The Algo app always contains all algorithms available to you. If you have a partner and they decide to share their new algorithm with others, you should see the algorithm being added to the app. The same algorithms will be shown on your partner's profile page in the Partner app.
*   By double-clicking an `.algo` file shared with you on any channel. If you have access to the algorithm, it will be added to the Algo application.
*   By creating a new algorithm and then synchronising it with Cloud. You can still launch algorithms locally without enabling synchronisation.

For partners and developers[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/?q=#for-partners-and-developers "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cTrader Algo provides unlimited opportunities for sharing algos with others and maximising your user and referral base.

Here are all the ways you can share algorithms:

*   Once you synchronise an algorithm with Cloud and enable sharing, the algorithm will be shown to your invited traders in their Algo apps. They can also be added to your cTrader profile page.
*   After sharing is enabled, you can also generate links to certain algorithms and send them to your invited traders. These links will not work for any users who are not among your invited traders.
*   You can simply share an `.algo` file with any user you want on any suitable channel. Provided you have allowed the user to access the algo, they will be able to launch your cBot in one or two clicks.

Note

While plugins and custom indicators can be freely shared using the above methods, only cBots can be run in Cloud. Users, however, can still run plugins and custom indicators locally.

You are also given several options for customising how your algorithms look and behave.

*   You can upload a custom image that will serve as an avatar for your algorithm. It will be displayed to all users.
*   You can customise whether your live account statistics are displayed on the cBot page. Note that these statistics will cover your entire account of choice, not just the trading operations performed by the cBot.
*   You can set default instance settings (such as symbol and timeframe) that will be provided to all users on every instance start.
*   You can show a backtesting report generated for a cBot on its page

Structure[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/?q=#structure "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can also read detailed tutorials about the different aspects of synchronisation and running cBots in Cloud.

*   [**Manage synchronisation settings**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/managing-synchronisation-settings/)
    
*   [**Manage Cloud instance parameters**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/managing-instance-parameters/)
    
*   [**Customise an algorithm**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/customising-an-algo/)
    
*   [**Share an algorithm**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/sharing-an-algo/)
    
*   [**Requirements for cBots**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/requirements-for-cbots/)
    

There is also an extensive [**FAQ**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/faq/)
.

Back to top

# FAQ - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/faq/#faq)

FAQ[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/faq/#faq "Permanent link")

========================================================================================

What is the best cBot?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/faq/#what-is-the-best-cbot "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There is no cBot that realistically could be classified as the best. cBots perform various functions depending on the intentions of their authors. Using a cBot does not guarantee earning profits. When using a cBot, you accept full risk for any actions it may perform.

Can I create a new algorithm in cTrader Web?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/faq/#can-i-create-a-new-algorithm-in-ctrader-web "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

No, creating a new algorithm is only possible via cTrader Windows.

Can I see the source code of an algorithm?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/faq/#can-i-see-the-source-code-of-an-algorithm "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are not the author of an algorithm, you can only see its source code with explicit permission from the owner and only on cTrader Windows.

Why did one of my algorithms disappear?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/faq/#why-did-one-of-my-algorithms-disappear "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The most probable cause is that the author has chosen to stop providing this product. If the author is your partner in the **Partner** app, you can go to their profile and see their contact details (if there are any). Contact your partner if you want them to restore the algorithm.

Note

You can also access the profile by tapping the nickname of the author in the **Author** field in the algorithm page.

One of my cBot instances has suddenly stopped or crashed. Why?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/faq/#one-of-my-cbot-instances-has-suddenly-stopped-or-crashed-why "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In rare cases, an instance may encounter an error and cease execution. If you go to the **Instance information** tab on the instance page, you should see a detailed log of actions performed by the instance. This journal should also contain the exact event when the instance encountered an error.

Instances run in Cloud on live accounts until you stop them, whereas the cloud execution of cBot instances on demo accounts is automatically stopped seven days after their start. To restart your instance, click **Start cBot instance** on the instance page or the green **Play** icon in the algorithm list.

How many cBot instances can I start in Cloud?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/faq/#how-many-cbot-instances-can-i-start-in-cloud "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can simultaneously run one cBot instance on demo accounts and up to ten cBot instances on live accounts in Cloud. Please note that these limits may vary depending on the broker.

Why can I not run custom indicators and plugins in Cloud?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/faq/#why-can-i-not-run-custom-indicators-and-plugins-in-cloud "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Custom indicators and plugins offer several features that are not currently supported across all cTrader platforms. For example, plugins can modify the cTrader UI. To ensure that your experience with cTrader is as smooth as possible, you can only run custom indicators and plugins on cTrader Windows.

If I am the author of an algorithm can I charge other people for using it?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/faq/#if-i-am-the-author-of-an-algorithm-can-i-charge-other-people-for-using-it "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

At the moment, cTrader does not offer any built-in means for charging one-time payments or regular fees for algorithm usage. You will need to use third-party services to process payments and ensure that your algos are distributed only to paying customers. Note that this may change with future releases of cTrader.

Need cTrader support?

Reach out through our official channels to resolve any issues:

*   Ask questions and connect with traders, developers and other users in the cTrader [**Discord Community**](https://go.spotware.com/discord)
    .
*   Browse archived discussions on the cTrader [**Community Forum**](https://community.ctrader.com/)
    . While the forum is no longer open for new posts, it is full of helpful answers to common issues.
*   Contact the cTrader Support Team at [**support@ctrader.com**](mailto:support@ctrader.com)
     for technical assistance.

Back to top

# Requirements for cBots - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/requirements-for-cbots/#requirements-for-cbots)

Requirements for cBots[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/requirements-for-cbots/#requirements-for-cbots "Permanent link")

=================================================================================================================================================

There are two main requirements you have to meet to ensure that your cBot instances can run smoothly in Cloud.

Compile-time references[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/requirements-for-cbots/#compile-time-references "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are multiple ways to work with dependencies in .NET. For example, you may supply dependencies as individual `.dll` files without including them directly in your project executable.

However, only compile-time references are fully compatible with running cBots in Cloud. Cloud acts as a dedicated environment that runs the cBot executable (the `.algo` file) without managing any external `.dll` files.

Compile-time dependencies are ensured in all cases where you reference a library in the `using` statements at the top of your code. However, if you reference a stand-alone `.dll` file within the body of your code (for example, via the `Assembly.Load()` method), this reference will not be compile-time, and your cBot will not perform any related operations in Cloud.

Use cross-platform dependencies[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/requirements-for-cbots/#use-cross-platform-dependencies "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Cloud executes all cBots in a Linux-based environment. This means that if you want to use external dependencies, these dependencies have to be cross-platform.

As an illustration, here are some dependencies that will not work in Cloud as they are specific to Windows.

*   WPF
*   WinForms
*   WinAPI

When adding a new dependency into your project, read its documentation to learn whether it is supported in Linux-based environments.

API features[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/requirements-for-cbots/#api-features "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are several Algo API features that work differently in Cloud when compared to local execution.

| Feature/method | How it works in Cloud |
| --- | --- |
| `MessageBox` | Always returns `MessageBoxResult.None` |
| `Window` | Is always ignored |
| `Notifications.PlaySound()` | Is always ignored |
| `Account.UserNickName` | Always calls the `OnException()` handler |
| `Chart.TakeChartshot()` | Always returns null |
| `WebSocketClient` | Only works if you connect to a service via port `25345` |
| `HTTP` | No HTTP requests are sent |
| `LocalStorage` and file operations | Only works if relative paths are specified or new directories are created from scratch |

Local storage and file operations

Whenever a Cloud instance is stopped or deleted, all resources allocated to it within Cloud are automatically freed. If your cBot creates files or directories and then accesses data from them, these files will be deleted on every instance restart or deletion.

Back to top

---

# Manage Cloud instance parameters - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/managing-instance-parameters/#manage-cloud-instance-parameters)

Manage Cloud instance parameters[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/managing-instance-parameters/#manage-cloud-instance-parameters "Permanent link")

===========================================================================================================================================================================

For traders[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/managing-instance-parameters/#for-traders "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The process for managing Cloud instance parameters is exactly the same as the process for managing the parameters of local instances.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/cloudParamsDesktop.png)

A cBot instance always has the following parameters:

*   The trading account whose funds the instance will use for trading
*   The symbol to which the instance is attached
*   The timeframe to which an instance is attached

Depending on the algorithm, instances may also have advanced parameters. These parameters are defined entirely by the algorithm author.

You have several options for managing instance parameters:

*   In the window that opens when creating a new instance
*   In the context menu that opens after clicking the **cog** icon next to the instance timeframe selector
*   In the **Parameters** section that appears below the chart when you click an instance
*   By right-clicking an instance and then choosing **Load parameters** if you have a valid `.cbotset` file

New instanceContext menuParameters sectionLoad parameters

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/addInstanceParamsDesktop.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/contextMenuInstanceParamsDesktop.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/belowChartInstanceParamsDesktop.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/loadInstanceParamsDesktop.png)

Note

*   It is only possible to modify the parameters of an inactive instance. Before changing the parameters of an existing instance, make sure it has stopped.
    
*   Be cautious when modifying advanced parameters as these can significantly change how a cBot behaves. If you are unsure what the optimal settings are, contact the algo author for assistance. The author's contact details can be found in their profile which can be accessed by clicking the author's nickname in the **Author** field.
    

After defining parameters, start the instance again and it will run using the new settings.

For algo authors[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/managing-instance-parameters/#for-algo-authors "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As an algo author, you can customise what parameter values are considered the default for all new instances. When a user attempts to add a new instance of your algorithm, your chosen default settings will be presented to them first. However, users will still be able to override them freely using all of the methods defined above.

Do the following to configure the default parameter values:

1.  Right-click any of your cBots in the algo list to the left
2.  Select **Public settings**
3.  In the newly opened window, navigate to the **Parameters** section
4.  Define the default parameter values either by hand or via a `.cbotset` file

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/defaultParamsDesktop.png)

Back to top

---

# Customise an algorithm - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/customising-an-algo/#customise-an-algorithm)

Customise an algorithm[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/customising-an-algo/#customise-an-algorithm "Permanent link")

==========================================================================================================================================

If you are the author of an algorithm, you can customise how it appears to other users.

To customise a cBot, open its page, tap the **three dots** icon in the bottom-right corner and then tap **Public settings**. For indicators and plugins, **Public settings** is instead a button located at the bottom of the algorithm page.

Set the avatar[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/customising-an-algo/#set-the-avatar "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can upload an image that will effectively serve as the avatar of an algorithm. It will be shown to all algorithm users, so make sure to upload something informative and good-looking.

To upload an image or change an existing one, tap the **pencil** icon next to the existing avatar or the placeholder image. Tap **Upload new image** and then select the file you would like to upload. Afterwards, apply your changes.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/uploadNewImageMobile.jpg)

Set performance statistics[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/customising-an-algo/#set-performance-statistics "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you want, you can make sure that ROI charts will always be displayed on the algorithm page.

Note

The ROI charts are generated based on the performance of your chosen live trading account. They reflect all actions you perform, not just the actions performed by the algorithm.

To enable this option, switch to the **Performance** tab in the **Public settings** screen. In this tab, tap the **Display ROI charts** checkbox and choose one of your live accounts in the selector below.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/displayROIChartsMobile.jpg)

Note

Backtesting statistics can only be set via cTrader Windows. [**Read more here**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/customising-an-algo/)
.

Back to top

---

# Share an algorithm - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/sharing-an-algo/#share-an-algorithm)

Share an algorithm[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/sharing-an-algo/#share-an-algorithm "Permanent link")

==============================================================================================================================

If you are the author of an algorithm, you have three options for sharing it with other users.

*   Automatically
*   As an `.algo` file
*   Via an invite link

Automatically[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/sharing-an-algo/#automatically "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are the owner of the algorithm, you can enable automatic access for your invited traders on a per-algorithm basis.

To do so, perform these actions:

1.  Open the algorithm page, tap the **three dots** icon in the lower-right corner and tap **Public settings**. For plugins and indicators, **Public settings** is instead a button shown directly on the algorithm page.
2.  Switch to the **Invite** tab
3.  Tick the **Enable access for your invited traders** checkbox
4.  Tap **Apply**

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/img/50_screenshots/shareViaInviteLinkMobile.jpg)

After your changes are applied, the algorithm will automatically be shown to all your invited traders in the **Algo** app.

As an `.algo` file[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/sharing-an-algo/#as-an-algo-file "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To use this option, you have to access cTrader Windows.

After generating an `.algo` file via cTrader Windows, you can share it on any platform you want.

Note

It is possible to restrict access to an algorithm only to a specific user. In this case, no other users will be able to access it even if they have the related `.algo`file.

When a user taps the file, they will be prompted to open it using one of the apps installed on their device. If they select cTrader and can access this algorithm, the user will see the algorithm being added to the **Algo** app. [**Read more here**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/sharing-an-algo/)
.

Via an invite link[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/sharing-an-algo/#via-an-invite-link "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can share an algorithm with your invited traders using a link. To enable this functionality, enable automatic access and apply your changes to see the invite link for your chosen algo.

If you have not enabled access for invited traders yet, the algorithm page will also show the **Manage invite link** box from which you will be able to go to the **Invite** tab directly.

After access is enabled, cTrader will generate a link to the algorithm that will function as an Invite link. As is the case with any other cTrader link, you can share it anywhere, and easily get new invited traders. Any of your invited traders who tap the link will be able to access the algorithm.

Note

When you share an algorithm via an Invite link, you do so for free. At the moment, cTrader does not offer a mechanism to charge traders for access to algorithms.

Profile page

If you want, your algos can also appear on your profile page as product cards. Your invited traders will be able to launch new instances and access algos directly from this page. If your profile is public, it can be accessed by traders by tapping your nickname in the **Author** field.

Back to top

---

# FAQ - cTrader Algo

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/faq/#faq)

FAQ[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/faq/#faq "Permanent link")

====================================================================================

What is the best cBot?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/faq/#what-is-the-best-cbot "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There is no cBot that realistically could be considered the best. cBots perform various functions depending on the intentions of their authors. Using a cBot does not guarantee earning profits. When using a cBot, you accept full risk for any actions it may perform.

Can I create a new algorithm in cTrader Mobile?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/faq/#can-i-create-a-new-algorithm-in-ctrader-mobile "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

No, creating a new algorithm is only possible via cTrader Windows.

Can I see the source code of an algorithm?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/faq/#can-i-see-the-source-code-of-an-algorithm "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are not the author of an algorithm, you can only see its source code with explicit permission from the owner and only in cTrader Windows.

Why did one of my algorithms disappear?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/faq/#why-did-one-of-my-algorithms-disappear "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The most probable cause is that the author has chosen to stop providing this product. If the author is your partner in the **Partner** app, you can go to their profile and see their contact details (if there are any). Contact your partner if you want them to restore the algorithm.

You can also access the profile by tapping the nickname of the author in the **Author** field in the algorithm page.

One of my cBot instances has suddenly stopped or crashed. Why?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/faq/#one-of-my-cbot-instances-has-suddenly-stopped-or-crashed-why "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In rare cases, an instance may encounter an error and cease execution. If you go to the **Journal** tab on the instance page, you should see a detailed log of actions performed by the instance. This journal should also contain the exact event when the instance encountered an error.

Instances run in Cloud on live accounts until you stop them, whereas the cloud execution of cBot instances on demo accounts is automatically stopped seven days after their start. To restart your instance, tap **Start cBot instance** in the instance page or the green **Play** icon in the algorithm list.

How many cBot instances can I start in Cloud?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/faq/#how-many-cbot-instances-can-i-start-in-cloud "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can simultaneously run one cBot instance on demo accounts and up to ten cBot instances on live accounts in Cloud. Please note that these limits may vary depending on the broker.

Why can I not run custom indicators and plugins in Cloud?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/faq/#why-can-i-not-run-custom-indicators-and-plugins-in-cloud "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Custom indicators and plugins offer several features that are not currently supported across all cTrader platforms. For example, plugins can modify the cTrader UI. To ensure that your experience with cTrader is as smooth as possible, you can only run custom indicators and plugins on cTrader Windows.

If I am the author of an algorithm, can I charge other people for using it?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/faq/#if-i-am-the-author-of-an-algorithm-can-i-charge-other-people-for-using-it "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

At the moment, cTrader does not offer any built-in means for charging one-time payments or regular fees for algorithm usage. You will need to use third-party services to process payments and ensure that your algos are distributed only to paying customers. Note that this may change with future releases of cTrader.

How can I share the results of backtesting with other users?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/mobile-algo/faq/#how-can-i-share-the-results-of-backtesting-with-other-users "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Backtesting is only available in cTrader Windows. However, when using cTrader Windows, you can choose to upload your backtesting report so that it can be shown on the algorithm page. The report will be shown in all cTrader terminals on all devices.

[**Read more here**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/synchronisation/customising-an-algo/)
.

Need cTrader support?

Reach out through our official channels to resolve any issues:

*   Ask questions and connect with traders, developers and other users in the cTrader [**Discord Community**](https://go.spotware.com/discord)
    .
*   Browse archived discussions on the cTrader [**Community Forum**](https://community.ctrader.com/)
    . While the forum is no longer open for new posts, it is full of helpful answers to common issues.
*   Contact the cTrader Support Team at [**support@ctrader.com**](mailto:support@ctrader.com)
     for technical assistance.
