# Range Bars - cTrader Web

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/range-bars/#range-bars)

Range Bars[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/range-bars/#range-bars "Permanent link")

===================================================================================================

Similarly to [Renko charts](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/renko-charts/)
, range bars are essential for detecting price trends and reducing market noise. In this article, we discuss how range bars are formed, their key differences from standard candlestick charts and Renko charts, and how range bars can be accessed in cTrader Web.

How Range Bars Are Formed[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/range-bars/#how-range-bars-are-formed "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A standard candlestick trading chart forms a new candlestick once a certain amount of time passes regardless of the price movement that occurs during this period. As a result, candlestick charts often include large amounts of ‘noise’ in the form of data that may not be particularly useful for creating and testing an effective trading strategy.

In contrast, range bars do not account for time and, instead, are formed purely based on price movements. Here is an example of a range bar chart that cTrader Web has constructed for GBPJPY using 10 pips as the volume threshold for new range bars.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/range-bars/basic-example.png)

As you can see, range bars are structured similarly to candlesticks in that they include a high price, a low price, an open price, and a close price. However, the way new range bars are formed is different from a conventional trading chart.

Specifically, once a symbol moves a certain amount of pips in either direction in comparison to the closing price of the previous range bar, a new bar is formed on the chart. Note that range bars are formed based on cumulative price movements in both directions.

In the above example, the ask price for GBPJPY may first decrease by 3 pips to 168.758 and then increase by 7 pips to reach 168.765. A new range bar will be formed on the chart as the cumulative price movement will reach 10 pips. Its low price will be 168.758 while its high and closing price will be equal to 168.765.

Contrast this process with how Renko charts are formed. Renko bricks are also formed when price movements exceed a certain threshold; however, this movement has to occur in a single direction. Range bars, on the other hand, reflect cumulative movements in any direction and, as some may argue, convey more information than Renko bricks.

How Range Bars Relate to Timeframes[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/range-bars/#how-range-bars-relate-to-timeframes "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

As stated previously, the process of forming new range bars completely ignores the time component. In fact, in the screenshot above, each unit on the X-axis includes different amounts of time.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/range-bars/timeframes.png)

As you can see, the five units between 12:57:31 and 17:40:05 cover a period of almost five hours. The next five units on the X-axis (from 17:40:05 to 21:48:50), nevertheless, only represent a period that is slightly longer than four hours.

On the one hand, ignoring the time component may seem counterintuitive when trading. In practice, however, range bars are very effective when filtering market noise, particularly in highly volatile market conditions.

How to Access Range Bars in cTrader Web[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/range-bars/#how-to-access-range-bars-in-ctrader-web "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To construct a new range bar chart, first, open the chart selector menu by clicking on the ‘three dots’ icon to the right of the timeframe selector.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/range-bars/chart-selector.png)

In the ‘Range’ column, select the volume threshold that should be used for creating new range bars. The larger the volume threshold, the slower new range bars will appear on the trading chart for the currently selected symbol.

Hover over a particular volume threshold option and press the ‘tick’ icon to its right to add it to your favorites. Afterward, it will always appear in the timeframe selector.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/range-bars/favorite.png)

After choosing a volume threshold, cTrader Web will generate a range bar chart for the currently active symbol.

You will then be able to interact with this chart using all toolbox controls offered by cTrader Web. In the screenshot below, we have placed two new horizontal lines on the 10-pip range bar chart for USDJPY.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/range-bars/horizontal-lines.png)

How Range Bars Benefit Traders[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/range-bars/#how-range-bars-benefit-traders "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

While different from traditional candlestick charts, range bar charts offer several advantages to all types of traders.

*   Range bars are effective at demonstrating price trends and the general level of market volatility completely independent of the time component.
*   Range bars provide more information than Renko charts by showcasing low and high prices in addition to open and close prices.
*   Range bars simplify drawing on charts by providing easy-to-interpret points of reference for price reversals, and support and resistance levels.

Having said that, range bar charts may not be all that useful when attempting to plot time-dependent price trends.

To summarize, range bar charts should be of use to anyone wanting to receive accurate and noise-free information about any currently ongoing price trends and market movements. You can use them in combination with chart drawings to create a powerful visual aid for improving the effectiveness of your manual trading strategies.

---

# cTrader FIX API

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/fix/#introduction)

Getting Started
===============

Introduction[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/fix/#introduction "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

This section provides general information on FIX API, it's characteristics, applications, possibilities and limitations.

FIX stands for Financial Information Exchange. It is an industry driven machine-to-machine communication protocol that has been specifically created to handle considerable amounts of financial information and transmit that information as fast as possible.

The FIX Protocol Specification provides the format for electronic messages and the communication model of those messages. It has been created and managed by FIX Trading Community previously known as FIX Protocol Limited (FPL).

The FIX Trading Community is a natural and non-profit organization which regulates the standard of FIX API through the collaboration of major industry players and committee members.

cTrader supports FIX version 4.4. For further information about this version please refer to the specifications published by the FIX Protocol Organization under:

[https://www.fixtrading.org/standards/fix-4-4/](https://www.fixtrading.org/standards/fix-4-4/)

Background of FIX API in FX Trading[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/fix/#background-of-fix-api-in-fx-trading "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FIX Protocol was created in the early 1990's for setting up electronic communication between exchanges, brokers, banks and institutional participants such as hedge funds.

It has successfully established itself against other API's used for transmitting financial markets data such as JAVA and C++ because of the improved speed and the effectiveness of data transferring.

In 1998 there was a non-profit company established for expanding, developing and supporting the protocol, it was called FIX Protocol Limited (FPL) and remains active to this day under a different brand.

Due to the simplicity and effectiveness, FIX Protocol has quickly become the standard communication method in the financial industry across all asset classes. It is now widely used for performing any trading or financial operation all over the world.

For a very long time, in the FX Trading industry only major participants, such as banks, brokers, liquidity providers and institutional traders had access to using FIX API.

Theses participants were able to create sophisticated solutions in order to improve trading effectiveness and a competitive edge over smaller investors such as retail traders.

The situation has changed completely in 2016, when Spotware Systems became the first retail focused technology company to provide a FIX API to anyone without prejudice. A FIX API can be obtained via cTrader with no conditions applied such as minimum trading volume or minimum deposit size.

Typical Application Methods of FIX API in the Industry[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/fix/#typical-application-methods-of-fix-api-in-the-industry "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FIX API is an industry standard messaging protocol which has multiple applications in the market.

### Brokerage[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/fix/#brokerage "Permanent link")

Brokers use FIX API to receive prices from the market and execute their clients orders.

### Providing Prices[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/fix/#providing-prices "Permanent link")

Liquidity providers and price makers such as banks or exchanges use FIX API to provide prices to brokers or hedge funds.

### Trading Hubs[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/fix/#trading-hubs "Permanent link")

Institutional traders may use FIX API for developing proprietary systems that allow them to open trades on any account they have with any broker from a single system.

### Advanced Trading Robots[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/fix/#advanced-trading-robots "Permanent link")

With FIX API trading robots including black box algos can receive information from multiple sources simultaneously and make trading decisions based on a much wider scope of data and number of sources.

### Trade Copiers[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/fix/#trade-copiers "Permanent link")

Systems that will automatically replicate trades on multiple trading accounts across multiple brokers or on the accounts of traders are connected to the copier. However we believe other Spotware API's are more suitable for this.

### Custom Trading Interfaces[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/fix/#custom-trading-interfaces "Permanent link")

Some traders prefer to use a minimalistic interface for trading, either because other interface parts distract them or because they feel they consume system resources. With FIX API, there is a possibility to create a trading interface which will fit the exact needs of such traders.

However we believe other Spotware API's are more suitable for this.

---

# FAQ - cTrader Invite

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/faq/#faq)

FAQ[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/faq/#faq "Permanent link")

==========================================================================

Common questions [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/faq/#common-questions "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### What is cTrader Invite?

cTrader Invite is an all-in-one toolkit designed to help partners attract traders with appealing cTrader products and maximise referral conversions. Invite allows partners to generate and manage invite links, track real-time statistics of invited traders, share contact information and promote successful products.

### Is cTrader Invite available in my app?

Invite is only available in broker-branded cTrader applications. Inquire with your broker if you are unable to access Invite in your cTrader app.

### How is a partner different from a trader?

A partner is an individual or entity that collaborates with a broker to attract new clients and engage existing ones in trading, performing different [**roles**](https://help.ctrader.com/partner-tools/)
. A trader performs trading activities on the platform but can become a partner after registering with the broker as such.

For invited traders [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/faq/#for-invited-traders "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Who is my partner?

You can see your current partner's profile in the [**Partner**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/partner/)
 tab of cTrader Invite. If you have this tab, the entire **Invite** application will appear as **Partner** in the cTrader UI.

### Why do I see several partners in the Partner tab?

The [**Partner**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/partner/)
 tab displays your current partner as well as other partners you have been attributed to. Only one partner can be 'current' at the moment.

### Why is my partner's profile invisible?

Your partner's profile may be non-public and, therefore, invisible to invited traders. If all of your partners are not public or you do not have a partner, the [**Partner**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/partner/)
 tab will not show up in your app.

### Can I contact my partner?

Yes, partners are usually open to support traders invited by them. Your partner's preferred communication channels are listed in the [**Partner**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/partner/)
 tab. Unfortunately, you cannot contact a partner with a non-public profile or hidden contact details.

### Can I access my partner's products?

In the [**Partner**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/partner/)
 tab of cTrader Invite, you will be able to review your partner's own and favourite products (algorithms, copy strategies, etc.), provided that the partner has enabled their display.

### How can I stay with my current partner?

You will remain with your current partner throughout the guaranteed attribution period. Use your partner's products (algorithms, copy strategies, etc.), and automatic reattribution will not occur within cTrader even if you click other partners' invite links. Also, your partner's profile should remain public (i.e., stay visible in the [**Partner**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/partner/)
 tab) to prevent automatic reattribution.

### How can I change my current partner?

After the mandatory attribution period elapses, you can change your current partner manually by tapping **Make current** on another partner's card in the [**Partner**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/partner/)
 tab. Alternatively, you can tap another partner's invite link for automatic reattribution. In the latter case, reattribution will occur only if you are not using the current partner's products and the partner's profile is not public.

### Can I identify a partner by their invite link?

Yes, [**invite links**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/)
 are readable for ordinary users because they carry the partner's username at the end as a parameter (`u={username}`). If you delete this parameter manually, the link will no longer function as an invite link, but it will still work within the cTrader ecosystem.

### Am I redirected from cTrader to the broker's client area for registration and attribution?

After you tap an invite link, cTrader will open, and you may be required to sign in/up. What happens next depends on your broker’s [**attribution flow**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/settings/)
. You may stay within cTrader or be redirected to the broker’s website to complete registration and attribution.

### Can I become a partner myself?

It’s easier than you think! Contact your broker and register in their partner programme. You will be able to leverage cTrader Invite to maximise referral conversions and keep your traders engaged.

For partners [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/faq/#for-partners "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### How should I set up cTrader Invite to start attributing traders?

You will be prompted in the [**Settings**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/settings/)
 tab of cTrader Invite which option complies with your broker's attribution flow:

*   Simply start sharing invite links, and you will be registered in the broker's partner programme automatically. Your partner ID will be automatically set in cTrader as soon as you invite your first trader.
*   Alternatively, set your BRL issued by the broker after your registration in the partner programme. Below, you should choose the in-app actions that will redirect your invited traders to the broker's website for registration and attribution.

### What should traders do to get attributed?

After tapping an invite link, traders open cTrader, and what happens next depends on the broker's [**attribution flow**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/settings/)
:

*   Traders are automatically registered and attributed to the partner without leaving the app if cTrader Invite is integrated with the broker's CRM.
*   Alternatively, traders are redirected to the broker's website via the BRL for registration and attribution once they complete one of the actions set by the partner.

### How can I create an invite link?

Use the functionality of the [**Invite link**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/)
 tab to access invite links. Here, you can find your basic invite link and product-specific invite links. Any cTrader link you share while logged in to the application will contain your username as a `(u)` parameter, meaning it will function as an invite link.

### How can I share an invite link?

You can send invite links directly to your preferred communication channels via the native sharing functionality of mobile devices or copy them to the clipboard for further sharing. Additionally, you can display your invite link as a QR code right from the [**Invite link**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/)
 tab when invited traders are physically near you. QR codes are customisable and can be generated for both basic and product-specific invite links.

### What products appear as invite links in the Invite Link tab?

Some of partners' existing products are automatically listed as invite link options in the [**Invite link**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/)
 tab (algorithms, copy strategies, etc.). Invite links leading to other features and products from the cTrader ecosystem (signal links, price alerts, deeplinks, etc.) can be generated in other areas of cTrader.

### How will I know that I have invited someone?

You will receive notifications from your cTrader app. You can also monitor your invitation performance in the [**Invited traders**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/invited-traders/)
 tab where all attributed users' usernames are displayed together with their joining date. Additionally, your broker may update you about every invited trader.

### How will I know which link my invited trader has followed?

The [**Invited traders**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/invited-traders/)
 tab demonstrates performance by an invite link. Analysing these statistics, you can refine your invitation strategy.

### How can I ensure that my invited traders remain attributed?

Your invited traders will remain attributed to you throughout the guaranteed attribution period and as long as they continue using your products, provided that your partner profile stays public. However, traders have the option to manually change their partner in the [**Partner**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/partner/)
 tab once the guaranteed attribution period has ended.

### How can I distribute my products via cTrader Invite?

The [**Partner**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/partner/)
 tab displays the partner's own and favourite products to invited traders and serves as a ready-to-go landing page that partners can share via the URL: `https://ctrader.com/u/{username}/`, where `{username}` is the partner's username. The [**Settings**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/settings/)
 tab of cTrader Invite allows you to configure the visibility of your profile and products.

### How can I promote my products via cTrader Invite?

cTrader Invite features always-on [**promotional mechanisms**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/settings/)
 to motivate invited traders to create a cTrader ID, open a demo trading account, open a live trading account, deposit funds and trade.

### How can I hide my profile from invited traders?

You can hide your profile from all users by disabling **Make my profile public** in [**Settings**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/settings/)
. Alternatively, your profile can remain public but you can choose not to show it in the [**Partner**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/partner/)
 section for your invited traders. For public profiles, you can customise the amount of contact information disclosed.

### Does cTrader Invite remunerate partners for invited traders?

No, please contact your broker regarding the commissions and other terms of the referral programme.

Need cTrader support?

Reach out through our official channels to resolve any issues:

*   Ask questions and connect with traders, developers and other users in the cTrader [**Discord Community**](https://go.spotware.com/discord)
    .
*   Browse archived discussions on the cTrader [**Community Forum**](https://community.ctrader.com/)
    . While the forum is no longer open for new posts, it is full of helpful answers to common issues.
*   Contact the cTrader Support Team at [**support@ctrader.com**](mailto:support@ctrader.com)
     for technical assistance.

Back to top

---

# Risk-Reward - cTrader Web

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/risk-reward/#risk-reward)

Risk-reward[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/risk-reward/#risk-reward "Permanent link")

======================================================================================================

The risk-reward tool allows you to quickly calculate the trade volume directly on the chart, based on the amount you are willing to risk or your desired profit target per trade.

Risk-reward typically compares the potential profit (reward) of a trade to its potential loss (risk). The reward is visualised as the distance between the entry price and the take-profit level, while the risk is visualised as the distance between the entry price and the stop-loss level.

Risk-reward in one minute!

*   Traders use risk-reward to quickly calculate the quantity or volume of their trade, and the potential profit-to-loss ratio is visualised on the chart.
*   After adding risk-reward to a chart, users can choose a calculation mode to fix values for risk, reward or order/position size.
*   For convenience, traders can specify the amount they are willing to risk or gain as volume in units, a percentage of their balance or a percentage of their equity.
*   Traders can enter order parameters, configure protection settings and place an order with a single click.

Add risk-reward to chart[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/risk-reward/#add-risk-reward-to-chart "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Click the **Risk-reward** icon in the chart instruments toolbar, then choose a buy or sell option with a fixed risk, reward or size.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/risk-reward/select-risk-reward.png)

Using the crosshair, click on the trading chart to specify your desired entry price. The risk-reward object will be placed at that level.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/risk-reward/risk-reward-object-on-chart.png)

Note

When you add or move the risk-reward object to a past period along the price axis, a possible past deal (with profit or loss) is highlighted with a deeper colour.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/risk-reward/past-possible-deal-highlighted.png)

Adjust risk-reward parameters[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/risk-reward/#adjust-risk-reward-parameters "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Right-click the risk-reward object or click the **Settings** icon in the risk-reward toolbar.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/risk-reward/settings-tab-risk-reward.png)

Here, you can configure the following parameters:

| Parameter | Value | Definition |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Order type | Market, limit, stop, stop-limit | Choose an [**order type**](https://help.ctrader.com/trading-with-ctrader/orders/)<br>. |
| Trade side | Buy, sell | Choose a trade direction. |
| Calculation mode | Risk, reward, size | For risk and reward, specify the volume in units, a percentage of equity or a percentage of balance. For size, set the volume in units or the quantity in lots. |
| Risk-reward ratio | Any value (most traders prefer from 2 to 3) | Possible to lock the risk-reward ratio at a fixed value. |
| Entry price | Any value | Set the entry price (available for non-market orders only). |
| Quantity/volume | Lots/units | Specify your trade quantity or volume. |
| Stop loss | Pips, price | Set your desired [**stop-loss protection**](https://help.ctrader.com/trading-with-ctrader/orders/?h=stop+loss#stop-loss-and-take-profit)<br> as a distance from the entry price or at a specific price level. |
| Take profit | Pips, price | Set your desired [**take-profit protection**](https://help.ctrader.com/trading-with-ctrader/orders/?h=stop+loss#stop-loss-and-take-profit)<br> as a distance from the entry price or at a specific price level. |

Confirm your settings by clicking **OK**. Otherwise, reset to default or delete the risk-reward object.

Alternatively, you can adjust some of these parameters directly on the chart. When the risk-reward object is active, move the entire object or drag its control dots along the price and time axes.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/risk-reward/control-dot-risk-reward.png)

Use manual adjustments to do the following:

*   Achieve a specific risk-reward ratio.
*   Modify the entry price for non-market orders.
*   Adjust the risk and reward areas, affecting stop-loss and take-profit parameters.

Note

Adjusting the risk-reward dimensions does not affect the pinned value of the selected calculation mode.

When the risk-reward ratio is fixed, dragging either the risk or reward area automatically adjusts the other area proportionally. The pinned value of the selected calculation mode remains unaffected.

Tip

Click the **Share** icon to copy a signal link containing the configured order parameters and protections to your clipboard. You can then share this link via social media, messaging apps or any other preferred communication channel.

Place orders with risk-reward[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/risk-reward/#place-orders-with-risk-reward "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Click **Buy** or **Sell** on the risk-reward object to place an order with the configured parameters.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/risk-reward/buy-button-risk-reward.png)

Warning

A market order is executed at the current price, regardless of the position of the risk-reward object on the chart.

Manage risk-reward objects[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/risk-reward/#manage-risk-reward-objects "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Options for managing risk-reward are displayed in the risk-reward toolbar.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/risk-reward/options-for-managing-risk-reward.png)

### Customise style[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/risk-reward/#customise-style "Permanent link")

Navigate to the **Style** tab of the risk-reward settings. Here, you can customise the risk-reward lines and areas separately:

*   Select a line type.
*   Set the line thickness.
*   Choose a colour and adjust the opacity.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/risk-reward/style-tab-risk-reward.png)

### Set visibility[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/risk-reward/#set-visibility "Permanent link")

In the **Visibility** tab, you can specify the chart periods for which the risk-reward object is shown on the chart. By default, it is set to display on all periods.

Example

If you set the range to 3–25 minutes and 2–4 hours, the risk-reward object will be shown on the chart only when the selected period falls within the range (e.g., m3, m5, h2, h3). The object will be hidden when periods outside the range (e.g., m1, m30, h1, h6, d1) are selected.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/risk-reward/visibility-tab-risk-reward.png)

### Duplicate[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/risk-reward/#duplicate "Permanent link")

Click the **Duplicate** icon, and an identical risk-reward object appears on the chart.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/risk-reward/duplicated-risk-reward.png)

### Lock[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/risk-reward/#lock "Permanent link")

Click the **Lock** icon to lock the selected risk-reward object in its current position on the chart.

### Delete[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/risk-reward/#delete "Permanent link")

Click the **Delete** icon to remove the selected risk-reward object from the chart.

Note

You can also manage risk-reward objects on the chart by opening **Object Manager** and then selecting **Drawings**.

In addition to the actions covered above, **Object Manager** allows you to do the following:

### Apply to all symbols[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/risk-reward/#apply-to-all-symbols "Permanent link")

Click the **Apply to all symbols** icon, and the risk-reward object will become visible on any symbol in the chart container.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/img/charts/risk-reward/chart-object-manager-risk-reward.png)

### Hide object[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-web/charts/risk-reward/#hide-object "Permanent link")

Click the **Hide** icon to hide the risk-reward object on the chart.

---

# Invite link - cTrader Invite

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/#invite-link)

Invite link[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/#invite-link "Permanent link")

======================================================================================

What is cTrader Invite?

cTrader Invite is an all-in-one toolkit designed to help partners attract traders with appealing cTrader products and effectively convert them into referrals. Any cTrader link shared by partners attributes traders who click it, and they later become referrals in the broker's client area. The Invite app allows partners to generate invite links and QR codes, track invited traders' stats in real time, promote engaging products and set up a personal profile page that outperforms a traditional landing page.

Check our [**brochure**](https://www.spotware.com/pdf/back-office/cTrader_Invite_Brochure.pdf)
 with quick instructions for partners on how to set up cTrader Invite.

cTrader Invite streamlines the process of user attribution in broker-branded applications. The **Invite link** tab is designed to generate and share invite links in just a few taps. It allows partners to manage different invite links leading to specific products from the cTrader ecosystem.

Invite link in one minute!

*   Any cTrader URL that contains a special `(u)` parameter carrying a partner's username is an invite link.
*   Partners can physically demonstrate a QR code, copy an invite link to the clipboard or share it directly across preferred communication channels.
*   In addition to the basic invite link, the **Invite link** tab allows partners to generate and share product-specific invite links for algorithms, copy strategies and more.
*   Traders who click an invite link are attributed to the partner within cTrader and later recognised as referrals in the broker's system if both are synchronised.

Types of invite links [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/#types-of-invite-links "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All invite links, regardless of their type, contain a special `(u)` parameter carrying a partner's username. The presence of this parameter ensures that invited traders are accurately linked to their partner within cTrader.

`https://app.ctrader.com/?u={username}` 

When a trader clicks an invite link and creates a cTID (or uses an existing one), they become your invited trader unless previously invited by another partner. They may need to take an action on the platform (e.g., create a new account) to be redirected to the broker's website for registration and attribution. Depending on the [**attribution flow**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/settings/)
, this redirect may not be required.

MobileWeb and desktop

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/img/invite-link/06.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/img/invite-link/01.png)

If a cTrader URL includes a `(u)` parameter, it can attribute invited traders to the partner within the application. Examples of product-specific links include algorithms, copy strategies, signal links and more.

*   cBot
    
    `https://app.ctrader.com/algo/22310?u={username}` 
    
*   Plugin
    
    `https://app.ctrader.com/algo/141591?u={username}`
    
*   Copy strategy  
    
    `https://app.ctrader.com/copy/strategy/66522?u={username}` 
    
*   Signal link
    
    `https://app.ctrader.com/signal?s=EURUSD&o=stop&d=buy&p=1.20124&sl=1.19123&tp=1.21125&c=Take%20action!&ts=trade&e-utc=31-12-2030_12-12?u={username}`
    

Similarly, all cTrader [**deeplinks**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-deeplinks/)
 with a `(u)` parameter attribute users who follow them.

*   Make a deposit
    
    `https://app.ctrader.com/funds/deposit?u={username}`  
    
*   Open a live account
    
    `https://app.ctrader.com/accounts/create-live?u={username}` 
    
*   Open transactions in Trade Watch
    
    `https://app.ctrader.com/transactions?u={username}` 
    

Tip

You can identify the partner just by looking at an invite link, thanks to the readable `(u)` parameter at the end. This enhances the transparency and clarity of the attribution process for all invited traders.

How to generate invite links [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/#how-to-generate-invite-links "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The **Invite link** tab of cTrader Invite enables partners to manage multiple links, thus making the attribution process more organised and tailored to specific trader needs. It is also possible to generate a customisable QR code by choosing between a basic invite link and links leading to specific partner products.

MobileWeb and desktop

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/img/invite-link/07.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/img/invite-link/02.png)

Some of the partners' products automatically appear as invite link options in the **Invite link** tab.

Tip

Beyond the options in the **Invite link** tab, you can simply share any product (e.g., a copy strategy) via the cTrader native functionality, and traders will receive your invite link. You can create a deeplink with a `(u)` parameter manually following our [**instructions**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-deeplinks/cross-platform-deeplinks/)
.

How to share invite links [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/#how-to-share-invite-links "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Invite links can be shared as a QR code, copied to the clipboard or sent directly to preferred communication channels via the native sharing functionality of mobile devices.

MobileWeb and desktop

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/img/invite-link/08.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/img/invite-link/03.png)

If you contact traders in person, you can demonstrate an invite QR code right on the screen. As an idea, you can add an invite QR code on your business cards and distribute them physically at trading expos and events.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/img/invite-link/04.png)

If your traders are remote from you, sharing and invite link on social media would be more convenient.

![Image title](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/img/invite-link/05.png)

Back to top

---

# Introduction - cTrader ID

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/#how-ctid-works-with-trading-accounts)

Introduction
============

A cTrader ID (cTID) is a set of credentials (email/username and password) used to sign in to all client applications across cTrader brokers and other products within the cTrader ecosystem.

Example

A username is automatically generated by cTrader based on the email you provide for cTID creation.  
Email: `letstrade@email.com`  
Username: `letstrade`

Note

You can use either the email or the username, along with your password, for cTrader authorisation.

How cTID works with trading accounts[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/#how-ctid-works-with-trading-accounts "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Traders often use several live and demo trading accounts with a cTrader broker. A cTID serves as a single login allowing users to access all their trading accounts with just a click within a cTrader application. Traders will not have to log out and re-enter different account numbers and passwords each time they want to access their trading accounts.

The image below illustrates how a cTrader ID and trading accounts are linked.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/img/introduction-0.png)

Multiple trading accounts can be linked to a single cTrader ID, enabling easy access and seamless switching between them. In addition to instant access to all your trading accounts with a specific broker, you also gain access to workspaces, watchlists, email settings, price alerts, copy-trading accounts and more.

Note

A trading account is [**specific to the broker**](https://www.spotware.com/featured-ctrader-brokers-and-prop)
 with which it was created and can be used in that broker's version of cTrader. A [**cross-broker cTrader app**](https://ctrader.com/download/)
 allows traders to access all trading accounts they have with all cTrader brokers within the same app.

cTID credentials are securely stored on dedicated servers with the highest level of encryption, ensuring a safe trading environment for users.

Difference between cTID and a trading account[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/#difference-between-ctid-and-a-trading-account "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A trading account is identified by a seven-digit number and belongs to the broker's system. Trading accounts hold trading details, such as account balance, deposit currency, leverage, open positions, pending orders and trade history.

The table below highlights the key differences between a cTrader ID and a trading account.

| cTrader ID | Trading account |
| --- | --- |
| Used across brokers, cTrader apps and products. | Belongs to the broker and can only be used in this broker's or the cross-broker cTrader apps. |
| An email and username you can change at any time. | A seven-digit unique number you cannot change. |
| Does not store any trading or financial information. | Contains trading and financial information with the broker. |

Back to top

---

# Create a new cTID - cTrader ID

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/create-a-new-ctrader-id/#sign-up)

Create a new cTID
=================

With the first launch of any cTrader application, the **Log in/Sign up** window pops up automatically.

You can log in with your existing credentials or sign up to create a new cTID.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/img/create-new-ctid-sign-up.png)

Sign up[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/create-a-new-ctrader-id/#sign-up "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To create a new cTID, enter your email and a password (twice) in the **Sign up** form and then click **Create cTrader ID**.

Note

The email and password you input become your cTID credentials. A cTrader username is generated automatically based on your email. You can further modify any of these [**cTID credentials**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/credentials/)
.

If the account creation is successful, you are automatically logged in to cTrader with your new cTID, and a confirmation email is sent to your email address. You can start using cTrader, but we strongly recommend that you confirm your email to use the full functionality of your cTID.

Confirm cTrader ID[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/create-a-new-ctrader-id/#confirm-ctrader-id "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Proceed to your email inbox and open the confirmation message. Click the **Confirm cTrader ID** button to complete the verification.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/img/confirm-email-email.png)

Tip

If there is no confirmation email in the inbox, check your spam folder.

Warning

If you have not received a confirmation email, go to the [**cTID website**](https://id.ctrader.com/)
 and click **Resend confirmation link**. A new confirmation request will be sent to your email.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/img/resend-confirmation-ctraderid.png)

After you confirm your cTrader ID, you will be redirected to your broker's cTrader Web.

Visit the cTID website, where you can [**manage your cTID**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/)
 credentials, notifications, active sessions and accounts.

Back to top

---

# Introduction - cTrader Mac

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mac/#introduction)

Introduction[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mac/#introduction "Permanent link")

=====================================================================================

cTrader Mac is a native trading application for macOS with built-in algorithmic trading and a social trading platform.

While the platform maintains a consistent user interface across all devices, cTrader Mac offers a fully seamless experience for Mac users with familiar windows, controls, and hotkeys. On top of that, cTrader for Mac leverages hardware resources, ensuring superior app performance.

This guide is focused on essential differences of using cTrader Mac compared to other cTrader applications. Every time the user flow or interface in cTrader Mac resembles other platforms (Desktop or Web), readers will be forwarded to relared guides in the Help Centre to avoid repetition.

System Requirements[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-mac/#system-requirements "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| System | Minimum Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Processor | Intel Core i5 |
| Memory | 8 GB |
| Operating System | macOS 10.15 Catalina |

---

# Introduction - cTrader deeplinks

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-deeplinks/#ctrader-deeplinks)

cTrader deeplinks[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-deeplinks/#ctrader-deeplinks "Permanent link")

=====================================================================================================

Introduction[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-deeplinks/#introduction "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This documentation describes cTrader deeplinks, their use cases, and their benefits.

Deeplinks are special URLs that, upon being opened by a user, transfer said user to a certain screen in cTrader Web or Mobile. Using query parameters, it is possible to perform certain actions (switching the language of the cTrader application opened by a user) before the chosen screen is shown to traders.

The primary function of deeplinks is facilitating the creation of custom calls to action (CTAs) by allowing traders to be directed to certain tabs/sections inside cTrader.

Deeplinks only work for the following applications:

*   cTrader Web
*   cTrader Mobile (iOS and Android)

Documentation structure[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-deeplinks/#documentation-structure "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The documentation, excluding the introduction, is divided into these main sections:

*   [**How deeplinks work**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-deeplinks/how-deeplinks-work/)
    . This section explains the core functionality of deeplinks and how deeplinks work in broker OAuth flows.
*   [**Use cases**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-deeplinks/use-cases/)
    . This guide lists the main use cases of deeplinks.
*   [**Cross-platform deeplinks**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-deeplinks/cross-platform-deeplinks/)
    . This page provides an extensive list of deeplinks that work on both cTrader Web and Mobile.
*   [**Mobile-only deeplinks**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-deeplinks/mobile-only-deeplinks/)
    . This section focuses on the deeplinks that are unique to cTrader Mobile.

Back to top

---

# Market Watch - cTrader Windows

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/interface/market-watch/#symbols)

Market Watch
============

Marketwatch is the panel to the left which contains the trading symbols and allows entering the trades in the QuickTrade mode and compound the symbols into the watchlists.

Symbols[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/interface/market-watch/#symbols "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A symbol is a financial instrument, any tradable asset purchased by an investor.

cTrader is a multi-asset-class CFD trading app that supports thousands of currency pairs, stocks, indices, commodities, Forex, ETFs, and cryptocurrencies.

All symbols are organized in the Watchlists on the MarketWatch panel to the left.

By default, there are 4 Watchlists available in the MarketWatch - Popular Markets, Metals, Crypto, and Assets.

You can add as many Watchlists to the MarketWatch as you need by clicking + Create.

New Watchlist button (![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-0.png)) to the bottom of the MarketWatch. Click on any Watchlist to expand or collapse it.

Click on a symbol in the Watchlist to expand or collapse the Symbol tile with the detailed information on it.

Note

You can expand as many Symbol tiles at a time as you need.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-1.png)

Use the Finder to the top of the Watchlist to find the desired symbol. Click in the search box to view the whole list of the asset classes and select the desired symbol.

Alternatively, use the shortcut Ctrl+f to proceed to the finder.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-2.png)

or type in the symbol tile or its part to find the desired symbol.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-3.png)

When you select a symbol from the Watchlist, it becomes a current symbol in the Active Symbol Panel to the right.

Note

If you have open positions, then you will see the positions count badge that displays the number of opened positions per symbol.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-4.png)

The Symbol tile displays the symbol information in real-time. The following information is available on the Symbol panel in the Watchlist:

*   The symbol name to the upper left (e.g. USDJPY). The number of your open Positions for this symbol is displayed next to it.
*   Daily Low - the lowest price that occurred for the symbol during the last 24 hours.
*   Daily High - the highest price that occurred for the symbol during the last 24 hours.
*   Daily Change - the difference between the current price of the symbol and the price a day before.
*   The current Ask and Bid prices for the symbol.
*   Market Sentiment - the overall attitude of investors toward the symbol. Hover over the Market Sentiment to see the percentage of client accounts that expect the price to fall or to rise.
*   Spread - the difference between the Bid and Ask prices.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-6.png)

To add a symbol to the Watchlists, click the Star button (![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-15.png)), and select the existing watchlist or create a new one where you would like to add it.

Click the New Order button (![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-16.png)) to create a new order. Click the Chart button (![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-17.png)) - to open the symbol chart.

### Manage Symbols[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/interface/market-watch/#manage-symbols "Permanent link")

Right-click on a symbol (no matter if it is expanded or not) to expand the additional symbol options drop-down.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-7.png)

*   Create New Order - click to open the New Order menu. Allows creating any kind of trading order.
*   New chart - click to open a new chart for the current symbol.
*   Remove from this Watchlist - click to remove the current symbol from the current watchlist.
*   Add to Watchlist - add the current symbol to the desired Watchlist.
*   Single Click - enable or disable expanding the symbol by a single click.

QuickTrade[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/interface/market-watch/#quicktrade "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The WatchLists allow entering the trades by creating the Limit Orders and Market Orders using the QuickTrade option.

To start trading with the QuickTrade, click on the desired symbol to expand it, then select the volume from the drop-down (alternatively, use the toggles or type in the value ![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-8.png), and click the Sell or Buy button to create a Market Order.

Use the Sell Limit or Buy Limit drop-downs to create a Limit order, and select the price that you want your order to be filled at.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-9.png)

Note

In the QuickTrade mode, the Market or Limit order will be sent without any further confirmation, if the QuickTrade mode is OFF - the Create Order menu will pop.

Only the most popular symbols are available in the Watchlists.

Use the Symbol bar of the Active Symbol Panel or the Marketwatch finder to find all the available symbols.

All the symbols are grouped by asset classes: Popular Markets, Metals, Energies, Forex, Indices, EU and US Shares, Powershares ETFs, iShares ETFs, Crypto Currencies, Asia/Pacific Shares, etc.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-10.png)

Watchlists[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/interface/market-watch/#watchlists "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cTrader allows trading thousands of symbols depending on your broker.

A Watchlist is a customizable group of symbols where you can add or delete the symbols of your choice.

In the Watchlists tab on the MarketWatch panel, there is one watchlist available by default - the Popular Markets, which contains the most popular symbols. This Watchlist is not editable.

You can create as many Watchlists as you need. To create a new Watchlist click +Create New Watchlist, specify its name and click OK.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-11.png)

Use the "+" button on your watchlist to add as many new symbols to it as you need. Use the search box above if you don't see the desired symbol in the list.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-12.png)

### Manage Watchlists[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/interface/market-watch/#manage-watchlists "Permanent link")

Right-click on a Watchlist to expand its additional options.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-13.png)

*   Detach - click to detach the current watchlist.
*   Rename - click Rename to rename the current watchlist. You can rename any of your Watchlists except the default Popular Markets watchlist.
*   Duplicate Watchlist - click to completely duplicate the current Watchlist. The Popular Market watchlist can also be duplicated. The duplicated watchlist will appear in the bottom of the list.
*   Delete Watchlist - click to completely remove the current Watchlist.
*   Expand all - click to expand all symbols.
*   Collapse all - click to collapse all symbols.
*   Add Symbol or Remove Symbol - click to mark the desired symbols to be added or removed from the list.
*   Watchlist Settings - click to enable or disable Daily Change for the symbols, and enable or disable expanding the symbol on a Single click.
*   Asset Units - click to open the Asset Units settings.

To reorder a watchlist, drag and drop the symbols in the list. Drag the watchlists to reorder them, or drag the symbols from one watchlist to another.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader/img/market-watch-14.png)

---

# Buy a product - cTrader Store

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/how-tos/buy-a-product/#buy-a-product)

Buy a product[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/how-tos/buy-a-product/#buy-a-product "Permanent link")

===============================================================================================================

Find an algorithm you want to buy by browsing product categories or using the search field.

Click the **Buy now** button displaying the price and proceed to the payment page.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/img/how-tos/buy-supermacd-bot.png)

Note

Free products are available in cTrader Store and can be downloaded instantly by clicking the **Free** button.

Before your first purchase in cTrader Store, your country is automatically detected based on your IP address. Alternatively, you can manually select and confirm your location.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/img/getting-started/select-location.png)

Warning

Your payment card must be issued in the specified country; otherwise, the payment will not be processed.

You can pay for a product with a debit or credit card. Supported cards include Visa, MasterCard and other popular payment systems.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/img/how-tos/secure-checkout-payment.png)

Note

cTrader does not collect and store your payment method details. All payments are securely processed by our trusted provider.

Back to top

# Getting Started - Open API

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/open-api/#getting-started)

Getting Started[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/open-api/#getting-started "Permanent link")

========================================================================================

The cTrader Open API is a service you can use to develop custom applications connected to the cTrader backend. This documentation provides everything you need to know including information about SDKs, structured tutorials, code snippets, and more.

What Is Open API?[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/open-api/#what-is-open-api "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The cTrader Open API is a service that allows anyone with a cTID to create an application sending and receiving information to and from the cTrader backend. You can use this API to develop trading-oriented apps or services or integrate the cTrader backend with any existing solutions you may have.

Using this API involves sending and receiving messages to and from the cTrader backend. This is done via sending/receiving either JSON objects or [**Google Protocol Buffers (Protobufs)**](https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers)
. Both of these means of data serialisation/deserialisation are language-neutral, meaning that you can use any programming language you want to interact with the API.

Message Names

Note that when this documentation references specific messages (e.g., [**`ProtoOAApplicationAuthReq`**](https://help.ctrader.com/open-api/messages/#protooaapplicationauthreq)
), it uses the Protobuf notation with `ProtoOA...` at the start of a message name.

The cTrader Open API is available for anyone registered with a cTrader-affiliated broker.

Use Cases

Here are just some of the possible applications you may create when interacting with the cTrader Open API.

*   A custom trading application that funnels new users to create new accounts with a certain broker.
*   A Telegram bot that automatically informs your followers of any new traders you may have placed.
*   An app for wearables that displays the current P&L of the five most recent positions opened by the user.
*   A mobile app that gives a market overview by using a generative AI service.

As you can see, the cTrader Open API is perfect for professional traders who want to go social and closely interact with their followers.

Essential Functionality[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/open-api/#essential-functionality "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Here is a non-exhaustive list of what the cTrader Open API allows your code to do.

*   Access real-time market data.
*   Perform all possible types of trading operations permitted in the official cTrader applications.
*   Retrieve and process information on past, current, and pending operations including deals, orders, and positions.

As shown in our [**Terms of Use**](https://help.ctrader.com/terms-of-use/)
, the API can be accessed by anyone with a cTID, and, by default, it is supported by all trading accounts of any cTrader-affiliated brokers.

Rate Limiting

Note that there exist some limits on how frequently you can perform certain requests to the cTrader backend.

*   You can perform a maximum of 50 requests per second per connection for any non-historical data requests.
*   You can perform a maximum of 5 requests per second per connection for any historical data requests.

Demo and Live Trading[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/open-api/#demo-and-live-trading "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can use the cTrader Open API to trade on behalf of both Demo and Live accounts.

We recommend using Demo accounts for development and testing, and then switching to Live after making sure that your integration with the cTrader Open API works as intended.

However, there are no hard restrictions, and you may freely choose to start development and testing under a Live account.

SDKs[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/open-api/#sdks "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------

When integrating with the Open API, you can use [**either JSON or Protobufs for data serialisation/deserialisation**](https://help.ctrader.com/open-api/protocol-buffers-json/)
.

You can use any language to implement the cTrader Open API. However, if you intend to use Protobufs, we highly recommend using a language that has official SDK support from Spotware. To date, these languages are as follows.

*   C# ([**access the SDK here**](https://github.com/spotware/OpenAPI.Net)
    )
*   Python ([**access the SDK here**](https://github.com/spotware/OpenApiPy)
    )

Every official SDK listed above contains 'helper' methods and classes that make the implementation of the cTrader Open API as smooth as possible.

If you intend to use JSON, there is no need to use our SDKs as handling serialisation/deserialisation in this case is relatively simple.

# Profile - cTrader ID

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/profile/#edit-profile-details)

Profile
=======

In the [**Profile**](https://id.ctrader.com/my/settings/profile)
 tab of the cTID website, you can edit your personal information and contacts shown on the profile page and adjust your visibility settings.

Note

The profile page of a cTrader user can be accessed:

*   Via a link `https://ctrader.com/u/username/`, where `username` is a real cTrader username, by all traders.
*   In the [**Partner**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-invite/partner/)
     tab of cTrader Invite, by invited traders whose partner has decided to make their profile visible.
*   On the algorithm author page in [**cTrader Algo**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/how-tos-index/)
    .
*   On the algorithm seller page in [**cTrader Store**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/)
    .

Edit profile details[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/profile/#edit-profile-details "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can edit the following profile details and settings in the top panel.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/img/profile-ctraderid-website.png)

### Cover image[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/profile/#cover-image "Permanent link")

Click the icon in the top-right corner of the cover image, select **Upload new image** and upload your preferred profile cover.

### Profile picture[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/profile/#profile-picture "Permanent link")

Click the icon on your round profile picture and choose **Upload new image**. Select a new profile picture and upload it.

### Username[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/profile/#username "Permanent link")

Click the edit icon to the right of your username, input a new username and then click **Save**.

### Preview[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/profile/#preview "Permanent link")

Click **Check how your profile appears to users** to preview your profile page.

### Visibility[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/profile/#visibility "Permanent link")

Manage your profile visibility with the **Make your profile public** toggle.

Warning

If you disable **Make your profile public**, all other traders, including your invited traders, will be unable to access your profile page.

Edit personal information[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/profile/#edit-personal-information "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In the next section, you can add and edit your personal information.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/img/personal-information-ctraderid-website.png)

Tip

Share your contact details so invited traders can always reach you. By staying in touch with your referral base, you will support their trading and further enhance your conversion rate.

*   **About** - enter information about yourself.

Note

The character limit for this text field is 1,024.

*   **Phone** - enter your phone number.
*   **Instagram** - enter your Instagram username or profile link.
*   **WhatsApp** - enter your phone number for WhatsApp or profile link.
*   **X platform** - enter your handle, username or profile link on X (formerly Twitter).
*   **Telegram** - enter your phone number for Telegram, username or profile link.
*   **YouTube** - enter your YouTube channel name, username or account link.
*   **Facebook** - enter your Facebook username or profile link.

Click **Save** to confirm the changes.

Adjust profile information visibility[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/profile/#adjust-profile-information-visibility "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Here, you can control the visibility of information on your profile page.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/img/profile-information-visibility-ctraderid-website.png)

*   **Contact information** - enable if you want your profile page visitors to see your contact details.
*   **Trading stats** - enable if you want your profile page visitors to see your trading statistics.
*   **Section about** - enable if you want your profile page visitors to see the **About** section.

Any changes you make here are applied immediately.

Adjust products visibility[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/profile/#adjust-products-visibility "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Here, you can choose which products to display to other traders, including your invited traders, on your profile page.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/img/products-visibility-ctraderid-website.png)

Tip

Partners use their profile page with appealing cTrader products as a ready-to-go landing page. Once traders click on your products, they are attributed to you within cTrader and can later convert into referrals in the broker's system.

*   **Your copy strategies** - enable if you want your profile page visitors to see the copy strategies you are providing.
*   **Favourite copy strategies** - enable if you want your profile page visitors to see the copy strategies you have marked as favourites.
*   **Your public algorithms** - enable if you want your profile page visitors to see the cTrader algorithms you are sharing via Invite.

Any changes you make here are applied immediately.

Back to top

# Managing cTID - cTrader ID

 

Managing cTID
=============

To manage your cTrader ID, sign in to the [**cTID website**](https://id.ctrader.com/)
, and the **Overview** tab will open.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/img/overview-settings.png)

Here, you can access the following settings:

*   [**Profile**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/profile/)
     - edit your profile information and adjust visibility settings.
*   [**cTrader ID credentials**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/credentials/)
     - change cTID credentials, such as your username, email and password.
*   [**Trading accounts**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/trading-accounts/)
     - view and manage demo and live trading accounts per broker linked to your cTID.
*   [**Notifications**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/notifications/)
     - manage notification settings and subscription to cTrader news and updates.
*   [**Sessions**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/managing-ctid/active-sessions/)
     - view and manage all the active sessions or instances where your cTID is being used.
*   [**Open API**](https://id.ctrader.com/my/settings/openapi)
     - view and manage Open API applications that have been granted access to your trading accounts.

Note

You can manage certain cTID settings directly from cTrader applications, without accessing the cTID website. Below, we explain which cTID management functionalities are available in each cTrader app.

cTrader MobilecTrader WebcTrader WindowscTrader Mac

Open the hamburger menu in the top-left corner and tap **Manage profile** in the list to access the following cTID settings.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/img/ios-manage-profile.png)

*   **Profile** - edit your profile information and adjust visibility settings.
*   **Change credentials** - change your cTID username, email or password.
*   **Active sessions** - view and manage all the sessions or instances where your cTID is being used.
*   **Notifications** - manage notification settings and subscription to cTrader news and updates.
*   **Delete account** - delete demo and live trading accounts per broker linked to your cTID.

Warning

You cannot delete a trading account under Spotware if it has any open positions or pending orders. Close positions and cancel pending orders first, then delete your account.

Open the account selector in the top-right corner and access the following cTID settings under your username/email.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/img/ctrader-web-ctid-settings.png)

*   **Active sessions** - view and manage all the sessions or instances where your cTID is being used.
*   **Change credentials** - change your cTID username, email or password.
*   **Manage profile** - edit your profile information and adjust visibility settings.

Open the account selector in the top-right corner and access the following cTID settings under your username/email.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/img/ctrader-desktop-ctid-settings.png)

*   **Active sessions** - view and manage all the sessions or instances where your cTID is being used.
*   **Change credentials** - change your cTID username, email or password.
*   **Manage profile** - edit your profile information and adjust visibility settings.

Open the account selector in the top-right corner and access the following cTID settings under your username/email.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/img/ctrader-mac-ctid-settings.png)

*   **Active sessions** - view and manage all the sessions or instances where your cTID is being used.
*   **Change credentials** - change your cTID username, email or password.
*   **Manage profile** - edit your profile information and adjust visibility settings.

Back to top

# Sign in - cTrader Store

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/sign-in/#sign-in)

Sign in[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/sign-in/#sign-in "Permanent link")

=====================================================================================

Use your [**cTrader ID**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/)
 (cTID) to sign in to cTrader Store.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/img/getting-started/ctrader-id-sign-in.png)

If you are a new user, click **Sign up** and then type in your details to register. For more information, read our dedicated guides on how to [**create a new cTID**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/create-a-new-ctrader-id/)
 or [**sign up with social accounts**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/sign-in-with-the-social-accounts/)
.

Alternatively, you can sign up via any [**cTrader app**](https://app.ctrader.com/)
 or the [**cTrader ID website**](https://id.ctrader.com/)
.

After you sign in to cTrader Store, you can immediately [**buy products**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/how-tos/buy-a-product/)
. However, additional [**verification**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/how-tos/become-a-seller/)
 is required to [**sell products**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/how-tos/publish-a-product/)
.

Back to top
