# Standard Deviation - Knowledge base

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/standard-deviation/#standard-deviation)

Standard Deviation[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/standard-deviation/#standard-deviation "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================================================================

Definition[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/standard-deviation/#definition "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Standard Deviation (SD) indicator measures the volatility of a symbol by assessing how much closing prices deviate from their average over a specific period. A higher SD indicates greater price fluctuations, suggesting increased market volatility, while a lower SD signifies stability. Traders use this indicator to identify potential trading opportunities, set risk management levels, and gauge overall market sentiment.

MobileDesktop

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/standard-deviation-ios.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/standard-deviation.png)

[Open live account](https://ctrader.com/brokers)
 [Download cTrader](https://ctrader.com/download/)

History[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/standard-deviation/#history "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The concept of the Standard Deviation became prominent through works like "Technical Analysis of Stock Trends" by Robert D. Edwards and John Magee (first published in 1948), which integrated statistical measures into trading. Eugene Fama's research in the 1970s emphasised volatility's role in market efficiency, cementing the SD indicator as a fundamental tool in technical analysis for assessing risk and identifying trading opportunities.

Calculations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/standard-deviation/#calculations "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Standard Deviation is calculated by taking the square root of the variance, which measures the dispersion of closing prices from their collective mean over a specified period:

\\\[ SD = { \\sqrt { { \\sum\_{i=1}^n { ( x\_i - {\\bar{x}} ) } ^2 } \\over { n - 1 } } } \\\]

\\(x\_i\\) – the closing prices

\\(\\bar{x}\\) – the mean of the closing prices over the specified periods

\\(n\\) – the number of periods

Interpretation[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/standard-deviation/#interpretation "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By default, the number of periods for the SD indicator is set to 14, but it can be adjusted depending on the trader strategy and the period of analysis.

The main patterns of the indicator behaviour can be interpreted as follows:

*   **Rising and falling** – a rising SD indicates increasing volatility, suggesting a potential breakout or trend formation. A falling SD reflects decreasing volatility, often associated with consolidation or range-bound markets.
    
*   **Reversal points** – extremely low SD values may indicate market complacency, often preceding sharp price moves or reversals. Conversely, extremely high SD values may indicate heightened market volatility, often occurring during sharp price movements, such as breakouts or strong trends.
    
*   **Divergence** – while the SD indicator does not directly show divergence, significant changes in the SD compared to price trends can hint at shifts in momentum.
    

Application[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/standard-deviation/#application "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Buy signal** – traders may enter a long position when the SD increases sharply, indicating rising volatility, and the price breaks above a resistance level.
    
*   **Sell signal** – traders may enter a short position when the SD rises while the price breaks below a key support level, pointing to increased downward momentum.
    
*   **Stop-loss placement** – traders can use the SD to set dynamic stop-loss levels based on market volatility. A higher SD suggests placing a wider stop-loss level to avoid premature exits during volatile conditions, while a lower SD warrants a tighter stop loss.
    
*   **Exit strategies** – spikes in the SD may signal trend exhaustion, prompting traders to exit positions before volatility fades, helping secure profits and avoid potential reversals.
    
*   **Confirming trades** – the SD works well with trend indicators like moving averages. For instance, combining the SD with a moving average crossover helps confirm whether a breakout has enough volatility to sustain a trend.
    

Note

You can take advantage of algo trading, with cBots executing trades based on the signals from this indicator, as shown in our [**examples**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/references/Indicators/StandardDeviation/)
. Learn more about how to [**use indicators in cBots**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/articles/for-developers/how-to-use-indicators-in-cbots/)
.

Limitations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/standard-deviation/#limitations "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The SD indicator relies on historical data, which may not accurately predict future volatility, leading to potential misinterpretations. Additionally, the SD can lag in rapidly changing markets, causing delayed signals that might result in missed opportunities. Furthermore, the SD does not provide directional bias, making it essential to combine it with other indicators for better trading decisions.

Summary[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/standard-deviation/#summary "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Standard Deviation is a crucial technical indicator used to measure market volatility by assessing the dispersion of price data over a specified period. It helps traders gauge market risk and identify potential price swings, making it a valuable tool for assessing volatility. By providing insights into price fluctuations, the SD assists traders in making informed decisions about entry and exit points. Additionally, the SD can be combined with other indicators to create effective trading strategies that account for market conditions.

[Open live account](https://ctrader.com/brokers)
 [Download cTrader](https://ctrader.com/download/)

Back to top

# True Range - Knowledge base

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/true-range/#true-range)

True Range[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/true-range/#true-range "Permanent link")

=====================================================================================================================

Definition[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/true-range/#definition "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The True Range (TR) indicator is a technical tool that calculates the volatility of currency pairs over a specified period. It is especially useful for identifying price fluctuations, helping traders understand market dynamics and potential price movements. The TR results indicate market volatility by showing the greatest price movement over the selected period. This insight helps traders adapt their strategies according to prevailing market conditions.

MobileDesktop

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/true-range-ios.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/true-range.png)

[Open live account](https://ctrader.com/brokers)
 [Download cTrader](https://ctrader.com/download/)

History[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/true-range/#history "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The TR indicator was developed by J. Welles Wilder Jr. and introduced in his 1978 book "New Concepts in Technical Trading Systems". Wilder created this indicator to address the limitations of traditional range-based volatility measures by factoring in gaps and price changes between sessions. It remains a foundational tool in technical analysis, especially for forex and commodity markets.

Calculations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/true-range/#calculations "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The True Range value is determined as the greatest value of the following:

\\\[ TR = { Max\\ \[(H − L), (H - C\_y), (L - C\_y)\] } \\\]

\\(H\\) – today's highest price

\\(L\\) – today's lowest price

\\(C\_y\\) – yesterday's closing price

Interpretation[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/true-range/#interpretation "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The TR indicator primarily reflects market volatility. It does not have divergence and convergence or other kinds of patterns. Instead, it is interpreted through rising and falling values:

*   A rising TR indicates increasing volatility, often signalling potential market instability or breakouts.
    
*   A falling TR suggests reduced volatility, typically pointing to consolidation or quieter market conditions.
    

This helps traders anticipate breakouts from periods of low volatility and adjust their strategies accordingly.

Application[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/true-range/#application "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Stop-loss placement** – traders often place a stop loss beyond the recent TR values to account for market volatility, reducing the chance of premature exits.
    
*   **Confirming trades** – the TR helps in combining with other indicators like the [**Average True Range (ATR)**](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/average-true-range/)
     to set volatility-based exit points or confirm trade setups by identifying when the market is prone to significant price movements.
    

Note

You can take advantage of algo trading, with cBots executing trades based on the signals from this indicator, as shown in our [**examples**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/references/Indicators/TrueRange/)
. Learn more about how to [**use indicators in cBots**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/articles/for-developers/how-to-use-indicators-in-cbots/)
.

Limitations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/true-range/#limitations "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The TR indicator reflects only volatility, offering no direct buy or sell signals. It can be reactive rather than predictive, as a rising TR indicates volatility after it occurs. The TR may also generate noise during erratic market conditions, making it less effective without complementary indicators or strategies for confirmation.

Summary[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/true-range/#summary "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The True Range indicator measures market volatility by capturing the largest price movement between a currency pair's high, low and previous close. It helps traders to understand price fluctuations and potential market instability. The TR is widely used for stop-loss placement, exit strategies and as part of volatility-based trading systems.

# 상품 게시하기 - cTrader 스토어

 

[콘텐츠로 이동](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/ko/how-tos/publish-a-product/#_1)

**상품 게시하기**[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/ko/how-tos/publish-a-product/#_1 "Permanent link")

=========================================================================================================

참고

[**인증된 계정**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/ko/how-tos/become-a-seller/)
만 상품을 게시할 수 있습니다.

**계정**으로 이동한 다음 **내 상품**을 열고 **새로 게시** 버튼을 클릭하세요.

**새 상품 게시** 페이지에서 양식을 작성하고 필요한 세부 정보를 입력하세요.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/img/how-tos/publish-new-algorithm.png)

작성이 완료되면 **게시** 버튼을 클릭하세요.

경고

cTrader는 cTrader 스토어를 통해 판매되는 모든 유료 알고리즘에 대해 30% 수수료를 부과합니다. 판매자로서 수입이 발생하면 관할 지역의 법률에 따라 세금을 납부해야 합니다.

**판매자를 위한 팁** [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/ko/how-tos/publish-a-product/#_2 "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

다음은 cTrader 스토어에서 판매자로서 성공하는 데 도움이 되는 팁입니다.

*   구매자가 받게 되는 것과 일치하도록 정확하고 상세한 상품 정보를 제공하세요.
*   구매자의 혼란을 방지하기 위해 각 상품마다 고유한 로고를 사용하세요.
*   상품의 기능을 보여주고 가치를 입증하는 고품질 이미지를 업로드하세요.
*   고객 인지도를 높이기 위해 상품 이미지 전반에 걸쳐 일관된 스타일을 사용하는 것을 고려하세요.
*   공정하고 투명한 상품 가격을 설정하세요. 프로모션 가격이 판매에 미치는 영향을 테스트해 보세요.
*   연락처 정보와 소셜 미디어 링크는 상품 설명이 아닌 cTrader [**프로필 페이지**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/ko/managing-ctid/profile)
    에 추가하세요.

**게시 규칙** [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/ko/how-tos/publish-a-product/#_3 "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

상품이 cTrader 스토어에서 금지되지 않도록 다음 규칙을 준수하세요.

1.  상품 이름과 설명에 다음 항목을 사용하지 마세요.
2.  노골적인 언어 또는 부적절한 내용
3.  cTrader 이외의 플랫폼 이름이나 상표
4.  메신저나 소셜 미디어 링크, 외부 리소스, 전화번호 또는 기타 연락처 정보
5.  상품 로고와 갤러리에 노골적이거나 부적절한 이미지를 사용하지 마세요.
6.  상품에 제3자 라이선스, 결제, 수익화 및 광고 책략, 연락처 정보 또는 외부 링크를 추가하지 마세요.
7.  특정 cTrader 사용자를 대상으로 하는 알고리즘을 업로드하지 마세요.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/img/how-tos/export-algorithm.png)

참고

cTrader Desktop/Mac에서 소스 코드와 함께 알고리즘을 내보내서 cTrader 스토어에 파일로 업로드하더라도 소스 코드는 구매자에게 공개되지 않아 귀하의 지적 재산권이 보호됩니다.

맨위로

---

# 发布产品 - cTrader 商店

 

[跳转至](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/zh/how-tos/publish-a-product/#_1)

**发布产品**[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/zh/how-tos/publish-a-product/#_1 "Permanent link")

======================================================================================================

注意

只有[**已验证账户**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/zh/how-tos/become-a-seller/)
才能发布产品。

前往**账户**，然后打开**您的产品**并点击**发布新产品**按钮。

在**发布新产品**页面填写表单并提供所需详细信息。

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/img/how-tos/publish-new-algorithm.png)

完成后点击**发布**按钮。

警告

cTrader 商店对所有付费算法收取 30% 的佣金。作为卖家，在获取收入后，您有义务根据所在地区的法律规定缴纳税款。

**卖家建议** [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/zh/how-tos/publish-a-product/#_2 "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

这些建议将帮助您成为 cTrader 商店的成功卖家:

*   提供准确详细的产品信息，确保描述与买家收到的产品相符。
*   为每个产品使用独特的标志，以防买家混淆。
*   上传高质量图片，展示产品特点和价值。
*   考虑在产品视觉效果中使用统一的风格，以实现一致的外观并提高客户认知度。
*   为产品设定公平透明的价格。 测试设置促销价，观察它对销量的影响。
*   在 cTrader [**个人资料页面**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/zh/managing-ctid/profile)
    添加您的联系信息和社交媒体链接，而不是写在产品描述中。

**发布规则** [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/zh/how-tos/publish-a-product/#_3 "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

请遵守以下规则，以确保产品不会被 cTrader 商店封禁:

1.  请勿在产品名称和描述中使用:
2.  不当用语或不适当内容
3.  除 cTrader 以外的平台名称或商标
4.  通讯软件或社交媒体链接、外部资源、电话号码或其他联系方式。
5.  请勿使用不当或不恰当的图片作为产品标志和图库。
6.  请勿在产品中添加第三方许可、支付、盈利和广告方案、联系方式或外部链接。
7.  请勿上传仅限特定 cTrader 用户使用的算法。

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/img/how-tos/export-algorithm.png)

注意

即使您从 cTrader Desktop/Mac 版本导出带有源代码的算法，然后将其作为文件上传到 cTrader 商店，买价也无法访问源代码，从而保护您的知识产权。

回到页面顶部

# 商品を公開する - cTraderストア

 

[コンテンツにスキップ](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/ja/how-tos/publish-a-product/#_1)

**商品を公開する**[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/ja/how-tos/publish-a-product/#_1 "Permanent link")

=========================================================================================================

注意

[**認証済みアカウント**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/ja/how-tos/become-a-seller/)
のみが商品を公開できます。

**アカウント**に移動し、**自分の商品**を開いて**新しい商品を公開**ボタンをクリックします。

**新しい商品を公開**ページでフォームに記入し、必要な詳細情報を入力します。

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/img/how-tos/publish-new-algorithm.png)

完了したら**公開**ボタンをクリックします。

警告

cTraderは、cTraderストアを通じて販売された有料アルゴリズムすべてに対し、30%の手数料を請求します。販売者として収入を得た場合は、該当する管轄区域の法律に従って納税する義務があります。

**販売者向けのヒント** [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/ja/how-tos/publish-a-product/#_2 "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

以下のヒントは、cTraderストアで販売者として成功を収めるのに役立ちます。

*   商品の正確で詳細な情報を指定して、その説明が、購入者が受け取る内容と一致するようにしてください。
*   購入者が混乱しないように、各商品に固有のロゴを使用してください。
*   商品の機能を紹介し、その価値を示す品質の高い画像をアップロードしてください。
*   統一感のある外観を実現し、顧客の認知度を高めるために、商品のビジュアルに一貫したスタイルを使用することを検討してください。
*   商品に公正で透明性の高い価格を設定してください。 プロモーション価格を設定することで、売上にどのような影響があるかテストしてください。
*   連絡先情報やソーシャルメディアのリンクは、商品の説明に含めずに、cTraderの[**プロフィールページ**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/ja/managing-ctid/profile)
    に追加するようにしてください。

**公開ルール** [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/ja/how-tos/publish-a-product/#_3 "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

商品がcTraderストアで禁止されないよう、以下のルールを遵守してください。

1.  商品名と説明文で以下を使用しないでください。
2.  露骨な表現や不適切な内容
3.  cTrader以外のプラットフォーム名や商標
4.  メッセンジャーやソーシャルメディアへのリンク、外部リソース、電話番号やその他の連絡先の詳細情報
5.  商品のロゴやギャラリーに露骨または不適切な画像を使用しないでください。
6.  サードパーティのライセンス、支払い、収益化、広告スキーム、連絡先の詳細情報、または外部リンクを商品に追加しないでください。
7.  特定のcTraderユーザーに限定されているアルゴリズムをアップロードしないでください。

![](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-store/img/how-tos/export-algorithm.png)

注意

cTrader DesktopまたはcTrader Macからソースコード付きでアルゴリズムをエクスポートし、それをファイルとしてcTraderストアにアップロードしても、ソースコードは購入者には利用できないままとなり、知的財産は保護されます。

ページトップへ戻る

# Thêm plugin WV - Plugin WebView

 

[Bỏ qua](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/vi/how-tos/add-wv-plugins/#them-plugin-webview)

Cảnh báo

Tính năng này đang trong giai đoạn tiền phát hành và hiện đang được thử nghiệm. Tài liệu chỉ mang tính tham khảo.

Thêm plugin WebView[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/vi/how-tos/add-wv-plugins/#them-plugin-webview "Permanent link")

=================================================================================================================================

Để sử dụng plugin WebView, trước tiên bạn phải thêm plugin. Bất kỳ plugin nào bạn thêm sẽ được liên kết với [**cTrader ID (cTID)**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/vi)
 hiện đang đăng nhập vào ứng dụng cTrader của bạn.

Mẹo

Bạn có thể khám phá và thêm plugin trực tiếp từ [**cTrader Store**](https://ctrader.com/webview-plugins)
. Khi đã được thêm, plugin sẽ tự động khả dụng trong tất cả các ứng dụng cTrader mà cTID của bạn đang hoạt động.

Trong ứng dụng **Trade** hoặc **Tổng quan** của bất kỳ ký hiệu nào, kéo xuống để xem bảng điều khiển plugin WebView, sau đó chạm vào biểu tượng **Thêm**.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/img/how-tos/trade-app-watchlists-mobile.png)

Một bảng điều khiển phía dưới sẽ mở ra hiển thị các plugin WV có sẵn trong cTrader Store.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/img/how-tos/webview-plugins-ctrader-store-mobile.png)

Trong danh sách plugin WV, chọn một plugin để mở trang chi tiết của plugin đó, sau đó chạm vào giá hoặc nút **Miễn phí** để thêm plugin này. Nếu không, hãy quay lại danh sách plugin.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/img/how-tos/selected-plugin-with-information-ctrader-store.png)

Sau khi thêm thành công, nút màu xanh lá cây sẽ chuyển thành **Đã cài đặt**, và bạn có thể đóng cửa sổ bằng cách vuốt xuống.

Plugin mới thêm sẽ xuất hiện.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/img/how-tos/updated-webview-plugin-list-mobile.png)

Nếu bạn không thấy plugin, hãy khởi động lại ứng dụng cTrader.

Trở lại mục lục

---

# Aceder a plugins WV - Plugins WebView

 

[Ir para o conteúdo](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/pt/how-tos/access-wv-plugins/#aceder-a-plugins-webview)

Aviso

Esta funcionalidade está em pré-lançamento e atualmente em fase de testes. A documentação serve apenas como referência.

Aceder a plugins WebView[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/pt/how-tos/access-wv-plugins/#aceder-a-plugins-webview "Permanent link")

==============================================================================================================================================

Depois de [**adicionar um plugin WV**](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/pt/how-tos/add-wv-plugins/)
, este fica imediatamente utilizável.

Na aplicação **Trade**, puxe para baixo para aceder a todos os plugins WebView associados ao seu [**cTID**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-id/pt)
.

Dica

O gesto de puxar para baixo no dispositivo móvel para mostrar o painel de plugins WV funciona em todos os separadores da aplicação **Trade**, bem como no separador **Visão geral** de qualquer símbolo.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/img/how-tos/default-plugins-screen-watchlists.png)

Pode tocar num plugin para abrir a sua folha inferior e aceder ao seu conteúdo.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/img/how-tos/expanded-view-webview-plugin.png)

Para fechar a folha inferior do plugin, toque em qualquer lugar fora da mesma ou simplesmente puxe para baixo.

No separador **Visão geral** de qualquer símbolo, o deslocamento para baixo revela uma vista expandida de cada plugin WebView.

![](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/img/how-tos/overview-tab-expanded-webview.png)

Voltar ao topo

# Mesej awam - Plugin WebView

 

[Langkau tajuk talian](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/ms/references/public-message/#mesej-awam)

Mesej awam[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/ms/references/public-message/#mesej-awam "Permanent link")

==================================================================================================================

ProtoGetServerDataReq [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/ms/references/public-message/#protogetserverdatareq "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Medan | Jenis | Label | Penerangan |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| payloadType | [PublicMessagePayloadType](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/ms/references/public-message/#publicmessagepayloadtype) | Pilihan | Kod yang disertakan dalam setiap mesej yang memberitahu cTrader dan plugin WebView permintaan, respons atau peristiwa khusus yang sedang dihantar supaya ia boleh dihalakan dan diproses dengan betul |
| data | rentetan | Diperlukan | Parameter permintaan yang diserialisasikan sebagai rentetan untuk mendapatkan data pelayan tertentu dari platform cTrader |
| clientMsgId | rentetan | Diperlukan | Pengecam unik yang dijana untuk setiap mesej, digunakan untuk mengaitkan permintaan dengan respons yang sepadan dan sebarang acara berkaitan |

ProtoGetServerDataRes [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/ms/references/public-message/#protogetserverdatares "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Medan | Jenis | Label | Penerangan |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| payloadType | [PublicMessagePayloadType](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/ms/references/public-message/#publicmessagepayloadtype) | Pilihan | Kod yang disertakan dalam setiap mesej yang memberitahu cTrader dan plugin WebView permintaan, respons atau peristiwa khusus yang sedang dihantar supaya ia boleh dihalakan dan diproses dengan betul |
| data | rentetan | Diperlukan | Data respons dari pelayan yang diserialisasikan sebagai rentetan yang mengandungi maklumat yang diminta |
| clientMsgId | rentetan | Diperlukan | Pengecam unik yang dijana untuk setiap mesej, digunakan untuk mengaitkan permintaan dengan respons yang sepadan dan sebarang acara berkaitan |

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Nama | Nilai | Penerangan |
| --- | --- | --- |
| REGISTER\_EVENT | 2000 | Plugin ke hos: permulaan berjabat tangan |
| CONFIRM\_EVENT | 2001 | Plugin ke hos: pengesahan berjabat tangan |
| GET\_SERVER\_DATA\_REQ | 2100 | Plugin ke hos: permintaan RPC. Digunakan dalam pengambilan data atau memanggil operasi (cth. `GetAccountInfo`). |
| GET\_SERVER\_DATA\_RES | 2101 | Hos ke plugin: respons RPC (hasil) |
| SERVER\_DATA\_EVENT | 2102 | Hos ke plugin: acara sehala (eventName + data) |
| OPEN\_INTERNAL\_URL\_REQ | 2103 | Plugin ke hos: permintaan untuk membuka URL |
| OPEN\_INTERNAL\_URL\_RES | 2104 | Hos ke plugin: perakuan pembukaan URL |
| PUBLIC\_ERROR\_RES | 2500 | Hos ke plugin: pembalut ralat generik |

# ข้อความสาธารณะ - ปลั๊กอิน WebView

 

[ข้ามไปที่เนื้อหา](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/th/references/public-message/#_1)

ข้อความสาธารณะ[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/th/references/public-message/#_1 "Permanent link")

==============================================================================================================

ProtoGetServerDataReq [¶](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/th/references/public-message/#protogetserverdatareq "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| ฟิลด์ | ประเภท | ป้ายกำกับ | คำอธิบาย |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| payloadType | [PublicMessagePayloadType](https://help.ctrader.com/webview-plugins/th/references/public-message/#publicmessagepayloadtype) | ตัวเลือก | รหัสที่รวมอยู่ในแต่ละข้อความที่บอก cTrader และปลั๊กอิน WebView ว่ากำลังส่งคำขอ การตอบสนอง หรือเหตุการณ์เฉพาะใด เพื่อให้สามารถจัดเส้นทางและประมวลผลได้อย่างถูกต้อง |
| data | สตริง | จำเป็น | พารามิเตอร์คำขอที่แปลงเป็นสตริงเพื่อดึงข้อมูลเซิร์ฟเวอร์เฉพาะจากแพลตฟอร์ม cTrader |
| clientMsgId | สตริง | จำเป็น | ตัวระบุเอกลักษณ์ที่สร้างขึ้นสำหรับแต่ละข้อความ ใช้เพื่อเชื่อมโยงคำขอกับการตอบสนองที่ตรงกันและเหตุการณ์ที่เกี่ยวข้อง |

| ชื่อ | ค่า | คำอธิบาย |
| --- | --- | --- |
| REGISTER\_EVENT | 2000 | ปลั๊กอินถึงโฮสต์: เริ่มต้นการแฮนด์เชค |
| CONFIRM\_EVENT | 2001 | ปลั๊กอินถึงโฮสต์: ยืนยันการแฮนด์เชค |
| GET\_SERVER\_DATA\_REQ | 2100 | ปลั๊กอินถึงโฮสต์: คำขอ RPC ใช้ในการดึงข้อมูลหรือเรียกใช้การดำเนินการ (เช่น `GetAccountInfo`) |
| GET\_SERVER\_DATA\_RES | 2101 | โฮสต์ถึงปลั๊กอิน: การตอบสนอง RPC (result) |
| SERVER\_DATA\_EVENT | 2102 | โฮสต์ถึงปลั๊กอิน: อีเวนต์ทางเดียว (eventName + data) |
| OPEN\_INTERNAL\_URL\_REQ | 2103 | ปลั๊กอินถึงโฮสต์: คำขอเปิด URL |
| OPEN\_INTERNAL\_URL\_RES | 2104 | โฮสต์ถึงปลั๊กอิน: การยืนยันการเปิด URL |
| PUBLIC\_ERROR\_RES | 2500 | โฮสต์ถึงปลั๊กอิน: wrapper ข้อผิดพลาดทั่วไป |

กลับไปด้านบนสุด

# High Minus Low - Knowledge base

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/high-minus-low/#high-minus-low)

High Minus Low[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/high-minus-low/#high-minus-low "Permanent link")

============================================================================================================================

Definition[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/high-minus-low/#definition "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The High Minus Low (HML) is a simple technical analysis tool that calculates the difference between the highest and lowest prices of an asset over a specified period. It is primarily used to measure market volatility, as the range between the high and low can indicate how much the price of an asset fluctuates within a given period.

MobileDesktop

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/high-minus-low-ios.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/high-minus-low.png)

[Open live account](https://ctrader.com/brokers)
 [Download cTrader](https://ctrader.com/download/)

History[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/high-minus-low/#history "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The High Minus Low has been used for decades as a straightforward method to measure price volatility. Though not attributed to a specific individual, the concept of calculating the range between high and low prices has long been a core element of technical analysis. It is commonly used by traders in conjunction with other indicators to gauge market conditions and assess potential entry or exit points.

Calculations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/high-minus-low/#calculations "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The calculation for the High Minus Low is straightforward. This value is calculated for each period (daily, weekly, etc.) and represents the price range within that period.

\\\[ HML\_t = { Round\\ (High\_t - Low\_t, Precision) } \\\]

\\(High\_t\\) – the high price of the current period \\(t\\)

\\(Low\_t\\) – the low price of the current period \\(t\\)

\\(Precision\\) – the decimal precision based on the symbol settings

Interpretation[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/high-minus-low/#interpretation "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Rising values** – indicates increasing market volatility, which can signal the potential for significant price movements. This often suggests that the market may be preparing for a breakout or heightened activity.
    
*   **Breakouts** – a sharp increase in the HML values can indicate that the market may be moving towards a breakout phase, which can present trading opportunities.
    
*   **Reversal points** – consistently high readings followed by a sudden drop may suggest that the current trend is losing momentum, potentially indicating an upcoming reversal.
    
*   **Shift** – by adjusting the shift parameter to alter the alignment of the HML indicator with price data on the chart, you can explore how the HML readings correspond to past or future price movements.
    

Application[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/high-minus-low/#application "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Buy signal** – traders might consider entering a buy position if the HML shows rising values alongside other bullish signals, as this can suggest increased volatility with upward potential.
    
*   **Sell signal** – a sell position may be considered when the indicator shows consistently high values followed by a decrease, signalling a potential shift in momentum and the possibility of a downtrend.
    
*   **Stop-loss placement** – use the HML range to set stop-loss orders just outside the recent high or low, allowing for protection against unexpected price swings during volatile periods.
    

Note

You can take advantage of algo trading, with cBots executing trades based on the signals from this indicator, as shown in our [**examples**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/references/Indicators/HighMinusLow/)
. Learn more about how to [**use indicators in cBots**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/articles/for-developers/how-to-use-indicators-in-cbots/)
.

Limitations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/high-minus-low/#limitations "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The High Minus Low does not provide any directional insight into price movement. It only measures the volatility range. The indicator may produce false signals during periods of erratic price movement without an actual trend.

Summary[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/high-minus-low/#summary "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The High Minus Low measures the price range between the highest and lowest values over a given period, providing insight into market volatility. Larger values indicate greater price fluctuations, while smaller values suggest lower volatility or consolidation. As simple as the concept may be, it remains an integral part of technical analysis used by many traders and professionals.

# Keltner Channels - Knowledge base

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/keltner-channels/#keltner-channels)

Keltner Channels[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/keltner-channels/#keltner-channels "Permanent link")

=======================================================================================================================================

Definition[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/keltner-channels/#definition "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Keltner Channels indicator is a volatility-based tool used in technical analysis to identify potential trend directions and price reversals. Composed of a central moving average with two bands set above and below, the Keltner Channels expand and contract based on the [**Average True Range (ATR)**](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/average-true-range/)
, highlighting periods of high and low volatility.

MobileDesktop

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/keltner-channels-ios.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/keltner-channels.png)

[Open live account](https://ctrader.com/brokers)
 [Download cTrader](https://ctrader.com/download/)

History[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/keltner-channels/#history "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Chester W. Keltner, a commodities trader, first introduced the Keltner Channels in the 1960s. Originally based on a 10-day moving average and typical price, modern versions incorporate the different moving averages and the ATR values for enhanced accuracy in trend and reversal detection.

Calculations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/keltner-channels/#calculations "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Keltner Channels are calculated using three elements.

*   **Middle band** – the moving average of the closing price over the specified MA period using the chosen MA type.

\\\[ Main = { MA\\ ( Close,\\ MAPeriod,\\ MAType ) } \\\]

*   **Top band** – calculated by adding the product of the band distance and the ATR to the middle band.

\\\[ Top = { Main + (BandDistance \\times ATR) } \\\]

*   **Bottom band** – calculated by subtracting the product of the band distance and the ATR from the middle band.

\\\[ Bottom = { Main - (BandDistance \\times ATR) } \\\]

\\(BandDistance\\) – the band distance multiplier, which adjusts the width of the channels

\\(ATR\\) – the Average True Range, given over the specified ATR period using the chosen ATR MA type

Interpretation[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/keltner-channels/#interpretation "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The default parameter values for the Keltner Channels calculation are as follows:

*   The default number of periods for MA calculation is 20, with the [**Simple Moving Average (SMA)**](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/trend/simple-moving-average/)
     as the default type of MA.
    
*   The default number of periods for ATR calculation is 10, with the SMA as the default type of ATR MA.
    
*   The default band distance value is set to 2.
    

The main patterns of the indicator behaviour can be interpreted as follows:

*   **Price breakouts** – breaking out above the upper band shows a potential overbought condition or breakout, possibly signalling an upward trend. Breaking out below the lower band shows a potential oversold condition or breakout, possibly indicating a downward trend.
    
*   **Trend direction** – sustained movement near the upper band generally indicates an uptrend. Sustained movement near the lower band suggests a downtrend.
    
*   **Reversals** – divergences, where the price and the indicator direction differ, can signal potential reversals.
    
*   **Shift** – by adjusting the shift parameter to alter the alignment of the Keltner Channels with price data on the chart, you can explore how the indicator readings correspond to past or future price movements.
    

Application[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/keltner-channels/#application "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Buy signal** – consider entering a buy position when the price breaks above the upper channel, signalling strong bullish momentum.
    
*   **Sell signal** – a sell position may be considered when the price breaks below the lower channel, indicating bearish momentum.
    
*   **Stop-loss placement** – traders can place a stop loss just outside the opposite channel line to manage risk during potential reversals.
    

Note

You can take advantage of algo trading, with cBots executing trades based on the signals from this indicator, as shown in our [**examples**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/references/Indicators/KeltnerChannels/)
. Learn more about how to [**use indicators in cBots**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/articles/for-developers/how-to-use-indicators-in-cbots/)
.

Limitations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/keltner-channels/#limitations "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Keltner Channels can produce misleading signals in sideways or low-volatility markets. The indicator may also lag during rapid price changes due to its reliance on historical data. These factors make it susceptible to false signals in choppy conditions, particularly when used alone.

Summary[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/keltner-channels/#summary "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Keltner Channels is a volatility-based tool that uses a central moving average and two bands derived from the [**Average True Range**](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/average-true-range/)
 to identify trends, price reversals and overbought or oversold conditions. Movements near the upper or lower bands highlight potential trend strength or shifts, while the central band acts as a trend indicator. Useful in trending markets, the Keltner Channels allow traders to identify breakout opportunities and gauge market volatility effectively.

# Ichimoku Kinko Hyo - Knowledge base

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/ichimoku-kinko-hyo/#ichimoku-kinko-hyo)

Ichimoku Kinko Hyo[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/ichimoku-kinko-hyo/#ichimoku-kinko-hyo "Permanent link")

========================================================================================================================================

Definition[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/ichimoku-kinko-hyo/#definition "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Ichimoku Kinko Hyo is a comprehensive technical analysis indicator designed to provide a view of market trends, momentum and support and resistance levels in a single chart. Commonly used in forex and stock markets, it consists of five lines that work together to provide insights into potential price movements, making it a powerful tool for identifying trends and reversals.

MobileDesktop

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/ichimoku-kinko-hyo-ios.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/ichimoku-kinko-hyo.png)

[Open live account](https://ctrader.com/brokers)
 [Download cTrader](https://ctrader.com/download/)

History[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/ichimoku-kinko-hyo/#history "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Ichimoku Kinko Hyo was developed by Goichi Hosoda, a Japanese journalist, in the late 1930s. He intended to create an indicator that would allow traders to gauge market conditions at a glance. After years of refinement, the indicator was publicly introduced in the 1960s and has since become widely popular for its ability to provide a quick and comprehensive market view.

Calculations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/ichimoku-kinko-hyo/#calculations "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Ichimoku Kinko Hyo consists of five main lines, each calculated to provide different insights into the market.

1\. **Tenkan Sen (conversion line)** – provides a short-term view of the market.

\\\[ Tenkan\\ Sen = { { Highest\\ High + Lowest\\ Low } \\over 2 }\\ over\\ n\\ periods\\ (typically\\ 9) \\\]

2\. **Kijun Sen (base line)** – gives a medium-term trend.

\\\[ Kijun\\ Sen = { { Highest\\ High + Lowest\\ Low } \\over 2 }\\ over\\ m\\ periods\\ (typically\\ 26) \\\]

3\. **Chikou Span (lagging span)** – the closing price shifted backward by 26 periods. This line helps identify the relationship between current price and past price levels.

\\\[ Chikou\\ Span\_{-m} = Current\\ Close \\\]

4\. **Senkou Span A (leading span A)** – forms one edge of the cloud and represents future support or resistance.

\\\[ Senkou\\ Span\\ A = { { Tenkan Sen + Kijun Sen } \\over 2 }\\ projected\\ forward\\ by\\ m\\ periods\\ (typically\\ 26) \\\]

5\. **Senkou Span B (leading span B)** – forms the opposite edge of the cloud and represents a longer-term perspective on support and resistance.

\\\[ Senkou\\ Span\\ B = { { Highest\\ High + Lowest\\ Low } \\over 2 }\\ over\\ p\\ periods\\ (typically\\ 52),\\ projected\\ forward\\ by\\ 26\\ periods \\\]

Interpretation[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/ichimoku-kinko-hyo/#interpretation "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Trend identification** – when prices are above the cloud, it indicates an uptrend, while prices below the cloud suggest a downtrend. The thickness of the cloud indicates the strength of the trend.
    
*   **Crossovers** – crosses between the Tenkan Sen and the Kijun Sen can serve as potential buy or sell signals.
    
*   **Cloud colour** – the cloud colour (based on which span is higher) can indicate whether the market sentiment is bullish or bearish.
    
*   **Support and resistance** – the cloud also acts as dynamic support and resistance, with thicker clouds indicating stronger barriers.
    
*   **Shift** – by adjusting the shift parameter to alter the alignment of the Ichimoku Kinko Hyo with price data on the chart, you can explore how the indicator readings correspond to past or future price movements.
    

Application[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/ichimoku-kinko-hyo/#application "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Trend confirmation** – traders use the indicator to confirm trends, paying attention to price positions relative to the cloud.
    
*   **Entry and exit signals** – crossovers between the Tenkan Sen and the Kijun Sen or between price and the cloud, provide entry or exit signals.
    
*   **Support and resistance zones** – the cloud itself can act as a buffer zone, with prices often bouncing off these levels in trending markets.
    

Note

You can take advantage of algo trading, with cBots executing trades based on the signals from this indicator, as shown in our [**examples**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/references/Indicators/IchimokuKinkoHyo/)
. Learn more about how to [**use indicators in cBots**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/articles/for-developers/how-to-use-indicators-in-cbots/)
.

Limitations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/ichimoku-kinko-hyo/#limitations "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Ichimoku Kinko Hyo uses multiple lagging and leading indicators, meaning it may produce delayed signals in volatile or rapidly changing markets. It may provide limited clarity in short-term trading. Additionally, the cloud can sometimes appear too complex for novice traders.

Summary[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/ichimoku-kinko-hyo/#summary "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Ichimoku Kinko Hyo is a comprehensive trend-following indicator developed by Goichi Hosoda, designed to provide a quick view of market trends, momentum and support and resistance levels. With its combination of moving averages and the unique Kumo cloud, it helps traders make informed trading decisions. It consists of five lines that work together to provide insights into potential price movements, making it a powerful tool for identifying trends and reversals. Moreover, the indicator shows a cloud that changes in colour to reflect market sentiment.

# Fractal Chaos Bands - Knowledge base

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/fractal-chaos-bands/#fractal-chaos-bands)

Fractal Chaos Bands[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/fractal-chaos-bands/#fractal-chaos-bands "Permanent link")

================================================================================================================================================

Definition[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/fractal-chaos-bands/#definition "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Fractal Chaos Bands (FCB) indicator is a volatility-based tool designed to identify price trends and potential reversals by marking key high and low points in the market using fractals. These fractals are created by examining recent price extremes and the bands help highlight market direction, distinguishing between trending and ranging (sideways) market conditions.

MobileDesktop

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/fractal-chaos-bands-ios.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/fractal-chaos-bands.png)

[Open live account](https://ctrader.com/brokers)
 [Download cTrader](https://ctrader.com/download/)

History[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/fractal-chaos-bands/#history "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Fractal Chaos Bands indicator is inspired by fractal geometry, introduced by Benoît Mandelbrot, which is applied to finance and market price movements. The indicator utilises fractals to represent repetitive patterns in the price structure and to detect market trends. Its development is influenced by the use of fractals in various trading strategies that analyse price behaviour through self-similar patterns at different periods.

Calculations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/fractal-chaos-bands/#calculations "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The calculation of the Fractal Chaos Bands involves identifying the most recent fractals; specific points where the price forms a local high (high fractal) or local low (low fractal). The bands are formed by plotting these fractal points across the chart, creating a boundary between upper and lower price levels.

*   **High fractal** – is determined when the current price is higher than the surrounding prices over a defined period.
    
*   **Low fractal** – is calculated when the current price is lower than the surrounding prices over a defined period.
    

The bands adjust dynamically as new fractals are identified, with the upper band following the high fractals and the lower band following the low fractals.

Interpretation[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/fractal-chaos-bands/#interpretation "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Breakouts** – price breaking the upper band suggests potential bullish momentum and the possibility of an uptrend. Price breaking the lower band suggests potential bearish momentum and the possibility of a downtrend.
    
*   **Trend identification** – when bands slope upward it indicates an uptrend. When bands slope downward it indicates a downtrend.
    
*   **Sideways movement** – when the bands move horizontally without significant slope, it signals a range-bound or consolidating market.
    
*   **Shift** – by adjusting the shift parameter to alter the alignment of the Fractal Chaos Bands with price data on the chart, you can explore how the indicator readings correspond to past or future price movements.
    

Application[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/fractal-chaos-bands/#application "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Buy signal** – traders might consider buying when the price approaches the lower band, as this could indicate oversold conditions and a potential upward reversal.
    
*   **Sell signal** – a sell signal might be triggered when the price moves towards the upper band, signalling overbought conditions or a potential reversal downward.
    
*   **Breakouts** – when the price breaks above the upper band or below the lower band, it could signal the start of a new trend, offering a trading opportunity in the direction of the breakout.
    
*   **Combining with other indicators** – to increase the reliability of signals, traders often use the Fractal Chaos Bands with other indicators such as the [**Relative Strength Index (RSI)**](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/oscillators/relative-strength-index/)
     for momentum or the moving average to confirm trend direction and filter out false signals during consolidation phases.
    

Note

You can take advantage of algo trading, with cBots executing trades based on the signals from this indicator, as shown in our [**examples**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/references/Indicators/FractalChaosBands/)
. Learn more about how to [**use indicators in cBots**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/articles/for-developers/how-to-use-indicators-in-cbots/)
.

Limitations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/fractal-chaos-bands/#limitations "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Fractal Chaos Bands indicator may lag during periods of extreme volatility, or when markets are moving quickly due to its reliance on historical price data to identify fractals. In addition, during range-bound or choppy markets, the bands may generate false signals.

Summary[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volatility/fractal-chaos-bands/#summary "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Fractal Chaos Bands provide a unique way to visualise market trends and volatility by tracking price extremes using fractals. The bands highlight potential trend directions and reversal points, making them useful for both trend-following and range-bound strategies. The indicator relies on fractals, points where the price forms a local high or local low. This helps represent repetitive patterns in the price structure and detect market trends.

# Ease of Movement - Knowledge base

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/ease-of-movement/#ease-of-movement)

Ease of Movement[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/ease-of-movement/#ease-of-movement "Permanent link")

===================================================================================================================================

Definition[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/ease-of-movement/#definition "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Ease of Movement (EMV) is a volume-based technical analysis indicator that measures the relationship between price and volume to gauge the ease with which an asset’s price moves. It helps traders identify whether price movements are happening with little or much effort from the market participants.

MobileDesktop

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/ease-of-movement-ios.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/ease-of-movement.png)

[Open live account](https://ctrader.com/brokers)
 [Download cTrader](https://ctrader.com/download/)

History[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/ease-of-movement/#history "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Ease of Movement was developed by Richard W. Arms in the 1990s. Arms, known for his work on other volume-based indicators such as the Arms Index (TRIN), designed the EMV to combine both price and volume in a straightforward calculation, providing a unique perspective on market momentum.

Calculations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/ease-of-movement/#calculations "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Ease of Movement formula consists of several components.

First, the midpoints of the high and low prices for the current and previous periods are calculated.

\\\[ Midpoint = { { High + Low } \\over 2 } \\\]

Then, their difference is divided by the volume and scaled by the current high-low price range.

\\\[ EMV = { { ( { Midpoint - Midpoint\_{prev} } ) } \\times { { High - Low } \\over Volume } } \\\]

This is then multiplied by 10,000 to adjust the scale. Finally, the result is smoothed by applying a moving average over the specified period.

Interpretation[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/ease-of-movement/#interpretation "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Value** – a positive EMV indicates that prices are rising with ease and buying pressure is dominant. A negative EMV shows that prices are falling easily, suggesting selling pressure.
    
*   **Zero-line crossovers** – the zero line acts as a critical threshold. When EMV crosses above zero, it signals a possible start of an upward trend and when it crosses below zero, it indicates potential bearish movement.
    
*   **Divergence** – a bullish divergence occurs when the price hits a lower low while the EMV makes a higher low, indicating weakening selling pressure and a potential reversal upward. A bearish divergence happens when the price hits a higher high but the EMV makes a lower high.
    

Application[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/ease-of-movement/#application "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Buy signal** – consider entering a long position when the EMV crosses above the zero line, indicating upward price movement with ease.
    
*   **Sell signal** – consider entering a short position when the EMV crosses below the zero line, signalling downward movement with ease.
    
*   **Stop-loss placement** – traders might place stop-loss orders below significant support levels for long positions or above resistance levels for short positions to manage risk.
    
*   **Combining with other indicators** – traders typically use the EMV with other tools like the [**Relative Strength Index (RSI)**](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/oscillators/relative-strength-index/)
     to confirm whether an asset is overbought or oversold or with moving averages to smooth out short-term noise.
    

Note

You can take advantage of algo trading, with cBots executing trades based on the signals from this indicator, as shown in our [**examples**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/references/Indicators/EaseOfMovement/)
. Learn more about how to [**use indicators in cBots**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/articles/for-developers/how-to-use-indicators-in-cbots/)
.

Limitations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/ease-of-movement/#limitations "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Ease of Movement can produce false signals in low-volume markets or during periods of market consolidation. It may not always be ideal for predicting short-term reversals, but rather for confirming ongoing trends.

Summary[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/ease-of-movement/#summary "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Ease of Movement provides insight into how effortlessly price movements occur based on volume. Positive EMV values suggest price rises with relative ease, while negative values signal ease of price declines. As the zero line determines its base value, a crossing over it signals an upward price movement with ease. Alternatively, a crossing below the zero line signals a downward movement with ease.

# Money Flow Index - Knowledge base

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/money-flow-index/#money-flow-index)

Money Flow Index[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/money-flow-index/#money-flow-index "Permanent link")

===================================================================================================================================

Definition[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/money-flow-index/#definition "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Money Flow Index (MFI) indicator is a momentum oscillator that calculates buying and selling pressure using both price and volume analysis. It oscillates between 0 and 100; a rising MFI reflects stronger buying pressure, while a falling MFI points to increasing selling pressure. The MFI indicator is typically used to identify trend reversals and price extremes.

MobileDesktop

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/money-flow-index-ios.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/money-flow-index.png)

[Open live account](https://ctrader.com/brokers)
 [Download cTrader](https://ctrader.com/download/)

History[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/money-flow-index/#history "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Money Flow Index was created in the late 1980s by Gene Quong and Avrum Soudack, both experienced technical analysts. They designed the MFI to improve upon traditional momentum indicators by incorporating volume data into price analysis, offering traders a more comprehensive view of market strength compared to the widely used [**Relative Strength Index (RSI)**](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/oscillators/relative-strength-index/)
.

Calculations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/money-flow-index/#calculations "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In most cases, the MFI value is calculated using the following steps:

1\. The Typical Price is calculated as the average of the high, low and closing prices for each period.

\\\[ Typical\\ Price = { {High + Low + Close} \\over 3 } \\\]

2\. The Raw Money Flow is calculated by multiplying the Typical Price by the volume for a given period.

\\\[ Raw\\ Money\\ Flow = { Typical\\ Price \\times Volume } \\\]

3\. The Money Flow Ratio (MFR) is calculated as the ratio of the Positive Money Flow to the Negative Money Flow.

\\\[ MFR = { {Positive\\ Money\\ Flow} \\over {Negative\\ Money\\ Flow} } \\\]

    \\(Positive\\) \\(Money\\) \\(Flow\\) – the sum of the Raw Money Flow on the days when the Typical Price is higher than the previous day

    \\(Negative\\) \\(Money\\) \\(Flow\\) – the sum of the Raw Money Flow on the days when the Typical Price is lower than the previous day

4\. The Money Flow Index is calculated by the following formula:

\\\[ MFI = { 100 - {100 \\over {1 + MFR} } } \\\]

Interpretation[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/money-flow-index/#interpretation "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The MFI indicator is calculated using 14 periods by default, but other periods can be adjusted based on the strategy.

The main patterns of the indicator behaviour can be interpreted as follows:

*   **Crossovers** – the MFI values range between 0 and 100. Readings above 80 are typically considered overbought, suggesting a potential price correction, such as a downward pullback or a consolidation phase. Conversely, values below 20 are seen as oversold, signalling possible upward price momentum.
    
*   **Divergence** – when the MFI indicator moves in the opposite direction to the symbol price, this can signal potential reversals. For instance, if prices rise while the MFI falls, it may indicate weakening buying pressure. The opposite behaviour, when the price makes a new high but the MFI shows a lower high, suggests diminishing strength in the upward trend, potentially forecasting a price decline.
    
*   **Failure swings** – failure swings can signal potential price reversals, functioning independently of price changes and focusing solely on the MFI. They can indicate either a bullish or bearish trend according to the swing direction. A bullish trend occurs when the MFI drops below 20 and then rebounds above it, maintaining strength. Conversely, a bearish trend happens when the MFI rises above 80, drops back and then dips below its previous low.
    
*   **Shift** – by adjusting the shift parameter to alter the alignment of the MFI indicator with price data on the chart, you can explore how the MFI readings correspond to past or future price movements.
    

Application[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/money-flow-index/#application "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Buy signal** – traders can consider entering a buy position when the MFI value drops below 20, indicating oversold conditions, then rebounds above it, suggesting increased buying pressure.
    
*   **Sell signal** – traders can consider entering a sell position when the MFI value rises above 80, indicating overbought conditions, and then falls below this level, indicating potential selling pressure.
    
*   **Stop-loss placement** – it is recommended to set a stop loss slightly below the recent swing low for buy signals and above the recent swing high for sell signals.
    
*   **Confirming trades** – to confirm the MFI signals, traders frequently utilise additional indicators like price action and trend analysis, as described above, to enhance their decision-making process.
    

Note

You can take advantage of algo trading, with cBots executing trades based on the signals from this indicator, as shown in our [**examples**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/references/Indicators/MoneyFlowIndex/)
. Learn more about how to [**use indicators in cBots**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/articles/for-developers/how-to-use-indicators-in-cbots/)
.

Limitations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/money-flow-index/#limitations "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Money Flow Index has several limitations, including its reliance on historical data, which can delay signals. It may produce false positives in choppy markets and is less effective during extreme market conditions. Additionally, the MFI can lag price movements, leading to missed opportunities. Traders should combine the MFI with other indicators for more robust analysis and risk management.

Summary[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/volume/money-flow-index/#summary "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Money Flow Index is a momentum indicator that measures the buying and selling pressure based on price and volume over a specific period, typically 14 days. It ranges from 0 to 100, with values above 80 indicating overbought conditions and those below 20 signalling oversold conditions. Traders utilise the MFI to identify potential reversals and market strength, helping them make informed trading decisions.

# Median Price - Knowledge base

 

[Skip to content](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/median-price/#median-price)

Median Price[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/median-price/#median-price "Permanent link")

======================================================================================================================

Definition[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/median-price/#definition "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Median Price indicator is a technical analysis tool that defines the midpoint of price data over a specified period. It is calculated by ordering the symbol prices within a specific period and finding the average value, making it less sensitive to outliers than the average. Traders use this indicator to assess market trends and identify potential support and resistance levels.

MobileDesktop

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/median-price-ios.png)

![](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/img/median-price.png)

[Open live account](https://ctrader.com/brokers)
 [Download cTrader](https://ctrader.com/download/)

History[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/median-price/#history "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Median Price indicator was developed to provide a more reliable measure of central tendency in financial markets. It gained prominence as traders sought to mitigate the impact of outliers affecting average prices. Over time, it has become a standard tool in technical analysis, helping traders identify market trends, support and resistance levels.

Calculations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/median-price/#calculations "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To define the Median Price for a given period, first calculate the median price for each day and then calculate the average median price over the desired period.

\\\[ Median\\ Price = { {High + Low} \\over 2 } \\\]

\\(High\\) – the highest price over a specified period

\\(Low\\) – the lowest price over a specified period

Interpretation[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/median-price/#interpretation "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Traders typically use the Median Price indicator over various periods, with the most common settings being 14, 21 or 30 periods. These durations provide a balance between capturing short-term price movements and maintaining relevance to longer trends. Once the period is selected, the following interpretations of the indicator are generally applicable:

*   **Rising and falling** – a rising Median Price indicates a bullish trend with generally increasing prices, while a falling Median Price indicates a bearish trend with generally decreasing prices.
    
*   **Crossovers** – when the price line crosses above the median line, it may indicate a potential buying opportunity. Conversely, if the price line crosses below the median line, it could signal a selling opportunity.
    
*   **Divergence and convergence** – if the price is making new highs or lows while the Median Price does not reflect the same movement, it may suggest weakening momentum and a potential reversal. If the price is moving in the same direction as the Median Price, it indicates strong momentum in that direction, reinforcing the trend.
    

Application[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/median-price/#application "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Buy signal** – traders can look for buying opportunities when the price crosses above the Median Price line. This crossover suggests a potential bullish trend, indicating that buyers are gaining control.
    
*   **Sell signal** – conversely, a sell signal may arise when the price crosses below the Median Price line. This crossover indicates a potential bearish trend, signalling that sellers are dominating the market.
    
*   **Stop-loss placement** – for long positions, traders might set a stop loss just below the Median Price, using it as a support level. For short positions, placing a stop loss just above the Median Price can serve as a resistance level, helping to manage risk effectively.
    
*   **Confirming trades** – supporting indicators for the Median Price include moving averages, which filter buy and sell signals by confirming price movements above or below the median, and the [**Relative Strength Index (RSI)**](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/oscillators/relative-strength-index/)
    , which helps confirm overbought or oversold conditions alongside the median levels.
    

Note

You can take advantage of algo trading, with cBots executing trades based on the signals from this indicator, as shown in our [**examples**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/references/Indicators/MedianPrice/)
. Learn more about how to [**use indicators in cBots**](https://help.ctrader.com/ctrader-algo/articles/for-developers/how-to-use-indicators-in-cbots/)
.

Limitations[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/median-price/#limitations "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Median Price indicator is classified as a lagging indicator, similar to other moving averages, which can diminish its effectiveness in fast-moving market conditions. While it helps reduce market noise, it can still generate false or misleading signals, particularly in choppy markets. Additionally, the indicator is most effective in trending conditions; when markets are range-bound or moving sideways, it may not provide clear, actionable signals for traders.

Summary[¶](https://help.ctrader.com/knowledge-base/indicators/other/median-price/#summary "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

​The Median Price indicator is calculated by averaging the highest and lowest prices over a specified period. This indicator helps traders identify trends, potential support and resistance levels, as well as market sentiment. The Median Price is particularly effective in trending markets and serves as a valuable tool for confirming signals when combined with other indicators like moving averages and the Relative Strength Index (RSI). Its simplicity makes it accessible, but it is crucial to use it alongside additional indicators to enhance accuracy and reduce false signals.
